** unique sub group headers

In the uploaded userscript,

the logic to check for duplicated sub header names and counting rows per sub header in the following code block

#+begin_example
                rows.forEach((row, index) => {
                    // Detect sub-grouping headers (e.g., "recording location for")
                    if (row.classList.contains('subh')) {
                        const thWithColspan = row.querySelector('th[colspan]');
                        if (thWithColspan) {
                            let rawName = thWithColspan.textContent.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();

                            // Logic to make the name unique
                            let uniqueName = rawName;
                            if (seenSubgroups.has(rawName)) {
                                let count = seenSubgroups.get(rawName) + 1;
                                seenSubgroups.set(rawName, count);

                                // Determine suffix based on what follows (e.g., check rows below for context)
                                // For simplicity, we use the counter, but you can refine this to look for
                                // entity links if necessary.
                                uniqueName = `${rawName} (${count})`;
                            } else {
                                seenSubgroups.set(rawName, 1);
                            }

                            currentSubgroup = uniqueName;
                            localSubgroupCount = 0;

                            const metaEntry = {
                                subgroup: currentSubgroup, // This is now unique
                                originalName: rawName,     // Keep the original for display if needed
                                url: null,
                                totalRowsPerSubGroup: 0,
                                totalRows: totalRows
                            };
                            subgroupData.push(metaEntry);
                        }
                        return;
                    }
#+end_example

should also be applied when the actual data is fetched (for rendering later in the final page) in the case we are on a
URL like https://musicbrainz.org/place/a727b970-8ea0-4f75-abc8-db131f72aecb/performances , which represents a pageType
which has no pagination, so all the data is present on that very page. The thing is, that sub header names
(e.g. "recording location for") can repeat on such a page (rows under each same named sub-heading have a different
meaning) and right now we accumulate all the rows under the different same named sub headers in the same data structure
for rendering later.

Please fix
AND Please do not make any other changes to working code, comments and logging statements.

*** fix

With your proposed patch

#+begin_example
-                                    currentStatus = node.textContent.trim() || 'Unknown';
+                                    const th = node.querySelector('th');
+                                    let rawName = (th ? th.textContent : node.textContent).replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim() || 'Unknown';
+
+                                    if (seenFetchSubgroups.has(rawName)) {
+                                        let count = seenFetchSubgroups.get(rawName) + 1;
+                                        seenFetchSubgroups.set(rawName, count);
+                                        currentStatus = `${rawName} (${count})`;
+                                    } else {
+                                        seenFetchSubgroups.set(rawName, 1);
+                                        currentStatus = rawName;
+                                    }
+
                                     // TODO: for 'place-performances' check as we can have same named categories but different entities, like releases and recordings recorded at the same place
                                     if ((pageType === 'releasegroup-releases' || pageType === 'place-performances') && currentStatus !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages) {
#+end_example

I actually get an "Unknown" for all the h3 headers, wheraes before it worked for all pages

** create button on h3 header when more than 100 rows

In the uploaded userscript,

when rendering a subgroup table below the "rawName" with the number of rows inside the subgroup appended in brackets,
create an additional button with text "Show all" after the "rawname (<number of rows>)" h3 headers for those subgroups
where a "seeAllCell" is encuntered. So basically when this code is executed

#+begin_example
                                else if (node.cells.length > 1 && !node.classList.contains('explanation')) {
                                    // Remove artificial non-data rows on non-paginated pages which have a link "See all <number of rows> relationships" to the full dataset instead
                                    if (activeDefinition && activeDefinition.non_paginated) {
                                        const seeAllCell = node.querySelector('td[colspan]');
                                        if (seeAllCell) {
                                            const link = seeAllCell.querySelector('a');
                                            if (link && link.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('see all')) {
                                                Lib.debug('parse', `Skipping "See all" relationship row.`);
                                                return; // Skip adding this row to data structures
                                            }
                                        }
                                    }
#+end_example

the corresponding h3 header should have an additional button appended in the same line

** debug

In the uploaded userscript,

please add comprehensive debug logging statements to the following function

#+begin_example
    function updateH2Count(filteredCount, totalCount) {
        const table = document.querySelector('table.tbl');
        if (!table) return;
        let allH2s = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('h2'));
        let targetH2 = null;

        if (pageType === 'artist-releasegroups') {
            targetH2 = document.querySelector('h2.discography');
        } else if (pageType === 'releasegroup-releases') {
            targetH2 = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('h2')).find(h => h.textContent.includes('Album'));
        } else if (pageType === 'place-performances') {
            targetH2 = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('h2')).find(h => h.textContent.includes('Performances'));
        }

        if (!targetH2) {
            for (let i = 0; i < allH2s.length; i++) {
                if (allH2s[i].compareDocumentPosition(table) & Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING) {
                    targetH2 = allH2s[i];
                } else break;
            }
        }

        if (targetH2) {
            const existing = targetH2.querySelector('.mb-row-count-stat');
            if (existing) existing.remove();
            const span = document.createElement('span');
            span.className = 'mb-row-count-stat';
            const countText = (filteredCount === totalCount) ? `(${totalCount})` : `(${filteredCount} of ${totalCount})`;
            span.textContent = countText;

            // Positioning Logic: Ensure the row count stays immediately after header text, before Master Toggle or Global Filter
            const referenceNode = targetH2.querySelector('.mb-master-toggle') || filterContainer;
            if (referenceNode && referenceNode.parentNode === targetH2) {
                targetH2.insertBefore(span, referenceNode);
            } else {
                targetH2.appendChild(span);
            }

            // Append global filter here for non-grouped pages (Artist/RG pages handle this in renderGroupedTable)
            if (pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups' &&
                pageType !== 'releasegroup-releases' &&
                pageType !== 'place-performances') {
                if (filterContainer.parentNode !== targetH2) {
                    targetH2.appendChild(filterContainer);
                    filterContainer.style.display = 'inline-flex';
                    filterContainer.style.marginLeft = '15px';
                    filterContainer.style.verticalAlign = 'middle';
                }
            }

            Lib.debug('render', `Updated H2 header count: ${countText}`);
        }
    }
#+end_example

*** ChatGPT

#+begin_example
Alright, thanks for the extremely detailed trace — that actually makes the problem very clear. 👍
What you’re seeing is not a random bug; it’s a logic mismatch between two different “modes” of the artist-relationships page, and your logs basically prove it.

I’ll break this down cleanly and then give you a concrete fix strategy.

What is actually happening (high-level)

We have two fundamentally different page contexts, but your script treats them too similarly:

1️⃣ Base artist relationships page (no query params)
/artist/<mbid>/relationships


MusicBrainz behavior:

One huge table

Many subgroups (arranged, instruments, vocals, …)

Each subgroup may contain a “See all N relationships” row

Pagination is hidden behind those “See all” links
Oour script correctly:

Detects this as non-paginated

Extracts subgroup metadata

Stores seeAllUrl per subgroup

Builds grouped tables (H3 sections)
✅ This part works very well.

2️⃣ Filtered relationship page (with link_type_id)
/artist/<mbid>/relationships?page=1&direction=1&link_type_id=148


MusicBrainz behavior:

Page already shows only ONE relationship type

Pagination is real and visible

There are no subgroups

All rows belong to the same relationship type (“instruments” here)

Our script incorrectly re-enters the “non-paginated subgroup extraction mode”.

That’s the core bug.

The smoking guns in your logs
🔥 Bug #1: Wrong fetch base URL (again)

Even though the context contains parameters:

📥 Starting fetch process... {direction: '1', link_type_id: '148'}


We still do:

📥 Initiating fetch for URL:
https://musicbrainz.org/artist/.../relationships


➡️ We drop the query parameters for the first fetch
➡️ Script thinks: “Ah, non-paginated, subgroup table”

🔥 Bug #2: Non-paginated subgroup logic runs when it should not

This line should never happen on a filtered page:

📝 Entering non-paginated subgroup extraction block.


Filtered pages:

Have real pagination

Have no subgroup headers

Should skip subgroup detection entirely

🔥 Bug #3: Grouping collapses to Unknown

On the filtered page:

📥 Summary: Unknown: +250 (Total: 250)


That happens because:

No .subh rows exist

Our grouping logic still expects them

So everything falls into the default bucket (Unknown)

Which is technically correct given the wrong mode — but logically wrong.

The real root cause (important)
❌ Current assumption (implicit)

“If the page initially looks non-paginated, then always treat it as subgroup-based.”

✅ Correct rule

If link_type_id is present → this is a filtered relationship page → treat as paginated single-group mode.

Even if page=1 and maxPage isn’t detected yet.

The fix: split logic by semantic page mode

We already have all the data you need.
We just need one decisive switch early.

1️⃣ Introduce a clear relationship page mode

Add this early, right after parsing URL params:

const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(location.search);

const isFilteredRelationshipPage =
    pageType === 'artist-relationships' &&
    urlParams.has('link_type_id');


Optionally also include entity0, entity1, etc later.

2️⃣ Use that flag to control fetch URL construction
❌ Current (problematic)
const fetchUrl = basePath; // drops params

✅ Fix
const fetchUrl = isFilteredRelationshipPage
    ? location.href
    : basePath;


This alone already fixes half the issue.

3️⃣ Completely skip subgroup extraction on filtered pages

Wrap this block:

if (isNonPaginated) {
    // subgroup extraction
}


➡️ Change to:

if (isNonPaginated && !isFilteredRelationshipPage) {
    // subgroup extraction
}


Filtered pages:

Do not

I repeat: DO NOT
run subgroup extraction.

4️⃣ Force single-group mode for filtered pages

When building groupedRows, inject a synthetic group name:

if (isFilteredRelationshipPage) {
    const h2 = document.querySelector('h2');
    const groupName = h2
        ? h2.textContent.replace(/\s*\(\d+\)\s*$/, '').trim()
        : 'Relationships';

    groupedRows.set(groupName, allRows);
}


That gives you:

"instruments" → 250 rows


instead of:

Unknown → 250 rows

5️⃣ Pagination detection: trust the pager, not assumptions

On filtered pages:

Pagination exists

Even if page=1

So change:

if (isFilteredRelationshipPage) {
    detectMaxPageFromPager(document);
}


Do not default to maxPage = 1 just because subgroup logic was skipped.

What your final behavior will be
From base page

✔ One click: Show all Relationships for Artists
✔ Grouped by relationship type
✔ Each H3 has its own “Show all” deep link

Clicking “Show all” on a group

✔ Redirects to filtered URL
✔ Script detects filtered paginated mode
✔ Fetches all pages for that relationship type
✔ Renders one clean group
✔ Header count updates correctly

Exactly how MusicBrainz should work, just faster and nicer.

TL;DR

My script currently confuses:

“Non-paginated subgroup page”

“Paginated filtered relationship page”

They look similar HTML-wise, but are semantically different.

Key rule to encode:

link_type_id present ⇒ paginated, single relationship type ⇒ NO subgroup logic

If you want, next step I can:

Patch this directly into ShowAllConsolidated.user.js

Or refactor the relationship handling into a clean mini state machine

Just tell me how deep you want to go.
#+end_example

I would expect that all the paginated pages which could be deduced from the URL https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f/relationships?page=1&direction=1&link_type_id=148
would be loaded.

Please fix

Actually the same 'h3 sub-heading which says "Unknown (250)"' shows up evertime I click one of the "Show all" overflow buttons on the previous page. (side question, why is it always 250 ?)

Here my analysys so far

#+begin_example
We have two fundamentally different page contexts, but your script treats them too similarly:

1️⃣ Base artist relationships page (no query params)
/artist/<mbid>/relationships


MusicBrainz behavior:

One huge table

Many subgroups (arranged, instruments, vocals, …)

Each subgroup may contain a “See all N relationships” row

Pagination is hidden behind those “See all” links

My script correctly:

Detects this as non-paginated

Extracts subgroup metadata

Stores seeAllUrl per subgroup

Builds grouped tables (H3 sections)
✅ This part works very well.

2️⃣ Filtered relationship page (with link_type_id)
/artist/<mbid>/relationships?page=1&direction=1&link_type_id=148


MusicBrainz behavior:

Page already shows only ONE relationship type

Pagination is real and visible

There are no subgroups

All rows belong to the same relationship type (“instruments” here)

My script incorrectly re-enters the “non-paginated subgroup extraction mode”.

That’s the core bug.

The smoking guns in your logs
🔥 Bug #1: Wrong fetch base URL (again)

Even though the context contains parameters:

📥 Starting fetch process... {direction: '1', link_type_id: '148'}


We still do:

📥 Initiating fetch for URL:
https://musicbrainz.org/artist/.../relationships


➡️ I drop the query parameters for the first fetch
➡️ Script thinks: “Ah, non-paginated, subgroup table”

🔥 Bug #2: Non-paginated subgroup logic runs when it should not

This line should never happen on a filtered page:

📝 Entering non-paginated subgroup extraction block.


Filtered pages:

Have real pagination

Have no subgroup headers

Should skip subgroup detection entirely

🔥 Bug #3: Grouping collapses to Unknown

On the filtered page:

📥 Summary: Unknown: +250 (Total: 250)


That happens because:

No .subh rows exist

My grouping logic still expects them

So everything falls into the default bucket (Unknown)

Which is technically correct given the wrong mode — but logically wrong.

The real root cause (important)
❌ Current assumption (implicit)

“If the page initially looks non-paginated, then always treat it as subgroup-based.”

✅ Correct rule

If link_type_id is present → this is a filtered relationship page → treat as paginated single-group mode.

Even if page=1 and maxPage isn’t detected yet.

The fix: split logic by semantic page mode
#+end_example

*** result

In the uploaded userscript

1.) Update pageDefinitions Data Structure

Please consolidate the "Place Performances" views into a single definition with multiple buttons. This is the most
effective way to present these options to the user while on the base page, and the params property in the button
configuration ensures the correct filters are applied during the fetch. Also add defaultParams to work-recordings.

#+begin_example
    // --- Configuration: Page Definitions ---
    // Define all supported page types, their detection logic, and specific UI configurations here.
    const pageDefinitions = [
        {
            type: 'place-performances',
            // Matches any performance list for a place
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/place\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/performances/),
            buttons: [
                { label: 'Show all Performances for Recordings', params: { direction: '1', link_type_id: '693' } },
                { label: 'Show all Performances for Engineering', params: { direction: '3', link_type_id: '696' } },
                { label: 'Show all Performances for Producing', params: { direction: '1', link_type_id: '781' } }
            ],
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
        {
            type: 'work-recordings',
            // Matches base work page or a work page already filtered for recordings
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/work\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) || (path.startsWith('/work/') && params.get('direction') === '2' && params.get('link_type_id') === '278'),
            defaultParams: { direction: '2', link_type_id: '278' },
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Work Recordings' } ],
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
        {
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
// ... (rest of the definitions remain the same)
#+end_example

2. Refactor maxPage Detection Logic

In startFetchingProcess, the hardcoded if/else blocks for specific page types should be replaced with a unified check for effectiveParams.

Something like:

#+begin_src bash
    // Determine maxPage based on context
    const effectiveParams = overrideParams || ((!params.get('direction')) ? activeDefinition?.defaultParams : null);

    if (effectiveParams) {
        const paramSource = overrideParams ? 'overrideParams' : 'defaultParams';
        Lib.info('fetch', `Context: ${pageType} with ${paramSource} detected. Fetching maxPage.`, effectiveParams);
        maxPage = await fetchMaxPageGeneric(path, effectiveParams);
    } else {
        Lib.info('fetch', 'Context: Standard page. Fetching maxPage with current URL parameters.');
        maxPage = await fetchMaxPageGeneric(path, Object.fromEntries(params));
    }
#+end_src

but remember there is special handling in the original code for "Context: Standard pagination. Parsing "ul.pagination" from current page"

#+begin_example
        } else {
            Lib.debug('fetch', 'Context: Standard pagination. Parsing "ul.pagination" from current page.');
            const pagination = document.querySelector('ul.pagination');
            if (pagination) {
                const links = Array.from(pagination.querySelectorAll('li a'));
                const nextIdx = links.findIndex(a => a.textContent.trim() === 'Next');
                if (nextIdx > 0) {
                    const urlObj = new URL(links[nextIdx - 1].href, window.location.origin);
                    const p = urlObj.searchParams.get('page');
                    if (p) {
                        maxPage = parseInt(p, 10);
                        Lib.debug('fetch', `Found "Next" link. Extracted page: ${maxPage}`);
                    }
                } else if (links.length > 0) {
                    const pageNumbers = links
                        .map(a => {
                            const p = new URL(a.href, window.location.origin).searchParams.get('page');
                            return p ? parseInt(p, 10) : 1;
                        })
                        .filter(num => !isNaN(num));
                    if (pageNumbers.length > 0) {
                        maxPage = Math.max(...pageNumbers);
                        Lib.debug('fetch', `Parsed page numbers from list. Max found: ${maxPage}`);
                    }
                }
            } else {
                Lib.debug('fetch', 'No pagination element found; assuming single page (maxPage = 1).');
            }
#+end_example


please also integrate this in the above 'if (effectiveParams) {' logic with another 'else if'

3. Refactored Fetch Loop Logic

Similarly, inside the page-fetching loop, the logic that sets fetchUrl.searchParams should be generalized.

#+begin_src bash
  try {
      for (let p = 1; p <= maxPage; p++) {
          if (stopRequested) {
              Lib.info('cleanup', 'Fetch loop stopped at page ' + p);
              break;
          }

          pagesProcessed++;

          const pageStartTime = performance.now();
          const fetchUrl = new URL(baseUrl);
          fetchUrl.searchParams.set('page', p.toString());

          // Apply linkTypeID parameters for specific pageTypes or overrides
          const effectiveParams = overrideParams || ((!params.get('direction')) ? activeDefinition?.defaultParams : null);

          if (effectiveParams) {
              Object.keys(effectiveParams).forEach(k => fetchUrl.searchParams.set(k, effectiveParams[k]));
          } else {
              // Keep existing params if no special handling and no overrides
              params.forEach((v, k) => fetchUrl.searchParams.set(k, v));
          }

          // ... (rest of the loop remains the same)
#+end_src

*** non_paginated: true

In the uploaded userscript

for URLs with parameter definition "non_paginated: true" e.g. pageType "type: 'place-performances-recording-location-for-recording'"
the fetching must initially assume that maxPage is always 1 (so there is NO pagination involved).

These types of pages need a special handling as you can see when downloading e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/place/a727b970-8ea0-4f75-abc8-db131f72aecb/performances

After first fetching the URL itself, meta information about the table structure needs to be parsed in a special data
structure first (which needs to be processed in a second step later), because the rows of the table could have
subheaders like

#+begin_example
 - engineering location for
 - producing location for
 - recording location for
 ...
#+end_example

Every table of this kind always has at least one sub-type grouping header after the main table header, e.g.

#+begin_example
<tr class="subh">
  <th>
  </th>
  <th>
  </th>
  <th colspan="4">engineering location for
  </th>
</tr>
#+end_example

where the grouping header name can be found as text element inside a '<th colspan="x">'.

After the grouping header there are usually a couple of rows of this type, until the next grouping header appears and so
on.

If the number of rows in a sub-grouping exceeds a specific amount of rows, the backend, which generates this kind of
pages, decides not to display all of them, but instead generates the following table row

#+begin_example
<tr>
  <td>
  </td>
  <td colspan="4" style="padding:1em">
    <a href="https://musicbrainz.org/place/a727b970-8ea0-4f75-abc8-db131f72aecb/performances?direction=1&amp;link_type_id=693&amp;page=1">See all 9,127 relationships
    </a>
  </td>
</tr>
#+end_example

which could also be decorated as

#+begin_example
<tr>
  <td>
  </td>
  <td>
  </td>
  <td colspan="4" style="padding: 1em; text-align: right;" class="treleases" title="SUPER&nbsp;MIND&nbsp;CONTROL&nbsp;Ⅱ&nbsp;X&nbsp;TURBO">
    <a href="https://musicbrainz.org/place/a727b970-8ea0-4f75-abc8-db131f72aecb/performances?direction=1&amp;link_type_id=693&amp;page=1">See all 9,127 relationships
    </a>
  </td>
</tr>
#+end_example

The fact is, there is the information how to ALL of these rows would be fetched (in a second step later) for this
sub-group type which is currently being processed. The relevant parts which need to be extracted are the URL, out of

#+begin_example
<a href="https://musicbrainz.org/place/a727b970-8ea0-4f75-abc8-db131f72aecb/performances?direction=1&amp;link_type_id=693&amp;page=1">
#+end_example

and the number of corresponding <total rows> from

#+begin_example
See all <total rows> relationships
#+end_example

SO, basically please when initially parsing the generated page extract the following info in a json data structure:

#+begin_example
 - subgroup header name
 - URL where we would fetch ALL rows per subgroup header
 - <total rows> for this kind of sub-group header (or also called a "relationship")
#+end_example

When the complete json structure per web pagae is available, please output all the information in the console, one row for each entry.

Not every URL really has the row with "See all ... relationships" onyl if a certain row trshehold is exceeded.

*** Ooops

Oops, I made a mistake The whole code "// Special handling for non-paginated pages with sub-grouping (e.g.,
Place-Performances)" should actually be executed already when the URL is initially entered, so at the time when also the
action button is rendered the first time, and NOT in the stage when the  action button has been pressed

*** checkout

            match: (path, params) => (path.startsWith('/place/') && params.get('direction') === '1' && params.get('link_type_id') === '693') || path.match(/\/place\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/performances/),


        {
            type: 'place-performances-recording-location-for-recording',
            match: (path, params) => (path.match(/\/place\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/performances/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Performances for Recordings' } ],
            tableMode: 'single',
            non_paginated: true
        },

*** refine the querySelector

Please refine the querySelector's in the following code block

#+begin_example
                // Special handling for non-paginated pages with sub-grouping (e.g., Place-Performances)
                if (activeDefinition && activeDefinition.non_paginated && p === 1) {
                    Lib.debug('parse', 'Entering non-paginated subgroup extraction block.');
                    const table = doc.querySelector('table.tbl');
                    if (table) {
                        Lib.debug('parse', `Subgroup table found. Scanning ${table.querySelectorAll('tr').length} rows.`);
                        const subgroupData = [];
                        let currentSubgroup = 'Unknown';
                        const rows = table.querySelectorAll('tr');

                        rows.forEach((row, index) => {
                            // Detect sub-grouping headers (e.g., "recording location for")
                            if (row.classList.contains('subh')) {
                                const th = row.querySelector('th');
                                if (th) {
                                    currentSubgroup = th.textContent.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
                                    Lib.debug('parse', `[Row ${index}] Detected subgroup header: "${currentSubgroup}"`);
                                }
                            }

                            // Look for any link that might be a "See all" relationship link
                            const links = row.querySelectorAll('a');
                            links.forEach(link => {
                                const linkText = link.textContent.trim();
                                // Check for "See all" text - this is the most reliable indicator
                                if (linkText.toLowerCase().includes('see all')) {
                                    Lib.debug('parse', `[Row ${index}] Found potential "See all" link: "${linkText}"`);

                                    const href = link.getAttribute('href') || '';
                                    // Extract the number of rows from text like "See all 9,127 relationships"
                                    const match = linkText.match(/See all ([\d,.]+) relationships/i);
                                    const totalRows = match ? parseInt(match[1].replace(/[,.]/g, ''), 10) : 0;

                                    // We accept the link if it contains 'link_type_id' or 'direction',
                                    // OR if it's the specific "See all" pattern we expect.
                                    if (href.includes('link_type_id') || href.includes('direction') || totalRows > 0) {
                                        const metaEntry = {
                                            subgroup: currentSubgroup,
                                            url: new URL(href, window.location.origin).href,
                                            totalRows: totalRows
                                        };
                                        subgroupData.push(metaEntry);
                                        console.log(JSON.stringify(metaEntry));
                                        Lib.info('parse', `Extracted: ${currentSubgroup} -> ${totalRows} total relationships.`);
                                    }
                                }
                            });
                        });
#+end_example

to actually target the '<th colspan=' columns to extract information from the line

#+begin_example
const th = row.querySelector('th');
#+end_example

and

#+begin_example
                            const links = row.querySelectorAll('a');
#+end_example

Every table of this kind always has at least one sub-type grouping header after the main table header, e.g.

#+begin_example
<tr class="subh">
  <th>
  </th>
  <th>
  </th>
  <th colspan="4">engineering location for
  </th>
</tr>
#+end_example

where the grouping header name can be found as text element inside a '<th colspan="x">'.

After the grouping header there are usually a couple of rows of this type, until the next grouping header appears and so
on.

If the number of rows in a sub-grouping exceeds a specific amount of rows, the backend, which generates this kind of
pages, decides not to display all of them, but instead generates the following table row

#+begin_example
<tr>
  <td>
  </td>
  <td colspan="4" style="padding:1em">
    <a href="https://musicbrainz.org/place/a727b970-8ea0-4f75-abc8-db131f72aecb/performances?direction=1&amp;link_type_id=693&amp;page=1">See all 9,127 relationships
    </a>
  </td>
</tr>
#+end_example

After first fetching the URL itself, meta information about the table structure needs to be parsed in a special data
structure first (which needs to be processed in a second step later), because the rows of the table could have
subheaders like

#+begin_example
 - engineering location for
 - producing location for
 - recording location for
 ...
#+end_example

to each subgroup, even if there is no "See all" relationship link row (specify null for the "url" in that case), but

*** extend

In the uploaded userscript

please extend the following code block and ONLY that one

#+begin_example
                // Special handling for non-paginated pages with sub-grouping (e.g., Place-Performances)
                if (activeDefinition && activeDefinition.non_paginated && p === 1) {
                    Lib.debug('parse', 'Entering non-paginated subgroup extraction block.');
                    const table = doc.querySelector('table.tbl');
                    if (table) {
                        Lib.debug('parse', `Subgroup table found. Scanning ${table.querySelectorAll('tr').length} rows.`);
                        const subgroupData = [];
                        let currentSubgroup = 'Unknown';
                        const rows = table.querySelectorAll('tr');

                        Lib.debug('parse', `Testing row count for subgrouping. Total rows: ${rows.length}`);

                        rows.forEach((row, index) => {
                            // 1. Check classes on every row to see what MusicBrainz is actually providing
                            if (index < 10 || row.classList.contains('subh')) {
                                Lib.debug('parse', `[Row ${index}] classes: "${row.className}", nodeName: ${row.nodeName}`);
                            }

                            // 2. Check if the specific subh class is present
                            if (row.classList.contains('subh')) {
                                const th = row.querySelector('th');
                                Lib.debug('parse', `[Row ${index}] FOUND subh. TH content: "${th ? th.textContent.trim() : 'null'}"`);
                            }
                        });

                        // 3. Log the "See all" link search result specifically
                        const seeAllLinks = doc.querySelectorAll('td > a[href*="performances"]');
                        Lib.debug('parse', `DEBUG: Found ${seeAllLinks.length} potential "See all" links.`);
                        seeAllLinks.forEach((link, i) => {
                            Lib.debug('parse', `Link ${i}: text="${link.textContent}", href="${link.href}"`);
                        });

                        rows.forEach((row, index) => {
                            // Detect sub-grouping headers (e.g., "recording location for")
                            // Refined: specifically target the <th colspan="..."> containing the group name
                            if (row.classList.contains('subh')) {
                                const thWithColspan = row.querySelector('th[colspan]');
                                if (thWithColspan) {
                                    currentSubgroup = thWithColspan.textContent.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
                                    Lib.debug('parse', `[Row ${index}] Detected subgroup header: "${currentSubgroup}"`);
                                }
                            }

                            // Look for the "See all" relationship link row
                            // Refined: target the <td> with colspan and look for the "See all" anchor within it
                            const seeAllCell = row.querySelector('td[colspan]');
                            if (seeAllCell) {
                                const link = seeAllCell.querySelector('a');
                                if (link) {
                                    const linkText = link.textContent.trim();
                                    // Check for "See all" text - this is the most reliable indicator
                                    if (linkText.toLowerCase().includes('see all')) {
                                        Lib.debug('parse', `[Row ${index}] Found potential "See all" link: "${linkText}"`);

                                        const href = link.getAttribute('href') || '';
                                        // Extract the number of rows from text like "See all 9,127 relationships"
                                        const match = linkText.match(/See all ([\d,.]+) relationships/i);
                                        const totalRows = match ? parseInt(match[1].replace(/[,.]/g, ''), 10) : 0;

                                        // We accept the link if it contains 'link_type_id' or 'direction',
                                        // OR if it's the specific "See all" pattern we expect.
                                        if (href.includes('link_type_id') || href.includes('direction') || totalRows > 0) {
                                            const metaEntry = {
                                                subgroup: currentSubgroup,
                                                url: new URL(href, window.location.origin).href,
                                                totalRows: totalRows
                                            };
                                            subgroupData.push(metaEntry);
                                            console.log(JSON.stringify(metaEntry));
                                            Lib.info('parse', `Extracted: ${currentSubgroup} -> ${totalRows} total relationships.`);
                                        }
                                    }
                                }
                            }
                        });
                        // Add further processing for subgroupData here if needed
                        if (subgroupData.length > 0) {
                            Lib.info('parse', `Successfully extracted metadata for ${subgroupData.length} subgroups.`);
                        } else {
                            Lib.debug('parse', 'No "See all" links found. All subgroups likely below the display threshold.');
                        }
                    } else {
                        Lib.debug('parse', 'Target table.tbl not found for subgroup extraction.');
                    }
                }
#+end_example

with the following:

The meta entry should get a new sub-parameter "totalRowsRowsPerSubGroup"

#+begin_example
const metaEntry = {
                                    subgroup: currentSubgroup,
                                    url: href ? new URL(href, window.location.origin).href : null,
                                    totalRowsRowsPerSubGroup: totalRowsPerSubGroup,
                                    totalRows: totalRows,
#+end_example

and then

- Track row counts per subgroup: It should count the actual table rows (tr) found between subgroup headers in the new variable "totalRowsPerSubGroup".

- Include all subgroups: A metaEntry should be created for every detected subgroup, regardless of whether a "See all" relationship link is present.

- Handle missing URLs: If no "See all" link is found, the url parameter should be set to null.

- Calculate total rows: The "totalRows" field now reflect the accumulated sum of rows across all subgroups processed so far.

- Dump data to console: The complete subgroupData array should be logged at the end of the extraction process.

but DO not remove, working code, comments and logging statements

Return JUST the new fixed code block to me, thx

*** one more fix

one more fix, for sub-groups with the "See all" links, use as "totalRowsPerSubGroup" for this group the value extracted
from the code in lines, actually the value of "match" in the following code, as these are the real numbers in taht group
if they would later be fetched (the server restricts typically the number of rows to 100 per group if that value is actually exceeded at the backend side)

#+begin_example
// Extract the number of rows from text like "See all 9,127 relationships"
                                        const match = linkText.match(/See all ([\d,.]+) relationships/i);
                                        const totalRows = match ? parseInt(match[1].replace(/[,.]/g, ''), 10) : 0;
#+end_example

** increase text width

In the uploaded library file, double the width of the elements <input type="text"

#+begin_example
        // --- 2. Settings Logic ---
        const settingsInterface = {
            values: {},
            init: function() {
                if (!configSchema) return;
                for (const key in configSchema) {
                    this.values[key] = GM_getValue(key, configSchema[key].default);
                }
            },
            save: function(newValues) {
                for (const key in newValues) {
                    GM_setValue(key, newValues[key]);
                }
                loggerInterface.info('init', "Settings saved. Reloading...");
                location.reload();
            },
            showModal: function() {
                if (!configSchema) return;
                const overlay = document.createElement("div");
                overlay.id = `${scriptId}-settings-overlay`;
                Object.assign(overlay.style, {
                    position: 'fixed', top: '0', left: '0', width: '100%', height: '100%',
                    backgroundColor: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.5)', zIndex: '10000', display: 'flex',
                    justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center'
                });

                const container = document.createElement("div");
                container.id = `${scriptId}-config-container`;
                Object.assign(container.style, {
                    backgroundColor: 'silver', border: '2px outset white', padding: '1em',
                    color: 'black', fontFamily: 'sans-serif', minWidth: '450px'
                });

                const tableRows = Object.entries(configSchema).map(([key, cfg]) => {
                    const inputId = `${scriptId}-input-${key}`;
                    const isCheck = cfg.type === "checkbox";
                    const valAttr = isCheck
                        ? (this.values[key] ? 'checked' : '')
                        : `value="${this.values[key]}"`;

                    return `
                        <tr>
                            <th style="background-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); text-align: left; padding-left: inherit;">
                                <label style="white-space: nowrap; text-shadow: rgb(153, 153, 153) 1px 1px 2px;">
                                    ${cfg.label}: <input type="${cfg.type}" id="${inputId}" ${valAttr} style="${isCheck ? '' : 'width: 60px;'} margin-left: 5px;">
                                </label>
                            </th>
                            <td style="opacity: 0.666; text-align: center;">${cfg.default}</td>
                            <td style="margin-bottom: 0.4em;">${cfg.description}</td>
                        </tr>`;
                }).join('');

                container.innerHTML = `
                    <p style="text-align: right; margin: 0px;">
                        <a id="${scriptId}-reset" style="cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold; color: black;">RESET</a> |
                        <a id="${scriptId}-close" style="cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold; color: black;">CLOSE</a>
                    </p>
                    <h4 style="text-shadow: white 0px 0px 8px; font-size: 1.5em; margin-top: 0px;">${scriptName.toUpperCase()}</h4>
                    <p>Settings are applied immediately upon saving.</p>
                    <table border="2" cellpadding="4" cellspacing="1" style="width: 100%; border-collapse: separate; background: #eee;">
                        <thead>
                            <tr style="background-color: #ccc;">
                                <th>setting</th>
                                <th>default setting</th>
                                <th>description</th>
                            </tr>
                        </thead>
                        <tbody>${tableRows}</tbody>
                    </table>
                    <div style="margin-top: 15px; text-align: right;">
                        <button id="${scriptId}-save-btn" style="padding: 4px 12px; cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold;">SAVE</button>
                    </div>
                `;

                overlay.appendChild(container);
                document.body.appendChild(overlay);

                const closeDialog = () => {
                    if (document.body.contains(overlay)) document.body.removeChild(overlay);
                    window.removeEventListener("keydown", handleEsc);
                };
                const handleEsc = (e) => { if (e.key === "Escape") closeDialog(); };
                window.addEventListener("keydown", handleEsc);

                document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-save-btn`).onclick = () => {
                    const newValues = {};
                    for (const key in configSchema) {
                        const input = document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-input-${key}`);
                        newValues[key] = configSchema[key].type === "checkbox" ? input.checked : parseInt(input.value, 10);
                    }
                    this.save(newValues);
                    closeDialog();
                };
                document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-reset`).onclick = () => {
                    for (const key in configSchema) {
                        const input = document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-input-${key}`);
                        if (configSchema[key].type === "checkbox") input.checked = configSchema[key].default;
                        else input.value = configSchema[key].default;
                    }
                };
                document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-close`).onclick = closeDialog;
                overlay.onclick = (e) => { if (e.target === overlay) closeDialog(); };
            }
        };
#+end_example

https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f/releases?va=1


Given the following code fragment from the uploaded userscript which is based on URLs of type "https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f"

#+begin_example
    // Logic for the 4 specific Artist views
    const isOfficialArtist =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '0' &&
        params.get('va') === '0';
    const isOfficialArtistBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isOfficialArtist;

    const isNonOfficialArtist =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '1' &&
        params.get('va') === '0';
    const isNonOfficialArtistBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isNonOfficialArtist;

    const isOfficialVariousArtists =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '0' &&
        params.get('va') === '1';
    const isOfficialVariousArtistsBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isOfficialVariousArtists;

    const isNonOfficialVariousArtists =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '1' &&
        params.get('va') === '1';
    const isNonOfficialVariousArtistsBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isNonOfficialVariousArtists;

    const isSpecialArtistView = isOfficialArtist || isNonOfficialArtist || isOfficialVariousArtists || isNonOfficialVariousArtists;
#+end_example

* DONE Include more log statements

In the uploaded script,
put log statements in side the "if then else" block

#+begin_example
        // Determine maxPage based on context
        if (isWorkBase) {
            maxPage = await fetchMaxPageGeneric(path, { direction: '2', link_type_id: '278' });
        } else if (overrideParams) {
            maxPage = await fetchMaxPageGeneric(path, overrideParams);
        } else {
            const pagination = document.querySelector('ul.pagination');
            if (pagination) {
                const links = Array.from(pagination.querySelectorAll('li a'));
                const nextIdx = links.findIndex(a => a.textContent.trim() === 'Next');
                if (nextIdx > 0) {
                    const urlObj = new URL(links[nextIdx - 1].href, window.location.origin);
                    const p = urlObj.searchParams.get('page');
                    if (p) maxPage = parseInt(p, 10);
                } else if (links.length > 0) {
                    const pageNumbers = links
                        .map(a => {
                            const p = new URL(a.href, window.location.origin).searchParams.get('page');
                            return p ? parseInt(p, 10) : 1;
                        })
                        .filter(num => !isNaN(num));
                    if (pageNumbers.length > 0) maxPage = Math.max(...pageNumbers);
                }
            }
        }
#+end_example

* Tampermonkey in general
** enable file access to scripts

#+begin_example
A402 There are multiple possible ways:

Chrome (and derivates users) can export and @require the local copy
Open Tampermonkey's dashboard, open the script and copy its content.
Create a local file and paste the content
Remove all content from the script inside Tampermonkey except the "UserScript" header
Add a @require tag with the path to the local file that you want to include in the script. For example, if the file is located at C:\Users\[USERNAME]\Documents\myscript.js, you would add the following line to the script:
// @require       file://C:/Users/[USERNAME]/Documents/myscript.js
Finally enable local file access.
#+end_example

to use the equivalent code like the "ShowAllEntityData.user.js" userscript does

#+begin_example
    const SCRIPT_BASE_NAME = "ReleasePageEnhancer";
    // SCRIPT_ID is derived from SCRIPT_BASE_NAME: CamelCase → kebab-case, lower-cased, prepend "vz-mb-"
    const SCRIPT_ID = 'vz-mb-' + SCRIPT_BASE_NAME.replace(/([A-Z])/g, '-$1').toLowerCase().replace(/^-/, '');
    const SCRIPT_NAME = (typeof GM_info !== 'undefined' && GM_info.script) ? GM_info.script.name : SCRIPT_BASE_NAME;

    // Remote URLs for changelog and help text.
    // The changelog is fetched and the GM menu item registered by VZ_MBLibrary
    // (via remoteConfig passed to the constructor below).
    // The help URL is only used lazily by showAppHelp() via Lib.fetchCachedText().
    const REMOTE_BASE          = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vzell/mb-userscripts/master/';
    const REMOTE_HELP_URL      = REMOTE_BASE + SCRIPT_BASE_NAME + '_HELP.txt';
    const REMOTE_CHANGELOG_URL = REMOTE_BASE + SCRIPT_BASE_NAME + '_CHANGELOG.json';
    const REMOTE_CACHE_TTL_MS  = 60 * 60 * 1000; // 1 hour
    const CACHE_KEY_HELP       = SCRIPT_BASE_NAME.toLowerCase() + '-remote-help-text';
    const CACHE_KEY_CHANGELOG  = SCRIPT_BASE_NAME.toLowerCase() + '-remote-changelog';
#+end_example

4.)

add a divider section to "const configSchema"

#+begin_example
        divider_caa_pics: {
            type: 'divider',
            label: '🖼️ CAA/EAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY'
        },
#+end_example

5.)

also change the block

#+begin_example
    // Initialize VZ-MBLibrary (Logger + Settings + Changelog)
    const Lib = (typeof VZMBLibrary !== 'undefined')
          ? new VZMBLibrary(SCRIPT_ID, SCRIPT_NAME, configSchema, changelog, DEBUG_ENABLED)
          : {
              settings: {}, // Fallback
              info: console.log, debug: console.log, error: console.error, time: console.time, timeEnd: console.timeEnd
          };

    Lib.info('init', "Script loaded with external library!");
#+end_example

to use the following features (like in "ShowAllEntityData.user.js")

#+begin_example
    //--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // Initialize VZ-MBLibrary (Logger + Settings + Changelog)
    // Use a ref object to avoid circular dependency during initialization
    const settings = {};
    const remoteConfig = {
        changelogUrl:      REMOTE_CHANGELOG_URL,
        cacheKeyChangelog: CACHE_KEY_CHANGELOG,
        cacheTtlMs:        REMOTE_CACHE_TTL_MS
    };

    const Lib = (typeof VZ_MBLibrary !== 'undefined')
          ? new VZ_MBLibrary(SCRIPT_ID, SCRIPT_NAME, configSchema, null, () => {
              // Dynamic check: returns current value of debug setting
              return settings.sa_enable_debug_logging ?? false;
          }, remoteConfig)
          : {
              settings: {},
              info: console.log, debug: console.log, error: console.error, warn: console.warn, time: console.time, timeEnd: console.timeEnd
          };

    // Get version information dynamically
    const scriptVersion = (typeof GM_info !== 'undefined' && GM_info.script) ? GM_info.script.version : 'unknown';
    const libVersion = (Lib && Lib.version) ? Lib.version : 'unknown';

    // Copy settings reference so the callback can access them
    Object.assign(settings, Lib.settings);
#+end_example

specific browser menu) with the new library (uploaded again as "VZ_MBLibrary.user.js") and the current codebase of the

* Userscripts - WORKFLOW
** temporary workflow

If you find yourself updating the library frequently and don't want to bump the main script version every time, you can temporarily append a "cache buster" to the @require line in your local dev copy:

#+begin_example
// During development only:
// @require https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vzell/mb-userscripts@master/lib/VZMBLibrary.user.js?v=1
#+end_example

** development workflow

The "Hybrid" Workflow (Recommended)
To prevent accidentally breaking things for others, here is how the "pros" usually handle this:

1. The Local Main Script
Create a second script in Tampermonkey called VZ: ShowAll (DEV).

Paste your main code there.

Point the @require to your local path: // @require file:///C:/.../VZMBLibrary.user.js.

Disable the "Production" version of the script in the Tampermonkey dashboard so they don't conflict.

2. The Development Library
Keep your VZMBLibrary.user.js on your hard drive. Open it in a real code editor (VS Code, Sublime, etc.).

Every time you save the file in your editor, simply refresh the MusicBrainz page.

The "DEV" script will immediately see the changes.

3. The Commit Step
When you are happy with the library:

Push the library to GitHub.

Go back to your Production script (ShowAllConsolidated.user.js).

Update its @version and ensure its @require still points to the raw.githubusercontent or cdn.jsdelivr URL.

Push the Production script.

** header
*** CDN

#+begin_example
// ==UserScript==
// @name         VZ: MusicBrainz - Accumulate Paginated MusicBrainz Pages With Filtering And Sorting Capabilities
// @namespace    https://github.com/vzell/mb-userscripts
// @version      1.6.0+2026-02-02
// @description  Consolidated tool to accumulate paginated MusicBrainz lists (Events, Recordings, Releases, Works, etc.) into a single view with real-time filtering and sorting
// @author       Gemini (directed by vzell)
// @tag          AI generated
// @homepageURL  https://github.com/vzell/mb-userscripts
// @supportURL   https://github.com/vzell/mb-userscripts/issues
// @downloadURL  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vzell/mb-userscripts/master/ShowAllConsolidated.user.js
// @updateURL    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vzell/mb-userscripts/master/ShowAllConsolidated.user.js
// @icon         https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?sz=64&domain=musicbrainz.org
// @require      https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@jaames/iro@5
// @require      https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vzell/mb-userscripts@master/lib/VZMBLibrary.user.js
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/artist/*
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/release-group/*
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/work/*

// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/series/*
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/place/*/events

// @grant        GM_info
// @grant        GM_setValue
// @grant        GM_getValue
// @grant        GM_registerMenuCommand
// @grant        GM_unregisterMenuCommand
// @license      MIT
// ==/UserScript==

#+begin_example
// ==UserScript==
// @name         Long Running Operation Overlay
// @namespace    ui.long.operation.overlay
// @match        *://*/*
// @grant        none
// ==/UserScript==

(function () {
    'use strict';

    // ---------- STYLES ----------
    const style = document.createElement('style');
    style.textContent = `
        .lr-overlay {
            position: fixed;
            inset: 0;
            background: rgba(15, 15, 15, 0.45);
            backdrop-filter: blur(4px);
            z-index: 999999;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            opacity: 0;
            pointer-events: none;
            transition: opacity 0.2s ease;
        }

        .lr-overlay.visible {
            opacity: 1;
            pointer-events: all;
        }

        .lr-card {
            background: #1e1e1e;
            color: #f2f2f2;
            padding: 24px 28px;
            border-radius: 14px;
            box-shadow: 0 20px 50px rgba(0,0,0,0.4);
            min-width: 260px;
            text-align: center;
            font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;
        }

        .lr-spinner {
            width: 42px;
            height: 42px;
            border: 4px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2);
            border-top-color: #4da3ff;
            border-radius: 50%;
            animation: lr-spin 0.8s linear infinite;
            margin: 0 auto 14px;
        }

        @keyframes lr-spin {
            to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
        }

        .lr-text {
            font-size: 14px;
            opacity: 0.85;
        }
    `;
    document.head.appendChild(style);

    // ---------- ELEMENT ----------
    const overlay = document.createElement('div');
    overlay.className = 'lr-overlay';
    overlay.innerHTML = `
        <div class="lr-card">
            <div class="lr-spinner"></div>
            <div class="lr-text">Working…</div>
        </div>
    `;
    document.body.appendChild(overlay);

    const textNode = overlay.querySelector('.lr-text');

    // ---------- API ----------
    window.LongRunningUI = {
        show(text = 'Working…') {
            textNode.textContent = text;
            overlay.classList.add('visible');
        },

        update(text) {
            textNode.textContent = text;
        },

        hide() {
            overlay.classList.remove('visible');
        }
    };
})();
#+end_example

All except "Integrate directly into your existing filter/sort script without touching logic"

#+begin_example
// ==UserScript==
// @name         Long Operation UI – Ultimate
// @namespace    userscript.longop.ui.ultimate
// @match        *://*/*
// @grant        none
// ==/UserScript==

(function () {
    'use strict';

    if (window.LongOpUI) return; // 🔒 singleton safety

    /* ---------------- CONFIG ---------------- */
    const AUTO_SHOW_DELAY = 120; // ms
    const MAX_STACK_LINES = 5;

    /* ---------------- STATE ---------------- */
    let visible = false;
    let cancelled = false;
    let startTime = 0;
    let showTimer = null;
    let elapsedTimer = null;
    let progress = null;

    /* ---------------- STYLES ---------------- */
    const style = document.createElement('style');
    style.textContent = `
        .lop-overlay {
            position: fixed;
            inset: 0;
            background: rgba(0,0,0,.45);
            backdrop-filter: blur(4px);
            z-index: 999999;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            opacity: 0;
            pointer-events: none;
            transition: opacity .2s ease;
        }

        .lop-overlay.visible {
            opacity: 1;
            pointer-events: all;
        }

        .lop-card {
            background: #1b1b1b;
            color: #eee;
            padding: 24px 28px;
            border-radius: 14px;
            width: 340px;
            box-shadow: 0 30px 70px rgba(0,0,0,.55);
            font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;
        }

        .lop-header {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 12px;
            margin-bottom: 14px;
        }

        .lop-spinner {
            width: 36px;
            height: 36px;
            border: 4px solid rgba(255,255,255,.2);
            border-top-color: #4da3ff;
            border-radius: 50%;
            animation: lop-spin .8s linear infinite;
        }

        @keyframes lop-spin {
            to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
        }

        .lop-lines {
            font-size: 13px;
            margin-bottom: 12px;
        }

        .lop-lines div {
            opacity: .85;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
        }

        .lop-time {
            font-size: 11px;
            opacity: .6;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }

        .lop-progress {
            height: 6px;
            background: #2a2a2a;
            border-radius: 99px;
            overflow: hidden;
            margin-bottom: 14px;
        }

        .lop-progress-inner {
            height: 100%;
            width: 0%;
            background: #4da3ff;
            transition: width .2s ease;
        }

        .lop-actions {
            text-align: right;
        }

        .lop-cancel {
            background: #2d2d2d;
            border: none;
            color: #ff6b6b;
            padding: 6px 14px;
            border-radius: 999px;
            cursor: pointer;
            font-size: 12px;
        }

        .lop-cancel:hover {
            background: #383838;
        }
    `;
    document.head.appendChild(style);

    /* ---------------- DOM ---------------- */
    const overlay = document.createElement('div');
    overlay.className = 'lop-overlay';
    overlay.innerHTML = `
        <div class="lop-card">
            <div class="lop-header">
                <div class="lop-spinner"></div>
                <div class="lop-lines"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="lop-time">0.0 s</div>
            <div class="lop-progress">
                <div class="lop-progress-inner"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="lop-actions">
                <button class="lop-cancel">Cancel (Esc)</button>
            </div>
        </div>
    `;
    document.body.appendChild(overlay);

    const linesEl = overlay.querySelector('.lop-lines');
    const timeEl = overlay.querySelector('.lop-time');
    const progressEl = overlay.querySelector('.lop-progress-inner');
    const cancelBtn = overlay.querySelector('.lop-cancel');

    /* ---------------- EVENTS ---------------- */
    cancelBtn.addEventListener('click', cancel);
    document.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
        if (e.key === 'Escape' && visible) cancel();
    });

    function cancel() {
        cancelled = true;
        pushLine('Cancelling…');
    }

    /* ---------------- HELPERS ---------------- */
    function show() {
        if (visible) return;
        visible = true;
        overlay.classList.add('visible');

        elapsedTimer = setInterval(() => {
            timeEl.textContent =
                ((performance.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(1) + ' s';
        }, 100);
    }

    function hide() {
        clearTimeout(showTimer);
        clearInterval(elapsedTimer);
        overlay.classList.remove('visible');
        visible = false;
        cancelled = false;
        progress = null;
        progressEl.style.width = '0%';
        linesEl.innerHTML = '';
    }

    function pushLine(text) {
        const div = document.createElement('div');
        div.textContent = text;
        linesEl.appendChild(div);

        while (linesEl.children.length > MAX_STACK_LINES) {
            linesEl.removeChild(linesEl.firstChild);
        }
    }

    /* ---------------- PUBLIC API ---------------- */
    window.LongOpUI = {
        start(text = 'Working…') {
            startTime = performance.now();
            cancelled = false;
            linesEl.innerHTML = '';
            pushLine(text);
            timeEl.textContent = '0.0 s';

            showTimer = setTimeout(show, AUTO_SHOW_DELAY);
        },

        phase(text) {
            pushLine(text);
        },

        progress(current, total) {
            if (!total) return;
            progress = Math.min(100, (current / total) * 100);
            progressEl.style.width = progress.toFixed(1) + '%';
        },

        update(text) {
            pushLine(text);
        },

        isCancelled() {
            return cancelled;
        },

        done() {
            hide();
        }
    };
})();
#+end_example

#+begin_example
Usage patterns (real-world ready)
Sorting with phases
LongOpUI.start('Sorting table');
await new Promise(requestAnimationFrame);

LongOpUI.phase('Collecting rows');
collectRows();

LongOpUI.phase('Applying sort');
sortRows();

LongOpUI.phase('Rendering');
renderRows();

LongOpUI.done();

Chunked filtering + progress + cancel
LongOpUI.start('Filtering table');
await new Promise(requestAnimationFrame);

for (let i = 0; i < groups.length; i++) {
    if (LongOpUI.isCancelled()) break;

    filterGroup(groups[i]);
    LongOpUI.progress(i + 1, groups.length);

    await new Promise(requestAnimationFrame);
}

LongOpUI.done();

🧠 Design decisions (why this survives abuse)

Singleton-safe → multiple scripts won’t spawn multiple overlays

ESC + Cancel → universal escape hatch

Stacked phases → explains “what’s happening now”

Auto-delay → zero flicker on fast ops

Progress optional → works even when total unknown

Userscript-safe CSS → no site collisions
#+end_example

#+begin_example
// ==UserScript==
// @name         Long Operation UI – Ultimate++
// @namespace    userscript.longop.ui.ultimatepp
// @match        *://*/*
// @grant        none
// ==/UserScript==

(function () {
    'use strict';
    if (window.LongOpUI) return;

    /* ---------------- CONFIG ---------------- */
    const AUTO_SHOW_DELAY = 120;
    const MAX_STACK_LINES = 5;
    const TOAST_DURATION = 4000;
    const BLOCKING_THRESHOLD = 200; // ms event loop stall
    const DEBUG = true; // 🔧 toggle debug logging

    /* ---------------- STATE ---------------- */
    let visible = false;
    let cancelled = false;
    let startTime = 0;
    let showTimer = null;
    let elapsedTimer = null;
    let progress = null;
    let phaseTimings = [];
    let lastPhaseTime = 0;

    /* ---------------- STYLES ---------------- */
    const style = document.createElement('style');
    style.textContent = `
        .lop-overlay {
            position: fixed;
            inset: 0;
            background: rgba(0,0,0,.45);
            backdrop-filter: blur(4px);
            z-index: 999999;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            opacity: 0;
            pointer-events: none;
            transition: opacity .2s ease;
        }
        .lop-overlay.visible {
            opacity: 1;
            pointer-events: all;
        }
        .lop-card {
            background: #1b1b1b;
            color: #eee;
            padding: 24px 28px;
            border-radius: 14px;
            width: 340px;
            box-shadow: 0 30px 70px rgba(0,0,0,.55);
            font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;
        }
        .lop-header {
            display: flex;
            gap: 12px;
            margin-bottom: 14px;
        }
        .lop-spinner {
            width: 36px;
            height: 36px;
            border: 4px solid rgba(255,255,255,.2);
            border-top-color: #4da3ff;
            border-radius: 50%;
            animation: lop-spin .8s linear infinite;
        }
        @keyframes lop-spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }
        .lop-lines div {
            font-size: 13px;
            opacity: .85;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
        }
        .lop-time {
            font-size: 11px;
            opacity: .6;
            margin: 10px 0;
        }
        .lop-progress {
            height: 6px;
            background: #2a2a2a;
            border-radius: 99px;
            overflow: hidden;
            margin-bottom: 14px;
        }
        .lop-progress-inner {
            height: 100%;
            width: 0%;
            background: #4da3ff;
            transition: width .2s ease;
        }
        .lop-actions { text-align: right; }
        .lop-cancel {
            background: #2d2d2d;
            border: none;
            color: #ff6b6b;
            padding: 6px 14px;
            border-radius: 999px;
            cursor: pointer;
            font-size: 12px;
        }
        .lop-toast {
            position: fixed;
            bottom: 24px;
            right: 24px;
            background: #1b1b1b;
            color: #eee;
            padding: 10px 14px;
            border-radius: 10px;
            box-shadow: 0 15px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.5);
            font-size: 12px;
            opacity: 0;
            transition: opacity .3s ease;
            z-index: 999999;
        }
        .lop-toast.visible { opacity: 1; }
    `;
    document.head.appendChild(style);

    /* ---------------- DOM ---------------- */
    const overlay = document.createElement('div');
    overlay.className = 'lop-overlay';
    overlay.innerHTML = `
        <div class="lop-card">
            <div class="lop-header">
                <div class="lop-spinner"></div>
                <div class="lop-lines"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="lop-time">0.0 s</div>
            <div class="lop-progress"><div class="lop-progress-inner"></div></div>
            <div class="lop-actions">
                <button class="lop-cancel">Cancel (Esc)</button>
            </div>
        </div>
    `;
    document.body.appendChild(overlay);

    const toast = document.createElement('div');
    toast.className = 'lop-toast';
    document.body.appendChild(toast);

    const linesEl = overlay.querySelector('.lop-lines');
    const timeEl = overlay.querySelector('.lop-time');
    const progressEl = overlay.querySelector('.lop-progress-inner');

    overlay.querySelector('.lop-cancel').onclick = cancel;
    document.addEventListener('keydown', e => e.key === 'Escape' && visible && cancel());

    /* ---------------- CORE ---------------- */
    function cancel() {
        cancelled = true;
        pushLine('Cancelling…');
    }

    function show() {
        if (visible) return;
        visible = true;
        overlay.classList.add('visible');
        elapsedTimer = setInterval(updateTime, 100);
    }

    function hide() {
        clearTimeout(showTimer);
        clearInterval(elapsedTimer);
        overlay.classList.remove('visible');
        visible = false;
        cancelled = false;
        progressEl.style.width = '0%';
        linesEl.innerHTML = '';
    }

    function updateTime() {
        timeEl.textContent =
            ((performance.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(1) + ' s';
    }

    function pushLine(text) {
        if (DEBUG) logPhaseTiming(text);
        const div = document.createElement('div');
        div.textContent = text;
        linesEl.appendChild(div);
        while (linesEl.children.length > MAX_STACK_LINES)
            linesEl.removeChild(linesEl.firstChild);
    }

    function showToast(text) {
        toast.textContent = text;
        toast.classList.add('visible');
        setTimeout(() => toast.classList.remove('visible'), TOAST_DURATION);
    }

    /* ---------------- DEBUG & TIMING ---------------- */
    function logPhaseTiming(label) {
        const now = performance.now();
        if (lastPhaseTime) {
            phaseTimings.push({
                label,
                duration: now - lastPhaseTime
            });
        }
        lastPhaseTime = now;
    }

    function dumpDebug() {
        if (!DEBUG || !phaseTimings.length) return;
        console.groupCollapsed('[LongOpUI] Phase timings');
        phaseTimings.forEach(p =>
            console.log(`${p.label}: ${p.duration.toFixed(1)} ms`)
        );
        console.groupEnd();
    }

    /* --------- EVENT LOOP BLOCKING DETECTION --------- */
    let lastTick = performance.now();
    setInterval(() => {
        const now = performance.now();
        const delta = now - lastTick;
        if (visible && delta > BLOCKING_THRESHOLD) {
            pushLine(`⚠ UI blocked for ${delta.toFixed(0)} ms`);
        }
        lastTick = now;
    }, 50);

    /* -------- console.time() INTEGRATION -------- */
    const origTime = console.time;
    const origEnd = console.timeEnd;
    const timers = new Map();

    console.time = label => {
        timers.set(label, performance.now());
        origTime.call(console, label);
    };

    console.timeEnd = label => {
        if (timers.has(label)) {
            const d = performance.now() - timers.get(label);
            pushLine(`${label}: ${d.toFixed(1)} ms`);
            timers.delete(label);
        }
        origEnd.call(console, label);
    };

    /* ---------------- PUBLIC API ---------------- */
    window.LongOpUI = {
        start(text = 'Working…') {
            startTime = performance.now();
            lastPhaseTime = startTime;
            phaseTimings = [];
            cancelled = false;
            linesEl.innerHTML = '';
            pushLine(text);
            timeEl.textContent = '0.0 s';
            showTimer = setTimeout(show, AUTO_SHOW_DELAY);
        },

        phase(text) {
            pushLine(text);
        },

        progress(current, total) {
            if (total)
                progressEl.style.width =
                    Math.min(100, current / total * 100) + '%';
        },

        isCancelled() {
            return cancelled;
        },

        done() {
            const total = performance.now() - startTime;
            hide();
            showToast(`Last operation took ${(total / 1000).toFixed(2)} s`);
            dumpDebug();
        }
    };
})();
#+end_example

Following your coding preferences, here is how you might log a "busy" state in a script while maintaining your console debug logs and clean formatting.

/**
 * Updates the UI and logs the busy state.
 */
function setBusyState(isLoading) {
    const statusIcon = isLoading ? "⏳" : "✅";
    
    // Existing logic for UI updates
    document.getElementById("status-indicator").innerText = statusIcon;

    // console debug logging statements
    console.debug(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] Busy state changed: ${isLoading}`);
}

// Example usage
setBusyState(true);

#+begin_example
function triggerSort(columnIdx, table) {
    // 1. Show the loading state immediately
    sortTimerDisplay.textContent = 'Sorting...⏳';
    document.body.classList.add('mb-sorting-active');

    // 2. Use setTimeout to defer the heavy work
    setTimeout(() => {
        const startTime = performance.now();
        
        // --- YOUR HEAVY SORTING LOGIC HERE ---
        // (e.g., allRows.sort(...) and renderFinalTable(...))
        
        const duration = ((performance.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(2);
        
        // 3. Clear the loading state
        sortTimerDisplay.textContent = `Sorted in ${duration}s`;
        document.body.classList.remove('mb-sorting-active');
    }, 50); // A short 50ms delay is enough for the UI to paint the "⏳" icon
}

/*
 *      VZ: MusicBrainz - Accumulate Paginated MusicBrainz Pages With Filtering And Sorting Capabilities
 *      is an userscript which accumulates paginated MusicBrainz lists (Events, Recordings, Releases, Works, etc.)
 *      into a single view with real-time filtering and sorting.
 *
 *      This script has been created by giving the right facts and asking the right questions to Gemini.
 *      When Gemini gots stuck, I asked ChatGPT for help, until I got everything right.
 *
 *      NOTICE: This script has only been tested with Tampermonkey (>=v5.4.1) on Vivaldi, Chrome and Firefox.
 */

    {version: '0.9.0+2026-01-28', description: '1st official release version.'}
];

** 1

In the uploaded userscript,
the '<div id="mb-show-all-controls-container"' should be updated with "statusDisplay.textContent" during the following stages from top to bottom with the content of the "Status text" column in the next table:

| Stage              | Status text                                                                                | Visual progress bar | Visual progress bar text       |
|--------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------|
| Initializing       | Initializing...                                                                            |                     |                                |
| Get maxPage        | Getting total pages...                                                                     |                     |                                |
| Get maxPage done   | Getting total pages...<total page numbers>                                                 |                     |                                |
|--------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------|
| Loading            | Loading page <current page number> of <total page numbers>... (current rows up to now)     | visible             | Estimated remaining time: <e>s |
| Loading finished   | Loading page <current page number> of <total page numbers>... (current total rows)         | visible             | Estimated remaining time: <e>s |
|--------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------|
| Fetching finished  | Loaded <total page numbers> pages (current rows), Fetching time: <f>s                      |                     |                                |
|--------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------|
| Rendering          | Loaded <total page numbers> pages (current rows), Fetching time: <f>s                      |                     |                                |
| Rendering finished | Loaded <total page numbers> pages (current rows), Fetching time: <f>s, Rendering time <r>s |                     |                                |

All the steps should also have detailed console logging.

The "Visual progress bar" should vanish as soon we change from stage "Loading finished" to "Fetching finished".

Please do not make any other changes to working code, comments and logging statements.
