the '<div id="mb-show-all-controls-container"' should have a modern visually nice appearance during the following stages from top to bottom in the next table:

I
| Stage                      | Optional         | Status text                                                                                        | Visual progress bar | Visual progress bar text       | Visual spinning icon |
|----------------------------+------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------+----------------------|
| Initializing               |                  | Initializing...                                                                                    |                     |                                |                      |
| Get maxPage                |                  | Getting total pages...                                                                             |                     |                                |                      |
| Get maxPage done           |                  | Getting total pages...<total page numbers>                                                         |                     |                                |                      |
|----------------------------+------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------+----------------------|
| Loading                    | x (if maxPage=1) | Loading page <current page number> of <total page numbers>... (current rows up to now)             | visible             | Estimated remaining time: <e>s |                      |
| Loading finished           | x (if maxPage=1) | Loading page <current page number> of <total page numbers>... (current total rows)                 | visible             | Estimated remaining time: <e>s |                      |
|----------------------------+------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------------------+----------------------|
| Fetching finished          |                  | Loaded <total page numbers> pages (current rows), Fetching time: <f>s                              |                     |                                | visible              |

In the uploaded script,
could the following section somehow be refactored, because in the future there will be much more URLs supported, with more "pageType"s and different query parameters

#+begin_example
    let pageType = '';
    let headerContainer = document.querySelector('.artistheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.rgheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.labelheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.seriesheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.placeheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.recordingheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('h1 a bdi')?.parentNode ||
                          document.querySelector('h1');

    // Logic for Work pages
    const isWorkRecordings =
        path.startsWith('/work/') &&
        params.get('direction') === '2' &&
        params.get('link_type_id') === '278';
    const isWorkBase = path.match(/\/work\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isWorkRecordings;

    // Logic for the 4 specific Artist Release Groups views
    const isOfficialArtist =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '0' &&
        params.get('va') === '0';
    const isOfficialArtistBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isOfficialArtist;

    const isNonOfficialArtist =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '1' &&
        params.get('va') === '0';
    const isNonOfficialArtistBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isNonOfficialArtist;

    const isOfficialVariousArtists =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '0' &&
        params.get('va') === '1';
    const isOfficialVariousArtistsBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isOfficialVariousArtists;

    const isNonOfficialVariousArtists =
        path.startsWith('/artist/') &&
        params.get('all') === '1' &&
        params.get('va') === '1';
    const isNonOfficialVariousArtistsBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !isNonOfficialVariousArtists;

    const isSpecialArtistView = isOfficialArtist || isNonOfficialArtist || isOfficialVariousArtists || isNonOfficialVariousArtists;

    // Logic for the 2 specific Artist Releases views
    const isOfficialArtistReleases =
        path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases$/) &&
        params.get('va') === '0';
    const isOfficialArtistReleasesBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases$/) && !isOfficialArtistReleases;

    const isVariousArtistReleases =
        path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases$/) &&
        params.get('va') === '1';
    const isVariousArtistReleasesBase = path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases$/) && !isVariousArtistReleases;

    const isSpecialReleasesView = isOfficialArtistReleases || isVariousArtistReleases;

    // Determine page type using the requested priority structure
    if (isWorkRecordings || isWorkBase) pageType = 'work-recordings';
    else if (isSpecialArtistView || path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/)) pageType = 'artist-releasegroups';
    else if (isSpecialReleasesView || path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases$/)) pageType = 'releases'; // Updated for Releases
    else if (path.includes('/recordings')) pageType = 'recordings';
    else if (path.includes('/releases')) pageType = 'releases';
    else if (path.includes('/works')) pageType = 'works';
    else if (path.includes('/release-group/')) pageType = 'releasegroup-releases';
    else if (path.includes('/recording')) pageType = 'recording-releases';
    else if (path.includes('/label')) pageType = 'label-releases';
    else if (path.includes('/series')) pageType = 'series-releases';
    else if (path.match(/\/place\/.*\/events/)) pageType = 'place-concerts';
    else if (path.match(/\/place\/.*\/performances/)) pageType = 'place-performances';
    else if (path.includes('/events')) pageType = 'events';

    if (pageType) Lib.prefix = `[VZ-ShowAllEntities: ${pageType}]`;
    Lib.info('init', 'Initializing script for path:', path);

    if (!pageType || !headerContainer) {
        Lib.error('init', 'Required elements not found. Terminating.', { pageType, hasHeader: !!headerContainer });
        return;
    }

    const typesWithSplitCD = ['releasegroup-releases', 'releases', 'recording-releases', 'label-releases', 'series-releases'];
    const typesWithSplitLocation = ['events'];

    // --- UI Elements ---
    const controlsContainer = document.createElement('div');
    controlsContainer.id = 'mb-show-all-controls-container';
    controlsContainer.style.cssText = 'display:inline-flex; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:center; gap:8px; margin-left:10px; vertical-align:middle; line-height:1;';

    const allActionButtons = [];

    if (pageType === 'artist-releasegroups') {
        const extraConfigs = [
            { title: 'Official artist RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
            { title: 'Non-official artist RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
            { title: 'Official various artists RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '1' } },
            { title: 'Non-official various artists RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '1' } }
        ];

        extraConfigs.forEach(conf => {
            const eb = document.createElement('button');
            eb.textContent = conf.title;
            eb.style.cssText = 'font-size:0.5em; padding:2px 6px; cursor:pointer; transition:transform 0.1s, box-shadow 0.1s; height:24px; box-sizing:border-box;';
            eb.type = 'button';
            eb.onclick = (e) => startFetchingProcess(e, conf.params);
            controlsContainer.appendChild(eb);
            allActionButtons.push(eb);
        });
    } else if (pageType === 'releases' && path.includes('/artist/')) {
        // Support for Artist Releases specific views
        const releaseConfigs = [
            { title: 'Official artist releases', params: { va: '0' } },
            { title: 'Various artist releases', params: { va: '1' } }
        ];

        releaseConfigs.forEach(conf => {
            const eb = document.createElement('button');
            eb.textContent = conf.title;
            eb.style.cssText = 'font-size:0.5em; padding:2px 6px; cursor:pointer; transition:transform 0.1s, box-shadow 0.1s; height:24px; box-sizing:border-box;';
            eb.type = 'button';
            eb.onclick = (e) => startFetchingProcess(e, conf.params);
            controlsContainer.appendChild(eb);
            allActionButtons.push(eb);
        });
    } else {
        const mainBtn = document.createElement('button');
        mainBtn.textContent = `Show all ${pageType.replace('-', ' ')}`;
        mainBtn.style.cssText = 'font-size:0.5em; padding:2px 6px; cursor:pointer; transition:transform 0.1s, box-shadow 0.1s; height:24px; box-sizing:border-box;';
        mainBtn.type = 'button';
        mainBtn.onclick = (e) => startFetchingProcess(e);
        controlsContainer.appendChild(mainBtn);
        allActionButtons.push(mainBtn);

#+begin_example
Normalize URL info once
const url = new URL(location.href);
const path = url.pathname;
const params = url.searchParams;

const MBID_RE = '[a-f0-9-]{36}';

const hasParams = expected =>
    Object.entries(expected).every(([k, v]) => params.get(k) === v);

2️⃣ Declarative page matchers (JSON-ish)

Think of this as a routing table.

const PAGE_DEFINITIONS = [
    {
        pageType: 'work-recordings',
        match: ({ path, params }) =>
            path.startsWith('/work/') &&
            hasParams({ direction: '2', link_type_id: '278' }),
    },
    {
        pageType: 'work-recordings',
        match: ({ path }) =>
            new RegExp(`/work/${MBID_RE}$`).test(path),
    },

    {
        pageType: 'artist-releasegroups',
        match: ({ path }) =>
            new RegExp(`/artist/${MBID_RE}$`).test(path),
        variants: [
            { title: 'Official artist RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
            { title: 'Non-official artist RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
            { title: 'Official various artists RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '1' } },
            { title: 'Non-official various artists RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '1' } }
        ]
    },

    {
        pageType: 'releases',
        match: ({ path }) =>
            new RegExp(`/artist/${MBID_RE}/releases$`).test(path),
        variants: [
            { title: 'Official artist releases', params: { va: '0' } },
            { title: 'Various artist releases', params: { va: '1' } }
        ]
    },

    {
        pageType: 'releasegroup-releases',
        match: ({ path }) => path.includes('/release-group/')
    },
    {
        pageType: 'recording-releases',
        match: ({ path }) => path.includes('/recording')
    },
    {
        pageType: 'label-releases',
        match: ({ path }) => path.includes('/label')
    },
    {
        pageType: 'series-releases',
        match: ({ path }) => path.includes('/series')
    },
    {
        pageType: 'place-concerts',
        match: ({ path }) => /\/place\/.*\/events/.test(path)
    },
    {
        pageType: 'place-performances',
        match: ({ path }) => /\/place\/.*\/performances/.test(path)
    },
    {
        pageType: 'events',
        match: ({ path }) => path.includes('/events')
    }
];


✨ Why this works well

Priority = array order (top wins)

Complex cases stay readable

Variants live with their pageType
#+end_example

** table rendering

What about the refactoring of the rendering functions
 - function renderFinalTable
 - function renderGroupedTable

#+begin_example
Refactoring the render functions focuses on decoupling the data processing (how rows are built) from the DOM insertion (where they are placed).

The current logic mixes header cleanup, column splitting (Country/Date), and DOM building in one place. By moving the logic into a more structured pattern, you can support future URL types by simply defining their "schema."

1. Refactored renderFinalTable
This becomes a clean, single-purpose engine that builds the table based on the provided row data.

JavaScript

/**
 * Generic renderer for flat (non-grouped) tables.
 */
function renderFinalTable(rows) {
    const table = document.querySelector('table.tbl');
    if (!table) return;

    // Use a DocumentFragment for high performance with large datasets
    const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const tbody = table.tBodies[0];
    
    // Clear existing content safely
    tbody.innerHTML = '';

    rows.forEach(row => {
        // Ensure standard MusicBrainz row classes for alternating colors
        row.classList.remove('even', 'odd'); 
        fragment.appendChild(row);
    });

    tbody.appendChild(fragment);

    // Apply column filters and sort icons to the newly rendered table
    addColumnFilterRow(table);
    makeTableSortable(table);
    
    Logger.info('render', `Rendered ${rows.length} rows to the final table.`);
}
2. Refactored renderGroupedTable
For grouped pages (like Artist/Release Group views), the complexity often lies in managing multiple headers and toggles. This refactor uses a "Group Config" approach.

JavaScript

/**
 * Renders data divided into categories (e.g., Album, Single, EP).
 */
function renderGroupedTable(groupedData, isArtistRGPage, filterActive = false) {
    const container = document.getElementById('content');
    if (!container) return;

    // 1. Identify where to insert (MusicBrainz usually puts tables after specific H2s)
    let insertionPoint = document.querySelector('h2.discography') || 
                         document.querySelector('h2.appears-on-releases') || 
                         document.querySelector('h2');

    // 2. Clear old generated content (using a marker class for safety)
    document.querySelectorAll('.mb-generated-content').forEach(el => el.remove());

    const mainFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();

    groupedData.forEach((group, index) => {
        if (group.rows.length === 0 && filterActive) return;

        // Create Section Header (H3)
        const h3 = createGroupHeader(group.category, group.rows.length);
        mainFragment.appendChild(h3);

        // Create the Table
        const table = createBaseTable();
        const tbody = table.tBodies[0];
        
        group.rows.forEach(row => tbody.appendChild(row.cloneNode(true)));
        
        // Add table to fragment
        const tableWrapper = document.createElement('div');
        tableWrapper.className = 'mb-generated-content';
        tableWrapper.appendChild(table);
        mainFragment.appendChild(tableWrapper);

        // Post-processing for this specific table
        cleanupHeaders(table.tHead);
        addColumnFilterRow(table);
        makeTableSortable(table);
    });

    insertionPoint.after(mainFragment);
    Logger.info('render', `Grouped render complete: ${groupedData.length} categories.`);
}

/**
 * Helper to create a standardized MusicBrainz table skeleton
 */
function createBaseTable() {
    const table = document.createElement('table');
    table.className = 'tbl mb-generated-content';
    
    // Clone the original header if it exists, or create a default
    const originalHeader = document.querySelector('table.tbl thead');
    if (originalHeader) {
        table.appendChild(originalHeader.cloneNode(true));
    }
    
    table.appendChild(document.createElement('tbody'));
    return table;
}
3. Proposed Schema-Based Refactoring
To handle "much more URLs" in the future, you should define a Render Schema. This tells the render functions which columns to add or split based on the pageType.

JavaScript

const PAGE_SCHEMAS = {
    'releases': {
        hasGroups: true,
        extraColumns: ['Country', 'Date', 'MB-Name'],
        groupSelector: 'h2.releases',
    },
    'work-recordings': {
        hasGroups: false,
        extraColumns: ['MB-Name', 'Comment'],
        groupSelector: 'h2.recordings',
    },
    'events': {
        hasGroups: false,
        extraColumns: ['Place', 'Area', 'Country'],
    }
};

// In your render functions, you can now do:
const schema = PAGE_SCHEMAS[pageType];
if (schema?.extraColumns) {
    schema.extraColumns.forEach(col => ensureColumnExists(table, col));
}
Key Improvements:
Performance: Switched to DocumentFragment. In your script, rendering 1000+ rows directly to the DOM can cause "jank." Fragments perform the work in memory first.

Statelessness: By passing filterActive as a boolean, the function doesn't need to guess if it's currently filtering.

Encapsulation: Using createBaseTable() ensures that even if MusicBrainz changes their table structure, you only have one place to update the skeleton.

No Logic Duplication: The "cleanup" and "sortable" triggers are moved into the render engine, so you don't forget to call them when adding new page types.
#+end_example

                                    const tdAreaOnly = document.createElement('td');
                                    const tdCountryOnly = document.createElement('td');
                                    if (typesWithSplitArea.includes(pageType) && areaIdx !== -1) {
                                        const locCell = newRow.cells[locationIdx];
                                        if (locCell) {
                                            locCell.querySelectorAll('a').forEach(a => {
                                                const href = a.getAttribute('href');
                                                const clonedA = a.cloneNode(true);
                                                if (href && href.includes('/area/')) {
                                                    const flagSpan = a.closest('.flag');
                                                    if (flagSpan) {
                                                        const flagImg = flagSpan.querySelector('img')?.outerHTML || '';
                                                        const abbr = flagSpan.querySelector('abbr');
                                                        const countryCode = abbr ? abbr.textContent.trim() : '';
                                                        const countryFullName = abbr?.getAttribute('title') || '';
                                                        const countryHref = a.getAttribute('href') || '#';
                                                        const span = document.createElement('span');
                                                        span.className = flagSpan.className;
                                                        if (countryFullName && countryCode) {
                                                            span.innerHTML = `${flagImg} <a href="${countryHref}">${countryFullName} (${countryCode})</a>`;
                                                        } else {
                                                            span.innerHTML = flagSpan.innerHTML;
                                                        }
                                                        tdCountryOnly.appendChild(span);
                                                    } else {
                                                        if (tdAreaOnly.hasChildNodes()) tdAreaOnly.appendChild(document.createTextNode(', '));
                                                        tdAreaOnly.appendChild(clonedA);
                                                    }
                                                }
                                            });
                                        }
                                    }

#+end_example

to use a more robust "Node-based Splitting" approach. Instead of just looking for <a> tags, we should iterate through
all childNodes. This ensures that the commas, the text inside aliases (<i>), and the specific flag spans are correctly
sorted into their respective new columns while maintaining the original MusicBrainz styling.

*** initial

In the uploaded script,
because MusicBrainz pages could potentially have more entry points to parse related data in the final rendered data table or data tables
e.g. httṕs://musicbrainz.org/area/489ce91b-6658-3307-9877-795b68554c98/releases

In that specific URL of pageType: 'area-releases', we have the following two h2 headers:

- Releases: paginated access to data (tableMode: 'single')
- Relationships: non_paginated access to data (tableMode: 'multi')

Please extend the "pageDefinitions" variable to support a variable which specifies which h2 header should be targeted
for data acumulation and display when pressing the corresponding action button (there should one action button per h2
header generated for these types of pages). The other (or maybe others) should be deleted from the final renderd page.
The "tableMode" and "non_paginated" mode should be specifyable for each button clicked

Please refactor accordingly.

*** Please add comprehensive debug statement to the following code

Please add comprehensive debug statement to the following code

#+begin_example
    function parseDocumentForTables(container, targetHeader) {
        const tables = Array.from(container.querySelectorAll('table.tbl'));
        if (!targetHeader) return tables;

        return tables.filter(table => {
            let prev = table.previousElementSibling;
            // Traverse upwards to find the nearest H2 or H3
            while (prev) {
                if (['H2', 'H3'].includes(prev.tagName)) {
                    return prev.textContent.trim().startsWith(targetHeader);
                }
                prev = prev.previousElementSibling;
            }
            return false;
        });
    }
#+end_example

*** answer

#+begin_example
function parseDocumentForTables(container, targetHeader) {
        const tables = Array.from(container.querySelectorAll('table.tbl'));
        Lib.debug('parse', `parseDocumentForTables: Found ${tables.length} total tables. Target header filter: "${targetHeader || 'none'}"`);

        if (!targetHeader) {
            Lib.debug('parse', 'No targetHeader provided; returning all tables found in container.');
            return tables;
        }

        const filteredTables = tables.filter((table, index) => {
            let prev = table.previousElementSibling;
            Lib.debug('parse', `Inspecting table index ${index} (ID: ${table.id || 'N/A'}, Classes: ${table.className})`);

            // Traverse upwards to find the nearest H2 or H3
            while (prev) {
                if (['H2', 'H3'].includes(prev.tagName)) {
                    const headerText = prev.textContent.trim();
                    const isMatch = headerText.startsWith(targetHeader);

                    Lib.debug('parse', `Found preceding header <${prev.tagName}>: "${headerText}". Start-match with "${targetHeader}": ${isMatch}`);
                    return isMatch;
                }
                prev = prev.previousElementSibling;
            }

            Lib.debug('parse', `Table index ${index} excluded: No preceding H2 or H3 found in the DOM path.`);
            return false;
        });

        Lib.debug('parse', `Filtering complete. Returning ${filteredTables.length} tables matching the criteria.`);
        return filteredTables;
    }
#+end_example

*** UI Cleanup:

In the uploaded userscript

During the UI Cleanup, when a targetHeader is active, the script should automatically remove ONLY those h2 headers and
their associated tables from the current page to provide a clean, consolidated view, which are part of the OTHER button
configurations.

Actually because each h2 header and the table below it are not inside a "div" container, adjust the logic so that
everything starting with the h2 header AND evenual optional elements after it PLUS the final table is removed from the current page

Please fix the following code

#+begin_example
    async function startFetchingProcess(e, buttonConfig) {
        const activeBtn = e.target;
        const overrideParams = buttonConfig.params || null;
        const targetHeader = buttonConfig.targetHeader || null;
        e.preventDefault();
        e.stopPropagation();
    // async function startFetchingProcess(e, overrideParams = null) {
    //     // Capture currentTarget immediately before any awaits
    //     const activeBtn = e.currentTarget;

        // UI Cleanup: If targeting a specific header, remove all other headers and tables from the current page
        if (targetHeader) {
            const container = document.getElementById('content') || document.body;
            container.querySelectorAll('h2, h3').forEach(h => {
                if (!h.textContent.trim().startsWith(targetHeader)) {
                    // Remove this header and any immediate table following it
                    let next = h.nextElementSibling;
                    while (next && !['H2', 'H3'].includes(next.tagName)) {
                        let toRemove = next;
                        next = next.nextElementSibling;
                        if (toRemove.tagName === 'TABLE' && toRemove.classList.contains('tbl')) {
                            toRemove.remove();
                        } else if (toRemove.tagName === 'BR') {
                            toRemove.remove();
                        }
                    }
                    h.remove();
                }
            });
            Lib.debug('cleanup', `Cleaned UI to target header: "${targetHeader}"`);
        }
#+end_example

[VZ-ShowAllEntities: area-releases] 🧹 Final cleanup complete: No consecutive <br> tags found. 
[VZ-ShowAllEntities: area-releases] 🎨 Initializing collapsible H2 headers... 
[VZ-ShowAllEntities: area-releases] 🚀 Initializing aggressive full-width sidebar toggle. 
[VZ-ShowAllEntities: area-releases] 🎨 DOM rendering finished in 4.20ms 
[VZ-ShowAllEntities: area-releases] ✅ Process complete. Final Row Count: 100. Total Time: 8.15s 
#+end_example

the script actually shows the right h2 header "Relationships" and the corresponding no_paginated table data, but it
still somehow assumes to fetch the data from the paginated "Releases table" (although just a single page because it
correctly identifies the maxPage size to be 1 according to no_paginated.

I think the problem is in this code

#+begin_example
                // Initialize fetchUrl from the full current URL to preserve Search parameters (query, type, etc.)
                const fetchUrl = new URL(window.location.href);
                fetchUrl.searchParams.set('page', p.toString());

                if (overrideParams) {
                    Object.keys(overrideParams).forEach(k => fetchUrl.searchParams.set(k, overrideParams[k]));
                }
#+end_example

This code should not be executed in the case when a non_paginated table is being deteced and already there (the cleanup has been run), so basically the current visible tabel should be processed and rendered for the final page

Please fix, it should actually just parse the non_paginated page below the h2 header "Relationships" and generate the final page

It should actually behave like all the other pageTypes.

I realize that this kind of pages do NOT have an h2 header like all the other pages, to place the number of rows in the
datatable, instead there is p element of class "pageselector-results" with the following structure:

#+begin_example
<p class="pageselector-results">Found <number of results> results for "<search string>"</p>
#+end_example

e.g.

#+begin_example
<p class="pageselector-results">Found 314 results for "Asbury Park"</p>
#+end_example

so the number of found searches together with the global filter search field should be placed in brackets behind that
string.

Probably the following variable also has to be changed accordingly

#+begin_example
    // TODO: probably need to be fixed for "Search" pages - should also be refactored in the "pageDefinitions" variable
    let headerContainer = document.querySelector('.artistheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.rgheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.labelheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.seriesheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.placeheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.areaheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('.recordingheader h1') ||
                          document.querySelector('h1 a bdi')?.parentNode ||
                          document.querySelector('h1');
#+end_example

#+begin_example
                // Header name for which a generic split into MB-Name and Comment columns will be done
                // TODO: Add 'Label' for "Area-Labels" view, but take care that in "Label" is also used in "Releasegroup-Releases" views
                const mainHeaders = ['Recording', 'Event', 'Release', 'Work', 'Title', 'Name', 'Alias', 'Place'];

                    referenceTable.querySelectorAll('thead th').forEach((th, idx) => {
                        const txt = th.textContent.trim();

                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_perf && txt.startsWith('Performance Attributes')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rating && txt.startsWith('Rating')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);

                            locationIdx = idx;
                        }

                    mainColIdx = 0;
                    Lib.debug('init', `mainColIdx was -1, forced to 0 for pageType: ${pageType}`);
                }

*** gemini

#+begin_example
Here is the refactored solution. This approach introduces a new property extractMainColumn within the features object of your pageDefinitions.

This allows you to specify exactly which column (by header name or index) should be used for the split, or omit the property to disable the feature entirely (as requested for Search or Artist-ReleaseGroups).

1. Update pageDefinitions
You need to update your pageDefinitions array to include the extractMainColumn property in the features object for the pages where you want this logic to apply.

Here is the strategy for the values:

'String': Look for a header with this exact text.

['Array', 'Of', 'Strings']: Look for any of these headers (useful if MB changes terminology).

0 (Number): Force a specific column index (replaces the releasegroup-releases hack).

undefined / null: Feature disabled (replaces the pageType !== '...' checks).

Example updates for your pageDefinitions:

JavaScript
    const pageDefinitions = [
        {
            type: 'search',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/search'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Search results' } ],
            tableMode: 'single',
            // No features defined -> extractMainColumn is undefined -> Feature DISABLED
        },
        // ...
        {
            type: 'instrument-releases',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/instrument\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Releases for Instruments' } ],
            features: {
                splitCD: true,
                extractMainColumn: ['Release', 'Title'] // Enable for Release/Title
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
        // ...
        {
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // ...
            features: {
                 // extractMainColumn is undefined -> Feature DISABLED (Matches your old exclusion logic)
            },
            tableMode: 'multi'
        },
        {
            type: 'releasegroup-releases',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/release-group/'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Releases for ReleaseGroup' } ],
            features: {
                splitCD: true,
                extractMainColumn: 0 // FORCE Index 0 (Matches your old hack)
            },
            tableMode: 'multi',
            non_paginated: false
        },
        {
            type: 'events',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/events'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Events for Artist' } ],
            features: {
                splitLocation: true,
                extractMainColumn: 'Event' // Specific header
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        }
        // ... You must apply extractMainColumn to all other page types where you want the split!
        // Common mappings:
        // Works -> 'Work'
        // Recordings -> 'Recording'
        // Places -> 'Place'
        // Labels -> 'Label'
        // Aliases -> 'Alias'
    ];
2. Refactor cleanupHeaders
Replace the hardcoded pageType exclusion with a check for the new configuration property.

JavaScript
        // Check if the generic split feature is enabled for this page definition
        const mainColConfig = activeDefinition.features?.extractMainColumn;
        const isMainColEnabled = mainColConfig !== undefined && mainColConfig !== null;

        // On pages where the configuration is enabled, create the "MB-Name" and "Comment" columns
        if (isMainColEnabled) {
            const headersText = Array.from(theadRow.cells).map(th => th.textContent.replace(/[⇅▲▼]/g, '').trim());
            if (!headersText.includes('MB-Name')) {
                const thN = document.createElement('th');
                thN.textContent = 'MB-Name';
                thN.style.backgroundColor = headerBgColor;
                theadRow.appendChild(thN);
            }
            if (!headersText.includes('Comment')) {
                const thC = document.createElement('th');
                thC.textContent = 'Comment';
                thC.style.backgroundColor = headerBgColor;
                theadRow.appendChild(thC);
            }
        }
3. Refactor Header Detection in startFetchingProcess
This replaces the block containing the mainHeaders array and the releasegroup-releases hack.

JavaScript
                let countryDateIdx = -1;
                let locationIdx = -1;
                let areaIdx = -1;
                let mainColIdx = -1;
                let indicesToExclude = [];

                // Retrieve configuration for the main column extraction
                const mainColConfig = activeDefinition.features?.extractMainColumn;

                // If configuration is a specific number, force that index immediately
                if (typeof mainColConfig === 'number') {
                    mainColIdx = mainColConfig;
                    Lib.debug('init', `mainColIdx forced to ${mainColIdx} by configuration.`);
                }
                // Prepare candidates list if config is string or array
                const mainColCandidates = Array.isArray(mainColConfig) ? mainColConfig : (mainColConfig ? [mainColConfig] : []);

                const referenceTable = doc.querySelector('table.tbl');
                if (referenceTable) {
                    referenceTable.querySelectorAll('thead th').forEach((th, idx) => {
                        const txt = th.textContent.trim();
                        if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rel && txt.startsWith('Relationships')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_perf && txt.startsWith('Performance Attributes')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rating && txt.startsWith('Rating')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_tagger && txt.startsWith('Tagger')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (typesWithSplitCD.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Country/Date') {
                            countryDateIdx = idx;
                        } else if (typesWithSplitLocation.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Location') {
                            locationIdx = idx;
                        } else if (typesWithSplitArea.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Area') {
                            areaIdx = idx;
                        }

                        // Dynamic detection based on config candidates
                        // We only search if mainColIdx wasn't already forced by a number config
                        if (mainColIdx === -1 && mainColCandidates.includes(txt)) {
                            mainColIdx = idx;
                        }
                    });
                }

                Lib.debug('init', `Determined mainColIdx: ${mainColIdx} for pageType: ${pageType}`);
4. Refactor Row Extraction
Replace the hardcoded pageType check with a check for mainColIdx. Since mainColIdx will remain -1 if the feature is disabled (undefined in config), this handles the logic cleanly.

JavaScript
                                    // Extraction logic for MB-Name and Comment
                                    const tdName = document.createElement('td');
                                    const tdComment = document.createElement('td');

                                    // If a main column was identified (via config or detection), perform the extraction
                                    if (mainColIdx !== -1) {
                                        const targetCell = newRow.cells[mainColIdx];
                                        if (targetCell) {
                                            const nameLink = targetCell.querySelector('a bdi')?.closest('a');
                                            if (nameLink) tdName.appendChild(nameLink.cloneNode(true));
                                            const commentBdi = targetCell.querySelector('.comment bdi');
                                            if (commentBdi) tdComment.textContent = commentBdi.textContent.trim();
                                        }
                                    }
#+end_example

*** chatgpt

#+begin_example
Yeah — this is a very sensible refactor 👍
Right now mainHeaders + mainColIdx are doing two jobs at once:

Deciding whether a page type even supports the generic split

Guessing which column to use by matching header text

That’s why the logic is spreading and why artist-releasegroups needs special-casing in three places.

The clean way out is exactly what you suggest:
👉 make the “MB-Name / Comment source column” a pageType capability, not a global heuristic.

Below is a concrete, low-risk refactor that keeps behavior identical but centralizes all decisions in pageDefinitions.

1️⃣ Extend pageDefinitions

Add an optional property, for example:

pageDefinitions = {
    'artist-releasegroups': {
        /* existing config */
        mainSplitHeader: null, // explicitly disables MB-Name/Comment
    },

    'releasegroup-releases': {
        /* existing config */
        mainSplitHeader: 'Release',
    },

    'recording-relationships': {
        /* existing config */
        mainSplitHeader: 'Recording',
    },

    'work-relationships': {
        /* existing config */
        mainSplitHeader: 'Work',
    },

    'place-performances': {
        /* existing config */
        mainSplitHeader: 'Place',
    },

    'search': {
        /* existing config */
        mainSplitHeader: null,
    },
};


Rules:

mainSplitHeader: string → enable generic split and use that column

mainSplitHeader: null / undefined → feature disabled for that pageType

No more guessing, no more mainHeaders list

2️⃣ Replace mainHeaders + mainColIdx detection
Before
const mainHeaders = ['Recording', 'Event', 'Release', 'Work', 'Title', 'Name', 'Alias', 'Place'];
...
if (mainHeaders.includes(txt)) mainColIdx = idx;

After (pageType-driven)
const pageDef = pageDefinitions[pageType] || {};
const mainSplitHeader = pageDef.mainSplitHeader ?? null;

const referenceTable = doc.querySelector('table.tbl');
if (referenceTable && mainSplitHeader) {
    referenceTable.querySelectorAll('thead th').forEach((th, idx) => {
        const txt = th.textContent.trim();

        if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rel && txt.startsWith('Relationships')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_perf && txt.startsWith('Performance Attributes')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rating && txt.startsWith('Rating')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_tagger && txt.startsWith('Tagger')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
        else if (typesWithSplitCD.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Country/Date') {
            countryDateIdx = idx;
        } else if (typesWithSplitLocation.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Location') {
            locationIdx = idx;
        }

        if (txt === mainSplitHeader) {
            mainColIdx = idx;
        }
    });
}

Keep your existing fallback (unchanged behavior)
Lib.debug('init', `Checking default mainColIdx for pageType: ${pageType}, current mainColIdx: ${mainColIdx}`);

if (mainColIdx === -1 && pageType === 'releasegroup-releases') {
    mainColIdx = 0;
    Lib.debug('init', `mainColIdx was -1, forced to 0 for pageType: ${pageType}`);
}

3️⃣ Rewrite header creation (no more hardcoded pageType checks)
Before
if (pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups') {
    ...
}

After
const pageDef = pageDefinitions[pageType] || {};
const hasMainSplit = typeof pageDef.mainSplitHeader === 'string';

if (hasMainSplit) {
    const headersText = Array.from(theadRow.cells)
        .map(th => th.textContent.replace(/[⇅▲▼]/g, '').trim());

    if (!headersText.includes('MB-Name')) {
        const thN = document.createElement('th');
        thN.textContent = 'MB-Name';
        thN.style.backgroundColor = headerBgColor;
        theadRow.appendChild(thN);
    }

    if (!headersText.includes('Comment')) {
        const thC = document.createElement('th');
        thC.textContent = 'Comment';
        thC.style.backgroundColor = headerBgColor;
        theadRow.appendChild(thC);
    }
}


This automatically:

Enables the feature where configured

Disables it for artist-releasegroups, search, etc.

Removes all pageType name coupling

4️⃣ Rewrite extraction logic (same simplification)
Before
if (mainColIdx !== -1 && pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups') {
    ...
}

After
const pageDef = pageDefinitions[pageType] || {};
const hasMainSplit = typeof pageDef.mainSplitHeader === 'string';

const tdName = document.createElement('td');
const tdComment = document.createElement('td');

if (hasMainSplit && mainColIdx !== -1) {
    const targetCell = newRow.cells[mainColIdx];
    if (targetCell) {
        const nameLink = targetCell.querySelector('a bdi')?.closest('a');
        if (nameLink) tdName.appendChild(nameLink.cloneNode(true));

        const commentBdi = targetCell.querySelector('.comment bdi');
        if (commentBdi) tdComment.textContent = commentBdi.textContent.trim();
    }
}

5️⃣ Why this refactor is solid

You gain:

🧠 Single source of truth (pageDefinitions)

🚫 No magic header lists

🧩 No scattered pageType exceptions

🛠 Easy future extension (e.g. "Label" for Area-Labels)

🧪 Much easier to reason about bugs

You do NOT change:

Existing behavior

Fallback logic

Debug output

Column extraction semantics

If you want, next step could be:

supporting multiple possible headers (mainSplitHeader: ['Name','Title'])

or letting pageDefinitions specify a custom extractor for weird pages

Just say the word.
#+end_example

NOT produce any output in the new "MB-Name" and "Comment" columns

What should happen:

Do not only account for the "MB-Name" column to be found inside '<bdi>' elements and the "Comment" column inside '<span class="comment"> --> <bdi>'
as can be seen in the following example:

#+begin_example
<td>
  <a class="wrap-anywhere" href="/release/9e630e98-055b-49aa-857b-981bb4e3c70f">
    <bdi>A Town Hall Concert
    </bdi>
  </a>
  <span class="comment">(
    <bdi>sterero
    </bdi>)
  </span>
</td>
#+end_example

The extraction would be "MB-Name: A Town Hall Concert", "Comment: stereo" in this case,
but also support the following fragment

#+begin_example
<td>German
  <!-- -->
  <!-- -->(
  <span class="comment">primary
  </span>)
</td>
#+end_example

with the "MB-Name" column to be found right after the '<td>' element and the "Comment" column inside '<span class="comment">' without the inner '<bdi>'

The extraction would be "MB-Name: German", "Comment: primary" in this case.

Do not only account for the "MB-Name" column to be found inside '<bdi>' elements and the "Comment" column inside '<span class="comment"> --> <bdi>'
as can be seen in the following example:

#+begin_example
<td>
  <a class="wrap-anywhere" href="/release/9e630e98-055b-49aa-857b-981bb4e3c70f">
    <bdi>A Town Hall Concert

**** logging

Please add comprehensive debug statement to the following code

#+begin_example
                                    const newRow = document.importNode(node, true);

                                    // Extraction logic for MB-Name and Comment
                                    const tdName = document.createElement('td');
                                    const tdComment = document.createElement('td');

                                    // If a main column was identified (via config or detection), perform the extraction
                                    if (mainColIdx !== -1) {
                                        const targetCell = newRow.cells[mainColIdx];
                                        if (targetCell) {
                                            // 1. Extract Name
                                            // Priority: Specific Entity Link (a > bdi) -> First Link -> First Meaningful Text
                                            const nameLink = targetCell.querySelector('a bdi')?.closest('a');
                                            if (nameLink) {
                                                tdName.appendChild(nameLink.cloneNode(true));
                                            } else {
                                                // Fallback: Scan child nodes for the first non-comment content
                                                let foundName = false;
                                                for (const node of targetCell.childNodes) {
                                                    // Skip comments and scripts
                                                    if (node.nodeType === Node.ELEMENT_NODE) {
                                                        if (node.classList.contains('comment') || node.tagName === 'SCRIPT' || node.tagName === 'STYLE') continue;
                                                        if (node.tagName === 'A') {
                                                            tdName.appendChild(node.cloneNode(true));
                                                            foundName = true;
                                                            break;
                                                        }
                                                    } else if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {
                                                        const txt = node.textContent.trim();
                                                        // Check for meaningful text (ignoring common separators like parens often wrapping comments)
                                                        if (txt && txt !== '(' && txt !== ')' && txt !== ',') {
                                                            tdName.textContent = txt;
                                                            foundName = true;
                                                            break;
                                                        }
                                                    }
                                                }
                                                // Fallback if iteration found nothing (e.g. complex nesting): get text excluding comments
                                                if (!foundName) {
                                                    const clone = targetCell.cloneNode(true);
                                                    clone.querySelectorAll('.comment').forEach(el => el.remove());
                                                    tdName.textContent = clone.textContent.trim();
                                                }
                                            }

                                            // 2. Extract Comment
                                            // Priority: .comment > bdi -> .comment text
                                            const commentSpan = targetCell.querySelector('.comment');
                                            if (commentSpan) {
                                                // If bdi exists (standard entity comment), use it; otherwise use the span text (simple comment)
                                                const val = commentSpan.querySelector('bdi') || commentSpan;
                                                tdComment.textContent = val.textContent.trim();
                                            }
                                        }
                                    }
#+end_example

**** answer

#+begin_example
const newRow = document.importNode(node, true);
Lib.debug('parse', 'Imported row via document.importNode()');

// Extraction logic for MB-Name and Comment
const tdName = document.createElement('td');
const tdComment = document.createElement('td');

Lib.debug('parse', `mainColIdx = ${mainColIdx}`);

// If a main column was identified (via config or detection), perform the extraction
if (mainColIdx !== -1) {

    const targetCell = newRow.cells[mainColIdx];

    Lib.debug(
        'parse',
        `Resolved targetCell for mainColIdx=${mainColIdx}; ` +
        `total cells=${newRow.cells.length}; ` +
        `targetCell ${targetCell ? 'FOUND' : 'MISSING'}`
    );

    if (targetCell) {

        Lib.debug('parse', `targetCell.innerHTML = ${targetCell.innerHTML}`);

        // ───────────────────────────────────────────
        // 1. Extract Name
        // ───────────────────────────────────────────

        const nameLink = targetCell.querySelector('a bdi')?.closest('a');

        Lib.debug(
            'parse',
            `Name lookup via "a > bdi": ${nameLink ? 'FOUND' : 'NOT FOUND'}`
        );

        if (nameLink) {

            tdName.appendChild(nameLink.cloneNode(true));

            Lib.debug(
                'parse',
                `Name extracted from linked <a>: "${nameLink.textContent.trim()}"`
            );

        } else {

            Lib.debug(
                'parse',
                `Falling back to scanning targetCell.childNodes (${targetCell.childNodes.length})`
            );

            let foundName = false;

            for (const child of targetCell.childNodes) {

                Lib.debug(
                    'parse',
                    `Inspecting child node: ` +
                    `type=${child.nodeType}, ` +
                    `tag=${child.tagName || 'TEXT'}, ` +
                    `class=${child.classList?.value || '-'}, ` +
                    `text="${child.textContent?.trim()}"`
                );

                // Skip comments and scripts
                if (child.nodeType === Node.ELEMENT_NODE) {

                    if (
                        child.classList.contains('comment') ||
                        child.tagName === 'SCRIPT' ||
                        child.tagName === 'STYLE'
                    ) {
                        Lib.debug(
                            'parse',
                            `Skipping excluded element: <${child.tagName}>.${child.classList?.value || ''}`
                        );
                        continue;
                    }

                    if (child.tagName === 'A') {

                        tdName.appendChild(child.cloneNode(true));
                        foundName = true;

                        Lib.debug(
                            'parse',
                            `Name extracted from <A>: "${child.textContent.trim()}"`
                        );

                        break;
                    }

                } else if (child.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {

                    const txt = child.textContent.trim();

                    Lib.debug('parse', `Text node candidate: "${txt}"`);

                    // Check for meaningful text
                    if (txt && txt !== '(' && txt !== ')' && txt !== ',') {

                        tdName.textContent = txt;
                        foundName = true;

                        Lib.debug(
                            'parse',
                            `Name extracted from TEXT node: "${txt}"`
                        );

                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Fallback if iteration found nothing (e.g. complex nesting)
            if (!foundName) {

                Lib.debug(
                    'parse',
                    'No name found during scan; using deep clone fallback (removing .comment nodes)'
                );

                const clone = targetCell.cloneNode(true);

                const removedComments = [];
                clone.querySelectorAll('.comment').forEach(el => {
                    removedComments.push(el.textContent.trim());
                    el.remove();
                });

                tdName.textContent = clone.textContent.trim();

                Lib.debug(
                    'parse',
                    `Removed comment text: [${removedComments.join(' | ')}]`
                );

                Lib.debug(
                    'parse',
                    `Name extracted via fallback textContent: "${tdName.textContent}"`
                );
            }
        }

        // ───────────────────────────────────────────
        // 2. Extract Comment
        // ───────────────────────────────────────────

        const commentSpan = targetCell.querySelector('.comment');

        Lib.debug(
            'parse',
            `.comment lookup: ${commentSpan ? 'FOUND' : 'NOT FOUND'}`
        );

        if (commentSpan) {

            const val = commentSpan.querySelector('bdi') || commentSpan;

            Lib.debug(
                'parse',
                `Comment extracted from ${val.tagName}: "${val.textContent.trim()}"`
            );

            tdComment.textContent = val.textContent.trim();
        }

    } else {
        Lib.debug('parse', 'targetCell is undefined despite valid mainColIdx');
    }

} else {
    Lib.debug('parse', 'mainColIdx === -1, skipping name/comment extraction');
}

Lib.debug(
    'parse',
    `Final extraction result → Name: "${tdName.textContent}", Comment: "${tdComment.textContent}"`
);
#+end_example

To implement the transformation of the search results paragraph into a collapsible <h2> element, we need to modify the
updateH2Count function. This ensures that when the script identifies the target element via rowTargetSelector (like
p.pageselector-results), it swaps it for an <h2> if the transformToH2 feature flag is enabled. This new <h2> will then
be processed by the existing makeH2sCollapsible logic.

* DONE refactor column splitting in different functions and take them out of the main flow
** initial

In the uploaded script,
is it possible to refactor the current column splitting in different functions and take them out of the main flow

** answer

#+begin_example
It is definitely possible to refactor the column splitting logic into standalone functions and move them out of the main execution flow.

In the current script, logic for handling splitCD, splitLocation, and splitArea often resides within the row processing or parsing loops. By extracting these into a dedicated utility object or private functions, you can make the main flow much more readable and maintainable.

Recommended Refactoring Strategy
You can consolidate the splitting logic into a helper object called ColumnSplitter. This allows the main fetching and rendering logic to simply "ask" for a split value without caring about the implementation details.

1. Define the Splitting Utility
Add these helper functions to the script (e.g., before the startFetchingProcess function):

JavaScript
const ColumnSplitter = {
    /**
     * Extracts the split category based on the feature type.
     */
    getCategory: function(row, features) {
        if (features.splitCD) return this.extractCD(row);
        if (features.splitLocation) return this.extractLocation(row);
        if (features.splitArea) return this.extractArea(row);
        return "General";
    },

    extractCD: function(row) {
        // Logic moved from main loop: Extracts "CD 1", "Digital Media", etc.
        const discCell = row.querySelector('.disc-number') || row.cells[0]; 
        return discCell ? discCell.textContent.trim() : "Other";
    },

    extractLocation: function(row) {
        // Logic moved from main loop: Extracts Venue or City
        const locationCell = row.querySelector('.location-name');
        return locationCell ? locationCell.textContent.trim() : "Unknown Location";
    },

    extractArea: function(row) {
        // Logic moved from main loop: Extracts Country/Area information
        const areaCell = row.querySelector('.area-name');
        return areaCell ? areaCell.textContent.trim() : "Unknown Area";
    }
};
2. Update the Main Flow
In the section where rows are processed (likely inside your fetching loop), you can now replace complex if/else chains with a single call to the refactored logic:

JavaScript
// Inside the row processing loop:
allRows.forEach(row => {
    // OLD: 
    // let cat = "";
    // if (activeDefinition.features.splitCD) { ... }
    // else if (activeDefinition.features.splitLocation) { ... }
    
    // NEW (Refactored):
    const category = ColumnSplitter.getCategory(row, activeDefinition.features);
    
    // Continue with grouping logic...
});
Benefits of this Refactor
Readability: The main startFetchingProcess and rendering functions become significantly shorter.

Scalability: If you need to add a new split type (e.g., splitFormat or splitYear), you only need to add one function to the ColumnSplitter object and a flag in the pageDefinitions.

Instruction Compliance: This approach allows you to keep your existing debug logging statements (Lib.debug) inside the specific extraction functions without cluttering the main logic.

Implementation Detail for Search Results
Since you recently added logic to transform search results (converting p to h2), you can also wrap that transformation into a dedicated function like handleSearchHeaderTransformation(target) to keep the initialization flow clean.

#+end_example

In the following code block

#+begin_example
                if (referenceTable) {
                    referenceTable.querySelectorAll('thead th').forEach((th, idx) => {
                        const txt = th.textContent.trim();
                        headerNames[idx] = txt; // Store the name

                        if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rel && txt.startsWith('Relationships')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_perf && txt.startsWith('Performance Attributes')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_rating && txt.startsWith('Rating')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_tagger && txt.startsWith('Tagger')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_release_events && txt.startsWith('Release events')) indicesToExclude.push(idx);
                        else if (typesWithSplitCD.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Country/Date') {
                            countryDateIdx = idx;
                        } else if (typesWithSplitLocation.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Location') {
                            locationIdx = idx;
                        } else if (typesWithSplitArea.includes(pageType) && txt === 'Area') {
                            areaIdx = idx;
                        }

                        // Dynamic detection based on config candidates
                        // We only search if mainColIdx wasn't already forced by a number config
                        if (mainColIdx === -1 && mainColCandidates.includes(txt)) {
                            mainColIdx = idx;
                        }
                    });
                }
#+end_example

could the "else if" structure somehow be refactored, it looks error prone

* DONE mismatch when using Regexps

In the uploaded userscript

When using a Regexp like "^Thunder Road" (so a column value must start with "Thunder Road") this works perfectly when
using the regepx as a filter string for pre-loading from disk, but fails to find any row when using it in the global
filter field.

For example on https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f/recordings when preloading it finds
rows which have "Thunder Road" at the beginning of the "Name" or "Release Group", "MB-Name" columns but fails on the
latter, 0 rows are shown

This the code for the global filter

#+begin_example
    function runFilter() {
        const isCaseSensitive = caseCheckbox.checked;
        const isRegExp = regexpCheckbox.checked;
        const globalQueryRaw = filterInput.value;
        const globalQuery = (isCaseSensitive || isRegExp) ? globalQueryRaw : globalQueryRaw.toLowerCase();

        let globalRegex = null;
        if (globalQueryRaw && isRegExp) {
            try {
                globalRegex = new RegExp(globalQueryRaw, isCaseSensitive ? '' : 'i');
                filterInput.style.border = '2px solid #ccc'; // Reset to standard if valid
            } catch (e) {
                filterInput.style.border = '2px solid red'; // Visual cue for invalid Regex
            }
        } else {
            filterInput.style.border = '2px solid #ccc';
        }
#+end_example

and this for prefilter

#+begin_example
    async function loadTableDataFromDisk(file, filterQueryRaw = '', isCaseSensitive = false, isRegExp = false) {
        if (!file) {
            Lib.warn('cache', 'No file selected.');
            return;
        }

        const filterQuery = (isCaseSensitive || isRegExp) ? filterQueryRaw : filterQueryRaw.toLowerCase();

        // Clear previous status message
        preFilterMsg.textContent = '';

        let globalRegex = null;
        if (filterQueryRaw && isRegExp) {
            try {
                globalRegex = new RegExp(filterQueryRaw, isCaseSensitive ? '' : 'i');
            } catch (e) {
                alert('Invalid Regular Expression in load filter field. Load aborted.');
                // Reset file input so change event fires again if they pick same file
                fileInput.value = '';
                return;
            }
        }
#+end_example

** DONE Bug
*** bug

For example on this URL https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/c497fc44-ddaf-3cce-a9b4-bfec958a0f3c when "Visible Columns"
has been used to filter out columns from display and after the "Auto-Resize" button is pressed, the rendering of the
table is somehow messed up. Specifically the header columns for "Country/Date" and Label" shrink to a minimum, only the
"⇅" symbol and one character + half of the next character are visible.

The following additional glitches show up:

 - The data cells of the "Country/Date" column show the country flag, the abbreviated country name like US and the date part SPREAD over THREE columns "Country/Date" itself but alos the next two columns "Label" and "Catalog#"
 - The "Label" cells show the complete label text but wrapped after a couple of chars again and again, so they actually use multiple rows for display
 - The cells for the "Catalog#" column actually shows up below the "Label" column, together/intervined with the contents of the "Label" column

It looks like if columns with special icons or such are involved, these glitches can happen.

By the way, pressing "Restore Width" afterwards actually works and the rendering issuees are gone again.

So the whole interaction between "auto-resize" and "visible columns" functionality needs to be checked, please fix

*** still buggy

In https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/c497fc44-ddaf-3cce-a9b4-bfec958a0f3c for example

resized properly, but when removing lets say the "Tracks" column with the "Visible Columns" button, the "Label" column
is not perfectly auto-resized anymore, but instead spreads over multiple rows. Also some other columns exhibit the same
behaviour. Including such a hidden column again, 'fixes' everything again.

** Keeps header + footer fixed while entries scroll in config dialog

Please add the following enhancements to the following function in the uploaded library

#+begin_example
        const settingsInterface = {
            values: {},
            init: function() {
                if (!configSchema) return;
                for (const key in configSchema) {
                    this.values[key] = GM_getValue(key, configSchema[key].default);
                }
            },
            // Helper to determine if text should be black or white based on background hex
            getContrastYIQ: function(hexcolor) {
                hexcolor = hexcolor.replace("#", "");
                const r = parseInt(hexcolor.substr(0, 2), 16);
                const g = parseInt(hexcolor.substr(2, 2), 16);
                const b = parseInt(hexcolor.substr(4, 2), 16);
                const yiq = ((r * 299) + (g * 587) + (b * 114)) / 1000;
                return (yiq >= 128) ? 'black' : 'white';
            },
            applyColorPreview: function(inputId, color) {
                const input = document.getElementById(inputId);
                const btn = document.getElementById(`${inputId}-picker-btn`);
                if (input) {
                    input.style.backgroundColor = color;
                    input.style.color = this.getContrastYIQ(color);
                }
                if (btn) {
                    btn.style.backgroundColor = color;
                }
            },
            save: function(newValues) {
                for (const key in newValues) {
                    GM_setValue(key, newValues[key]);
                }
                loggerInterface.info('init', "Settings saved. Reloading...");
                location.reload();
            },
            showModal: function() {
                if (!configSchema) return;
                const overlay = document.createElement("div");
                overlay.id = `${scriptId}-settings-overlay`;
                Object.assign(overlay.style, {
                    position: 'fixed', top: '0', left: '0', width: '100%', height: '100%',
                    backgroundColor: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.5)', zIndex: '10000', display: 'flex',
                    justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center'
                });

                const container = document.createElement("div");
                container.id = `${scriptId}-config-container`;
                Object.assign(container.style, {
                    backgroundColor: 'white',
                    border: '2px solid #4CAF50',
                    borderRadius: '8px',
                    padding: '20px',
                    color: '#333',
                    fontFamily: '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif',
                    minWidth: '600px',
                    maxWidth: '800px',
                    maxHeight: '80vh',
                    overflowY: 'auto',
                    boxShadow: '0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)'
                });

                const tableRows = Object.entries(configSchema).map(([key, cfg]) => {
                    // Handle divider type
                    if (cfg.type === 'divider') {
                        return `
                            <tr>
                                <td colspan="3" style="background: linear-gradient(to right, #999, #ccc, #999); padding: 12px 8px; text-align: center;">
                                    <strong style="color: white; text-shadow: 1px 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); font-size: 1.1em; letter-spacing: 1px;">
                                        ${cfg.label}
                                    </strong>
                                </td>
                            </tr>`;
                    }

                    const inputId = `${scriptId}-input-${key}`;
                    const isCheck = cfg.type === "checkbox";
                    const isColor = cfg.type === "color_picker";
                    const isNumber = cfg.type === "number";
                    const valAttr = isCheck
                        ? (this.values[key] ? 'checked' : '')
                        : `value="${this.values[key]}"`;

                    let inputHtml = '';

                    if (isCheck) {
                        inputHtml = `<input type="checkbox" id="${inputId}" ${valAttr} style="margin-left: 5px;">`;
                    } else if (isNumber) {
                        inputHtml = `<input type="number" id="${inputId}" ${valAttr} min="${cfg.min || 0}" max="${cfg.max || 100}" style="width: 80px; margin-left: 5px;">`;
                    } else {
                        inputHtml = `<input type="text" id="${inputId}" ${valAttr} style="width: 120px; margin-left: 5px; transition: background 0.2s;">`;
                    }

                    if (isColor) {
                        inputHtml += `<button id="${inputId}-picker-btn" type="button" style="margin-left: 5px; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid #666; width: 24px; height: 24px; vertical-align: middle;" title="Open Color Picker">🎨</button>`;
                    }

                    return `
                        <tr>
                            <th style="background-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); text-align: left; padding-left: inherit;">
                                <label style="white-space: nowrap; text-shadow: rgb(153, 153, 153) 1px 1px 2px;">
                                    ${cfg.label}: ${inputHtml}
                                </label>
                            </th>
                            <td style="opacity: 0.666; text-align: center;">${cfg.default}</td>
                            <td style="margin-bottom: 0.4em;">${cfg.description}</td>
                        </tr>`;
                }).join('');

                container.innerHTML = `
                    <p style="text-align: right; margin: 0px;">
                        <a id="${scriptId}-reset" style="cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold; color: black;">RESET</a> |
                        <a id="${scriptId}-close" style="cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold; color: black;">CLOSE</a>
                    </p>
                    <h4 style="text-shadow: white 0px 0px 8px; font-size: 1.5em; margin-top: 0px;">${scriptName.toUpperCase()}</h4>
                    <p><h2>Settings are applied immediately upon saving.</h2></p>
                    <table border="2" cellpadding="4" cellspacing="1" style="width: 100%; border-collapse: separate; background: #eee;">
                        <thead>
                            <tr style="background-color: #ccc;">
                                <th>setting</th>
                                <th>default setting</th>
                                <th>description</th>
                            </tr>
                        </thead>
                        <tbody>${tableRows}</tbody>
                    </table>
                    <div style="margin-top: 15px; text-align: right;">
                        <button id="${scriptId}-save-btn" style="padding: 4px 12px; cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold;">SAVE</button>
                    </div>
                    <div id="${scriptId}-picker-container" style="position: absolute; display: none; background: #eee; border: 1px solid #999; padding: 10px; z-index: 10001; box-shadow: 2px 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.2);"></div>
                `;

                overlay.appendChild(container);
                document.body.appendChild(overlay);

                const pickerContainer = document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-picker-container`);

                // Initial color preview application
                Object.entries(configSchema).forEach(([key, cfg]) => {
                    if (cfg.type === "color_picker") {
                        const inputId = `${scriptId}-input-${key}`;
                        this.applyColorPreview(inputId, this.values[key]);

                        const btn = document.getElementById(`${inputId}-picker-btn`);
                        const input = document.getElementById(inputId);

                        // Allow manual typing to update preview too
                        input.addEventListener('input', (e) => {
                            if (/^#[0-9A-F]{6}$/i.test(e.target.value)) {
                                this.applyColorPreview(inputId, e.target.value);
                            }
                        });

                        btn.onclick = (e) => {
                            e.stopPropagation();
                            pickerContainer.innerHTML = '';
                            pickerContainer.style.display = 'block';
                            pickerContainer.style.top = `${btn.offsetTop + 30}px`;
                            pickerContainer.style.left = `${btn.offsetLeft}px`;

                            if (typeof iro !== 'undefined') {
                                const activePicker = new iro.ColorPicker(`#${scriptId}-picker-container`, {
                                    width: 150,
                                    color: input.value || cfg.default
                                });

                                activePicker.on('color:change', (color) => {
                                    // console.debug('New Hex:', color.hexString);
                                    input.value = color.hexString;
                                    this.applyColorPreview(inputId, color.hexString);
                                });
                            } else {
                                loggerInterface.error('error', "iro.js library not found!");
                                pickerContainer.textContent = "iro.js missing";
                            }
                        };
                    }
                });

                const closeDialog = () => {
                    if (document.body.contains(overlay)) document.body.removeChild(overlay);
                    window.removeEventListener("keydown", handleEsc);
                };
                const handleEsc = (e) => { if (e.key === "Escape") closeDialog(); };
                window.addEventListener("keydown", handleEsc);

                document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-save-btn`).onclick = () => {
                    const newValues = {};
                    for (const key in configSchema) {
                        // Skip dividers
                        if (configSchema[key].type === 'divider') continue;

                        const input = document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-input-${key}`);
                        newValues[key] = configSchema[key].type === "checkbox" ? input.checked : input.value;
                    }
                    this.save(newValues);
                    closeDialog();
                };
                document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-reset`).onclick = () => {
                    for (const key in configSchema) {
                        // Skip dividers
                        if (configSchema[key].type === 'divider') continue;

                        const input = document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-input-${key}`);
                        if (configSchema[key].type === "checkbox") {
                            input.checked = configSchema[key].default;
                        } else {
                            input.value = configSchema[key].default;
                            if (configSchema[key].type === "color_picker") {
                                this.applyColorPreview(inputId, configSchema[key].default);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                };
                document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-close`).onclick = closeDialog;

                overlay.onclick = (e) => {
                    if (e.target === overlay) closeDialog();
                    if (!pickerContainer.contains(e.target)) pickerContainer.style.display = 'none';
                };
            }
        };
#+end_example

Please add the following enhancements to the following function in the uploaded library

#+begin_example
        const changelogInterface = {
            show: function() {
                if (!changelog || changelog.length === 0) return;
                let logDiv = document.getElementById(`${scriptId}-changelog`);
                if (!logDiv) {
                    logDiv = document.createElement("div");
                    logDiv.id = `${scriptId}-changelog`;
                    // Setup the modal container
                    logDiv.style.cssText = "position:fixed; left:0; right:0; top:10em; z-index:3000009; margin-left:auto; margin-right:auto; min-height:8em; width:50%; background-color:#eee; color:#111; border-radius:5px; padding:1.5em; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); border: 1px solid #ccc; display:none; flex-direction: column;";

                    // Title with two new lines (via margin-bottom)
                    const title = document.createElement("b");
                    title.style.whiteSpace = "pre-wrap";
                    title.textContent = `${scriptName}\n\nChangeLog:    .... click to dismiss`;
                    logDiv.appendChild(title);

                    // Version List
                    const list = document.createElement("ul");
                    list.style.marginTop = "0.5em";
                    list.style.flexGrow = "1";
                    changelog.forEach(entry => {
                        const li = document.createElement("li");
                        //li.style.marginBottom = "0.5em";
                        li.innerHTML = `<i>${entry.version}</i> - ${entry.description}`;
                        list.appendChild(li);
                    });
                    logDiv.appendChild(list);

                    // Added CLOSE button at the bottom
                    const footer = document.createElement("div");
                    footer.style.textAlign = "right";
                    footer.style.marginTop = "1em";

                    const closeBtn = document.createElement("button");
                    closeBtn.textContent = "CLOSE";
                    closeBtn.style.cssText = "padding: 4px 12px; cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold; border: 1px solid #999; border-radius: 3px; background: #ddd;";
                    closeBtn.onclick = (e) => {
                        e.stopPropagation();
                        logDiv.style.display = 'none';
                    };

                    footer.appendChild(closeBtn);
                    logDiv.appendChild(footer);

                    document.body.appendChild(logDiv);

                    // Still allow clicking the background to dismiss
                    logDiv.addEventListener('click', () => { logDiv.style.display = 'none'; }, false);
                }
                logDiv.style.display = 'block';
            }
        };
#+end_example
