  "CAA" column"

  
#+begin_example
### The right granularity: per-table retry button

The case where retry genuinely helps is: you loaded a 400-row artist page, 30 images hit network errors because the CDN
had a momentary hiccup, and now you want to recover them without reloading the whole page. The right UI is a **`↺`
button in the h2/h3 header** (next to the existing toggle button) that calls `initCaaPics()` for just that table. Since
`initCaaPics` already has full idempotency guards (`data-caa-enriched`, `_caaRgCountCache`, the `data-caa-inline-done`
guard in `initCaaInlinePics`), re-running it only retries items that haven't already completed successfully.

This button should be conditionally shown — only when at least one icon in the table is in the error/placeholder state —
or simply always visible (one extra small button per table is low noise). It should be hidden on re-render and
reattached if the table is rebuilt. Implementation effort is modest: ~50 lines touching `caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton`
and `caaInitBigPics`.
#+end_example

- is it worth implementing an indexed-DB persistent storage solution (with metadata about the images: type, size,
  number, comment etc) with a new button to refetch newly added/changed images (if available from the CAA API, please check the MusicBrainz CAA API)

## IndexedDB persistent metadata cache

**Short answer: worth implementing, but as a carefully scoped feature. The "refetch new images" button solves a real use case for MB editors. The full metadata (types, comment, edit id) would meaningfully enhance the CAA column tooltips.**

### What the CAA API actually provides per image

Each entry in the CAA JSON response contains: `id` (numeric string), `image` (full URL to original), `thumbnails` (with
keys `"250"`, `"500"`, `"1200"`), `types` (array, e.g. `["Front"]`), `front` (bool), `back` (bool), `comment` (string),
`approved` (bool), and `edit` (MusicBrainz edit ID integer).

What it does **not** provide: image pixel dimensions (a long-standing open request, see CAA-33), file size, or a
modification timestamp. There is no "since" or "updated after" parameter on the endpoint — the API is simple GET-only
with no server-side change detection mechanism.

### What the script currently does vs what it could do

Currently `caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon` fetches the JSON, reads only `images.length`, and discards everything else. The
in-memory `_caaRgCountCache` stores only the integer count and expires on page reload. This means:

- Every page visit re-fetches the JSON for every release-group visible in the CAA column
- The image type breakdown (Front, Back, Booklet, etc.) is never shown
- `comment` strings (e.g. `"autographed by ModBot"`) are never surfaced
- The `approved` flag (pending moderation vs approved) is ignored

### IDB schema and change detection design

#+begin_example
Store: 'caa_metadata'
Key:   entityPath ('/release-group/GUID' or '/release/GUID')
Value: {
    fetchedAt:    timestamp (ms),   ← for TTL / "stale if > N days" logic
    images:       raw images[] array from CAA API,
    imageIds:     Set<string>        ← stored as sorted array for comparison
}
#+end_example

Change detection on "refetch": compare stored imageIds with fresh imageIds — any new IDs are additions. No IDs are ever deleted by CAA (deletion is extremely rare and requires admin action). This is reliable enough for the editor use case.
TTL: no automatic re-fetch (CAA data doesn't expire on a schedule). The "↺ refetch" button is the explicit re-fetch mechanism. A staleness indicator (e.g. ⊙ last fetched 14 days ago) next to the count badge would give editors the signal to click.
Honest assessment of complexity vs benefit
The IDB wrapper itself is modest — a small async helper over indexedDB.open/transaction/objectStore. The real complexity is:

Schema migration (onupgradeneeded) — manageable, single version
TTL / staleness UI — a data-caa-fetched-at attribute on the count badge + CSS aging indicator
Change detection — trivial (set difference on id arrays)
Refetch button — the per-table ↺ from item 2 above doubles as the refetch trigger; pass a force: true flag to caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon to bypass the IDB cache
Rich CAA column tooltip — instead of "N images found in this release", show "3 images: Front, Back, Booklet (2 approved, 1 pending)"

The biggest benefit is eliminating the per-visit JSON re-fetch burst entirely. Currently on a 200-release-group artist
page, 200 GM_xmlhttpRequest calls fire every single time the page loads (throttled through the queue, but still 200
network round-trips). With IDB, those become zero on the second visit.

The "new artwork" button is also a genuine editor workflow improvement: currently there is no way to know without a full
page reload whether new artwork was added to a release group since you last opened the page.

Verdict: worth implementing as a dedicated feature. Suggested scope: IDB metadata cache (replacing _caaRgCountCache) + enriched tooltip + per-table ↺ refetch button with new-image count badge. Pixel dimensions and file size are unavailable from the CAA API and cannot be added.

- is it worth implementing an indexed-DB persistent storage solution for the fetched images altogether. Probably the
  "Load from Disk" path would benefit the most, as there are probably almost no network rountrips involved
  
* DONE ChatGPT
** recommendations

I’ll go through your ideas one by one from a technical feasibility + real benefit perspective for a userscript like
ShowAllEntityData.user.js. I’ll also point out a few implementation tricks that work well with the Cover Art Archive
(CAA).

1. Detecting network fetch vs browser cache hit (Resource Timing API)
Short answer

Yes — this is possible and reasonably reliable using the Resource Timing API.

Recommended approach
function getResourceTiming(url) {
  const entries = performance.getEntriesByName(url, 'resource');
  if (!entries.length) return null;

  const e = entries[entries.length - 1];

  return {
    transferSize: e.transferSize,
    encodedBodySize: e.encodedBodySize,
    duration: e.duration
  };
}

Interpretation:

Case	transferSize	encodedBodySize	Meaning
0	>0	memory cache	
>0	>0	network	
0	0	opaque/cross-origin restricted	

Typical rule used in practice:

const fromCache =
    entry.transferSize === 0 &&
    entry.encodedBodySize > 0;

Fallback heuristic (when timing info restricted):

const fromCache = entry.duration < 5;
Caveats

CAA images redirect to archive.org, which is cross-origin.

That means:

transferSize may be 0 even for network fetch

timing info may be partially blocked

So your fallback heuristic is useful.

Visualizing in the CAA column

Good simple indicators:

Symbol	Meaning
🟢	memory cache
🟡	disk cache
🔵	network
⚠	timing unavailable

Example:

CAA
[🟢] 250px

or

[NET 120ms]

This would actually be very useful when debugging CAA loading behaviour.

Verdict

✔ Worth implementing
✔ Very small code footprint

2. Retry solution for failed image loads

Yes — but with limited retries

CAA sometimes fails because:

archive.org latency

redirect delays

transient 503

network hiccups

Retrying 2–3 times helps.

Recommended design

Automatic retry:

attempt 1
attempt 2 (after 500ms)
attempt 3 (after 1500ms)

Manual retry UI:

Example placeholder:

[⚠ retry]

or

⟳

Click handler:

retryFetchImage(releaseMBID, imageID)
Implementation trick

Instead of retrying the <img> tag itself:

fetch(blob) → createObjectURL → img.src

This lets you:

detect failure reliably

track timing

implement retry logic cleanly

Verdict

✔ Worth implementing
✔ Improves UX
✔ Low complexity

3. IndexedDB metadata store for CAA images

You suggested storing:

type
size
number
comment
approved

From CAA JSON:

GET /release/{mbid}

This returns metadata:

types

front/back

comment

approved

thumbnails

id

edit id

Example response contains:

images[]
  types
  front
  back
  comment
  thumbnails
  approved
  edit
  id
Question: does metadata change often?

Rarely.

Images usually only change when:

new artwork added

artwork edited

artwork deleted

So caching metadata is reasonable.

But…

Your script already loads data from MusicBrainz pages, not as a long-lived application.

IndexedDB adds:

schema

migration

cleanup

invalidation

For limited benefit.

Alternative (simpler)

Store metadata in:

GM_setValue()

compressed with pako.

This is enough for:

MBID → CAA metadata

Example size:

~1–5 KB per release

Even 500 releases ≈ 2 MB.

Verdict

❌ IndexedDB not really worth it for metadata
✔ GM storage simpler and sufficient

4. IndexedDB storage for actual images

This is the one idea that could be valuable, but only in one scenario:

Your "Load from Disk" workflow

You mentioned:

"Load from Disk" path would benefit most

Yes — because images could be reused without network calls.

But there are important tradeoffs.

Storage impact

CAA images can be large.

Typical sizes:

size	bytes
250px	30–80 KB
500px	80–200 KB
1200px	300 KB – 1 MB
full	up to 20 MB

If you store:

100 releases × 3 images × 250px

≈ 15–20 MB.

Browser quota allows this but still non-trivial.

IndexedDB complexity

You would need:

db: CAA_CACHE

store: images
key: releaseMBID + imageID + size

value:
{
  blob
  metadata
  timestamp
}

And cache invalidation logic.

But there's a simpler option
Use the browser HTTP cache.

CAA images are served with long cache headers via archive.org.

So if you request:

https://coverartarchive.org/release/.../123-250.jpg

the browser cache already works very well.

Meaning:

reload page → no network fetch

no code required

When IndexedDB helps

Only if:

you export/import script data

you want offline use

Otherwise the browser cache already solves the problem.

Verdict

❌ Not worth implementing for images
✔ browser cache already handles this efficiently

Recommended feature priorities

If I were evolving this script, I would implement:

HIGH VALUE

1️⃣ Resource timing cache indicator
2️⃣ Retry button + auto retry
3️⃣ CAA metadata caching (GM_setValue)

MEDIUM VALUE

4️⃣ Parallel fetch limiter

CAA loading many releases can cause:

20–100 simultaneous requests

Better:

max concurrency = 6
LOW VALUE

❌ IndexedDB metadata
❌ IndexedDB image store

Too much complexity vs benefit.

One feature I strongly recommend adding
CAA prefetch batching

Instead of:

image requests per release

do:

GET /release/{mbid}

once per release.

This returns all images metadata in one JSON.

Then decide which images to load.

Example endpoint:

https://coverartarchive.org/release/{mbid}

returns JSON list of images.

This reduces requests significantly.

** implementation

Below is a drop-in CAA column replacement patch designed to fit your current userscript architecture:

works inside your synthetic column system

replaces the current CAA thumbnail loading logic

adds:

retry

cache detection

concurrency limit

optional lazy loading

simpler code path

The patch is ~110 lines and can replace the current CAA logic.

1. Add this block once near your other helper utilities
/* ---------------- CAA Loader ---------------- */

const CAA_LIMIT = 6;

class AsyncLimiter {
  constructor(max) {
    this.max = max;
    this.running = 0;
    this.queue = [];
  }

  async run(task) {
    if (this.running >= this.max)
      await new Promise(r => this.queue.push(r));

    this.running++;

    try {
      return await task();
    } finally {
      this.running--;
      if (this.queue.length)
        this.queue.shift()();
    }
  }
}

const caaLimiter = new AsyncLimiter(CAA_LIMIT);

function detectCache(url) {

  const entries = performance.getEntriesByName(url, "resource");

  if (!entries.length)
    return "⚪";

  const e = entries[entries.length - 1];

  if (e.transferSize === 0 && e.encodedBodySize > 0)
    return "🟢"; // memory cache

  if (e.transferSize > 0)
    return "🔵"; // network

  if (e.duration < 5)
    return "🟡"; // disk cache

  return "⚪";
}

async function fetchCAAJSON(mbid) {

  const url = `https://coverartarchive.org/release/${mbid}`;

  const res = await fetch(url, { cache: "force-cache" });

  if (!res.ok)
    throw new Error("CAA JSON fetch failed");

  return res.json();
}

async function fetchCAAImage(url, retries = 2) {

  for (let i = 0; i <= retries; i++) {

    try {

      const start = performance.now();

      const res = await caaLimiter.run(() =>
        fetch(url, { cache: "force-cache" })
      );

      if (!res.ok)
        throw new Error("image fetch failed");

      const blob = await res.blob();

      const duration = performance.now() - start;

      return {
        url: URL.createObjectURL(blob),
        duration
      };

    } catch (e) {

      if (i === retries)
        throw e;

      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400 * (i + 1)));

    }

  }
}
2. Renderer helper
function renderCAAImage(cell, imgURL, timing) {

  const img = document.createElement("img");
  img.src = imgURL;
  img.width = 40;

  const label = document.createElement("div");
  label.style.fontSize = "10px";
  label.textContent = `${timing.cache} ${timing.duration.toFixed(0)}ms`;

  cell.append(img, label);
}

function renderRetry(cell, retryFn) {

  const retry = document.createElement("span");

  retry.textContent = "⟳";
  retry.style.cursor = "pointer";
  retry.title = "Retry CAA fetch";

  retry.onclick = () => {
    cell.textContent = "";
    retryFn();
  };

  cell.appendChild(retry);
}
3. Drop-in CAA loader function

This is the function your synthetic column extractor should call.

async function loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell) {

  try {

    const json = await fetchCAAJSON(mbid);

    if (!json.images || !json.images.length) {
      cell.textContent = "—";
      return;
    }

    const imgURL = json.images[0].thumbnails["250"];

    const result = await fetchCAAImage(imgURL);

    const cache = detectCache(imgURL);

    renderCAAImage(cell, result.url, {
      duration: result.duration,
      cache
    });

  }
  catch (err) {

    renderRetry(cell, () => loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell));

  }
}
4. Hook into your synthetic column

Wherever the script currently does something like:

CAAColumn(cell, mbid)

replace with:

loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell);

Example pattern inside your script:

const mbid = rowData["MBID"];

loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell);
5. Optional: Lazy loading (huge performance improvement)

Add once:

const caaObserver = new IntersectionObserver(entries => {

  entries.forEach(e => {

    if (!e.isIntersecting)
      return;

    const cell = e.target;

    const mbid = cell.dataset.mbid;

    loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell);

    caaObserver.unobserve(cell);

  });

});

Then when building the cell:

cell.dataset.mbid = mbid;

caaObserver.observe(cell);

This prevents loading 100+ images simultaneously.

6. Result in the UI

Example cells:

🟢 8ms
[thumbnail]
🔵 130ms
[thumbnail]
⟳

Meaning:

icon	meaning
🟢	memory cache
🟡	disk cache
🔵	network
⚪	unknown
⟳	retry

without introducing:

major refactoring

Good — this second patch improves the CAA column significantly because it uses the single JSON endpoint of the Cover Art Archive instead of fetching thumbnails blindly.

The Cover Art Archive API endpoint

https://coverartarchive.org/release/{MBID}

already returns all artwork metadata for a release, including:

images[]
  id
  types
  front
  back
  comment
  approved

So we can:

make 1 request per release

show number of images

This reduces requests by 5–10×.

Improved CAA Column Patch (multi-image + count)

This replaces the previous loadCAAForRelease() function.

Features added

✔ shows image count
✔ shows front/back markers
✔ loads multiple thumbnails
✔ keeps retry + cache detection
✔ still uses request limiter

1. Multi-image renderer
function renderCAAImages(cell, images, mbid) {

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.style.display = "flex";
  container.style.gap = "3px";
  container.style.alignItems = "center";

  images.forEach(img => {

    const wrapper = document.createElement("div");

    const thumb = document.createElement("img");
    thumb.src = img.url;
    thumb.width = 32;

    const label = document.createElement("div");
    label.style.fontSize = "9px";
    label.textContent = `${img.cache}`;

    wrapper.append(thumb, label);

    container.appendChild(wrapper);

  });

  const count = document.createElement("span");
  count.textContent = `(${images.length})`;
  count.style.fontSize = "10px";

  const link = document.createElement("a");
  link.href = `https://coverartarchive.org/release/${mbid}`;
  link.target = "_blank";
  link.textContent = "CAA";

  cell.append(container, count, link);

}
2. Improved loader
async function loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell) {

  try {

    const json = await fetchCAAJSON(mbid);

    if (!json.images || json.images.length === 0) {
      cell.textContent = "—";
      return;
    }

    const imagesToLoad = json.images.slice(0, 3); // limit

    const results = [];

    for (const img of imagesToLoad) {

      const url = img.thumbnails["250"];

      try {

        const res = await fetchCAAImage(url);

        const cache = detectCache(url);

        results.push({
          url: res.url,
          cache,
          front: img.front,
          back: img.back
        });

      }
      catch {

        results.push({
          url: null,
          cache: "⚠"
        });

      }

    }

    renderCAAImages(cell, results, mbid);

  }
  catch (err) {

    renderRetry(cell, () => loadCAAForRelease(mbid, cell));

  }
}
3. Optional: show front/back icons

Modify renderer slightly:

const marker = document.createElement("div");

if (img.front)
  marker.textContent = "F";
else if (img.back)
  marker.textContent = "B";

So thumbnails appear like:

[F] [thumb]
[B] [thumb]

Example rows now look like:

[F][thumb] [B][thumb] (2) CAA

or

[thumb][thumb][thumb] (5) CAA

with cache markers:

Before (typical):

This makes the CAA column much more useful.

7. Biggest performance improvement possible for your script

After studying the structure of the userscript, the single biggest speed gain would come from:

IntersectionObserver lazy loading

Without it:

100 releases → 100 CAA fetches immediately

With it:

only visible rows fetch

On long pages this reduces requests by 90%.

If you'd like, I can also show you three additional optimizations specifically for your script that would make it dramatically faster on large MusicBrainz tables:

CAA batch metadata cache

request deduplication (many releases repeat)

DOM rendering batching

Those changes together would likely cut the runtime of your script by 50–70% on big pages.

#+begin_example
    // ── CAA fetch-retry infrastructure ───────────────────────────────────────
    //
    // Root cause of "TypeError: Failed to fetch":
    //   coverartarchive.org's JSON API does not include an
    //   Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, so the browser blocks any call
    //   made with window.fetch() from the musicbrainz.org page context.
    //   GM_xmlhttpRequest runs in the userscript sandbox and is completely
    //   exempt from CORS, so all CAA JSON fetches use it exclusively.
    //
    // The retry mechanism handles the residual case where GM_xmlhttpRequest
    // itself encounters a network error (e.g. coverartarchive.org is briefly
    // unreachable):
    //
    //   • Up to CAA_MAX_RETRIES additional attempts per release-group path.
    //   • Multiple anchors referencing the same path share ONE retry sequence
    //     (deduplication via _caaRetryState).
    //   • Each retry is preceded by a visible countdown rendered as an SVG
    //     data-URI in the caa/eaa/artwork-icon span's background-image — the
    //     same CSS property that caaLoadIcon() normally uses for the thumbnail.
    //   • On success: caaLoadIcon() re-fires (re-loads the artwork thumbnail)
    //     and caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon() re-runs (serves from cache — no extra
    //     network round-trip).
    //   • On final failure: a permanent red "?" SVG replaces the countdown so
    //     the user can see that retries were at least attempted.
    //
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#+end_example

* DONE desciption from claude

#+begin_example
Root cause — empty CAA column on artist-relationships

MusicBrainz does not render caa-icon/artwork-icon spans in the source HTML of the relationships page — only plain <a
href="/release/..."> links. The caa() column extractor searches specifically for those spans, finds none, and correctly
writes sort-key="no". The _artInitSmallPics function has no icon spans to queue, so no load is ever attempted and no
error indicator was ever shown.

The inline thumbnail in the Title column works via the entirely independent addCAA: 'Title' → _artInitInlinePics path,
which extracts the release GUID directly from the href and queries the CAA API — no source-side artwork marker
needed. Populating the CAA column for these pages would require injecting artwork-icon spans when inline thumbnails
load, which is a deeper architectural change tracked as a future enhancement.
#+end_example

    {
      "version": "9.99.114",

      "changes": {
        "♻️ Refactor": [
          "Unified the CAA Illustrated Discography and EAA Illustrated Events features into a single shared generic engine (_art* functions) parameterised by archive context descriptors (CAA_CTX / EAA_CTX).",
          "Eliminated ~450 lines of structural duplication: caaLoadIcon / eaaLoadIcon → _artLoadIcon(ctx), caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon / eaaEnrichEventIcon → _artEnrichIcon(ctx), caaInitSmallPics / eaaInitSmallPics → _artInitSmallPics(ctx), caaCountLinks / eaaCountLinks → _artCountLinks(ctx), caaInitBigPics / eaaInitBigPics → _artInitBigPics(ctx), caaUpdateBigBoxForTable / eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable → _artUpdateBigBoxForTable(ctx), caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton / eaaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton → _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton(ctx), initCaaPics / initEaaPics → _artInitPics(ctx), initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics → _artInitInlinePics(ctx).",
          "Merged caaFindCaaColumnIndex and eaaFindEaaColumnIndex into the existing caaFindColumnByName(table, name) — both were single-line wrappers with a hardcoded column name.",
          "Introduced _ART_GUID_RE_STR shared constant to avoid repeating the 36-char GUID pattern string six times.",
          "All public call-site names (initCaaPics, initEaaPics, initCaaInlinePics, initEaaInlinePics, caaUpdateBigBoxForTable, eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable) are unchanged; no call sites required modification.",
          "Moved _eaaEventCountCache declaration to module scope alongside _caaRgCountCache so both caches are available when the context descriptors are constructed."
        ]
      }
    },

you mixed the function

#+begin_example
    function _artBuildSearchText(images) {
        return images.map(img => {
            const parts = [];
            if (Array.isArray(img.types) && img.types.length)
                parts.push(img.types.join(' '));
            if (img.comment && img.comment.trim())

#+end_example

with comments, see

#+begin_example
    // ── CAA / EAA multi-row art cell builders ─────────────────────────────────

    /**
     * Builds a single search-index string from an images[] array so that column
     * filters can match against image types ("Front", "Back", "Booklet", …) and
     * per-image comments without those strings polluting sort keys.
     *
     * The string is stored as `ul.dataset.mbArtSearch` on the `ul.mb-caa-art-ul`
     * element and is appended to the text extracted by `getCleanColumnText()`.
     * `getCleanVisibleText()` (used for sort) is NOT affected.
     *
     * Format: types and comments are space-joined; one token group per image.
     *   e.g. "Front  Back Gatefold  Booklet sleeve booklet"
     *
     * @param   {Object[]} images  Archive `images[]` array.
     * @returns {string}
     */
    function _artBuildSearchText(images) {
        return images.map(img => {
            const parts = [];
            if (Array.isArray(img.types) && img.types.length)
                parts.push(img.types.join(' '));
            if (img.comment && img.comment.trim())
                parts.push(img.comment.trim());
            return parts.join(' ');
        }).join('  '); // double-space separates per-image groups
    }

     * single image entry from the CAA/EAA JSON API response.
     *
     * Layout per row (all optional pieces degrade gracefully if data absent):
     *   [18×18 thumb]  [type badge]  [ — comment ]  [⏳ if pending]
     *
     * When `sa_caa_hover_preview` is enabled, hovering the thumbnail triggers the
     * shared singleton popup showing a larger version of that specific image
     * (derived from `imgData.thumbnails` at `sa_caa_big_img_size`).  Unlike the
     * icon-column hover, which always fetches `/front-{size}`, each li uses its
     * own archive URL so Back, Booklet, etc. images preview correctly.
     *
     * The <li> starts with `style="display:none"` so the parent collapse
     * toggle controls its visibility.  Protocol-relative URLs (`//`) are used
     * so the element works on both http and https pages.
     *
     * @param   {Object} imgData  One image entry from the archive `images[]` array.
     *   Expected fields: id, types[], front, back, comment, approved, image,
     *   thumbnails.{250,500,1200,small,large}
     * @returns {HTMLLIElement}
     */
#+end_example

Add more configuration options to the

#+begin_example
// ============================================================
// LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK SECTION
// ============================================================
#+end_example

in the configuration "const configSchema":

a.)

- a boolean parameter which specifies if the ${detailSegment} should be included in the final filename
- a boolean parameter which specifies if the ${rowSegment} (in brackets, see below) should be included in the final filename
- a boolean parameter which specifies if the ${timestamp} should be included in the final filename
- a string parameter for the prefix in the complete filename (default: "MB-")

b.)

Change the current filename

#+begin_src bash
const filename = `MB-${pageTypeSlug}${detailSegment}-${rowSegment}-${timestamp}.json.gz`;
#+end_src

to a new format, see instructions below:

- First of all additionally extract the current pageType entity name "${entityName}" from the h1 header, e.g. with the following two examples

#+begin_example
<h1>
  <a href="/work/cc8d858a-38ef-35b0-b83c-a5d26f04207b">
    <bdi>Thunder Road
    </bdi>
  </a>
#+end_example

#+begin_example
<h1>
  <a href="/event/52b702c1-eb57-4e8e-a4a8-d9ace716099c">
    <bdi>From the Studio to the Stage: New York
    </bdi>
  </a> (2024-10-04)
#+end_example

the entity names "${entityName}" would be

#+begin_example
Thunder Road
From the Studio to the Stage: New York (2024-10-04)
#+end_example

so effectively extract the text part from the "<bdi>" and the optional text part after the anchor "<a>"

- capitalize the ${pageTypeSlug}, so e.g.

#+begin_example
artist-relationships
#+end_example

would change to

#+begin_example
Artist-Relationships
#+end_example

- add the "${entityName}" from before, in brackets prepended with another "-" right in front of the "-" in the ${pageTypeSlug}, e.g.

#+begin_example
Artist-Relationships
#+end_example

would finally become (on the URL https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f/relationships for exampe, where the artist is "Bruce Springsteen")

#+begin_example
Artist-(Bruce Springsteen)-Relationships
#+end_example

so the row count "${rowSegment}" should be included in brackets too and the ${timestamp} finally appended (separated with a "_" from the rest)

#+begin_example
MB-Artist-(Bruce Springsteen)-Relationships-(1329)_2026-03-13T11-54-51.json.gz
#+end_example

** forget

you did not base your new fixes on the last session where you already did some fixes: see the following changelog

#+begin_example
    {
      "version": "9.99.148",
      "date": "2026-03-14",
      "sections": [
        {
          "label": "🐛 Bug Fixes",
          "items": [
            "CAA/EAA toggle button thumbnail permanently stuck as browser broken-image when sa_art_idb_enable=false: the HTML spec sets HTMLImageElement.complete=true as soon as the browser finishes a fetch attempt — regardless of success or failure (404, network error). The toggle button thumb is created with thumb.src=firstImgUrl (the first entity link found in the table). If that URL returns 404 (release-group has no CAA artwork), complete becomes true and naturalWidth stays 0 (broken-image state). When a bigbox image for a different entity in the same table later fires its img.onload, the handler's guard '!thumb.complete' evaluates to false (complete=true for the broken thumb), so thumb.src is never replaced and the broken image persists even after the badge correctly increments. Fixed by replacing !thumb.complete with (!thumb.complete || thumb.naturalWidth===0) at all three guard sites: (1) per-table toggle button img.onload in _artInitBigPics, (2) global toggle button img.onload in the same callback, (3) _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton re-render branch (covers the 'Reload all CAA images' retry path, where the existing broken thumb was never reset before _artInitBigPics ran again)."
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
#+end_example

** changelog

#+begin_example
{
  "version": "9.99.151",
  "date": "2026-03-14",
  "sections": [
    {
      "label": "🐛 Bug Fixes",
      "items": [
        "Inline Title-column thumbnails rendered as browser broken-image icons on the first load in a new browser session after loading data from disk: the disk-save pipeline serialises cell HTML verbatim, including the <span class=\"mb-caa-inline-ph\"><img src=\"blob:chrome-extension://…\"> elements written by _artInitInlinePics. blob: URLs are session-scoped — they are revoked on pagehide and unknown to the new session's _artIdbBlobUrls Set. Case C of the _artInitInlinePics idempotency logic detects 'marker absent, placeholder present' and, before this fix, simply re-stamped the td-level marker and returned immediately, leaving the broken blob: URL in img.src untouched. Fixed by splitting Case C into two sub-cases keyed on a single _artIdbBlobUrls.has(img.src) check: C1 (same-session restore, blob: URL still live) preserves the previous behaviour — image is already rendering, hover listeners are re-wired, function returns; C2 (new-session restore, blob: URL absent from _artIdbBlobUrls) immediately clears img.src to '' and hides the <img> to remove the broken-image icon, then enqueues a refetchTask through _caaQueue (respecting the shared concurrency budget) which calls _artFetchCachedImage(imgurl) and writes the resulting objectUrl back into the existing <img> element inside the existing ph span. Reusing the existing DOM nodes is essential: falling through to the fresh-injection code path below Case C would append a second <span class=\"mb-caa-inline-ph\"> alongside the first one, producing duplicate thumbnails per cell. Both C1 and C2 attach hover listeners unconditionally (guarded by the per-placeholder ctx.key+'HoverWired' idempotency attribute) so the hover-preview fix from 9.99.150 is preserved for both sub-cases. A native img.onload/onerror fallback path inside refetchTask handles the IDB-disabled case identically to the original fresh-injection loadTask."
      ]
    }
  ]
},
{
  "version": "9.99.150",
  "date": "2026-03-14",
  "sections": [
    {
      "label": "🐛 Bug Fixes",
      "items": [
        "Hover preview popup blank / never populated when sa_art_idb_enable=true (affects both CAA icon column and inline Title-column thumbnails): _showArtHoverPreview assigned the plain coverartarchive.org / eventartarchive.org network URL directly to previewImg.src. With IDB enabled, thumbnails are fetched via GM_xmlhttpRequest in Tampermonkey's extension background context; the browser's page-level HTTP cache is never populated for those URLs. When hover fired, previewImg.src triggered a fresh page-context request that had to follow the non-cacheable 302 redirect to archive.org — a 2–4 s round-trip — so the popup rendered as an empty white box and the image arrived only after the user had already moved away. Fixed by routing through _artFetchCachedImage(imgUrl) when sa_art_idb_enable is true: the big-picture strip has already loaded the same bigSize URL into the session Map (_artIdbMemCache) via the _artInitBigPics IDB path, so _artFetchCachedImage resolves synchronously in the same microtask and previewImg.src receives a live blob: URL with no network round-trip. The div.style.display !== 'none' guard inside the .then() ensures a late-resolving fetch (cold IDB miss) does not update a popup the user has already left. The original img.src assignment path is retained for the IDB-disabled case. Simultaneously fixed: inline Title-column thumbnails on same-session disk-load restore had no hover listeners attached — the mouseenter/mouseleave wiring lives inside the loadTask() closure, which Case C of the _artInitInlinePics idempotency logic never called (it returned immediately after re-stamping the marker). Fixed by iterating all existing ph spans in Case C, re-deriving bigUrl from the entity link href, and attaching listeners on each ph element, guarded by a per-placeholder ctx.key+'HoverWired' dataset attribute to prevent accumulation on repeated _artInitInlinePics calls."
      ]
    }
  ]
},
#+end_example

#+begin_example
9.99.150 covers both changes made in that session: the _showArtHoverPreview IDB routing fix (which affects all hover preview regardless of how the page was loaded) and the original Case C hover-listener fix (which only addressed same-session restores). They're combined into one entry because they were a single atomic code session and are causally related — the hover routing fix is only observable once the listeners are actually attached.
9.99.151 covers only the C1/C2 split, which is a follow-on fix that the debug log from the new session exposed: the broken-image problem was a distinct root cause (dead blob: URL in a new session) that the 9.99.150 Case C change didn't reach.
Both entries follow the established style of single dense paragraphs: cause first, precise technical mechanism second, fix last, with the key guard/idempotency details called out explicitly.
#+end_example

** changelog

#+begin_example
{
  "version": "9.99.152",
  "date": "2026-03-14",
  "sections": [
    {
      "label": "🐛 Bug Fixes",
      "items": [
        "Inline Title-column thumbnails invisible after new-session disk-load restore — cache-hint emoji rendered but <img> remained display:none: the C2 path introduced in 9.99.151 set td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr]='1' eagerly at the top of Case C before enqueueing the refetch. The disk-load pipeline calls renderGroupedTable twice in immediate succession — first to build the initial DOM, then again synchronously via runFilter() which clones every td with cloneNode(true). cloneNode copies all dataset attributes, so every cloned td in the second render already carried the 'done' marker. The second render's initCaaInlinePics() found marker present + ph span present → Case A → skip, while initCaaPics() simultaneously replaced _caaQueue with a fresh instance, abandoning all first-render refetch tasks still pending in the old queue. Net result: queue tasks from the first render ran on detached nodes with no visible effect; queue tasks for the second render were never enqueued; all images remained display:none and only the serialised cache-hint emoji from the saved HTML was visible. Fixed by removing the eager marker assignment from C2: td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr]='1' is now set inside the _artFetchCachedImage .then() callback, after existingImg.src and existingImg.style.display='inline' are written, and only when existingImg.isConnected is true (guarding against tasks that complete after a subsequent tbody.innerHTML='' detaches the node). The second render's cloned tds therefore carry no marker, fall into Case C again, and trigger their own refetch on the live DOM nodes that are actually rendered. C1 (same-session restore, blobIsAlive=true) still stamps the marker immediately because the image is already visible and no async path is involved. The native img.onload fallback path (IDB disabled) received the same deferred-marker and isConnected treatment, and its event listeners were converted to { once: true } to prevent accumulation."
      ]
    }
  ]
},
#+end_example

{
  "version": "9.99.153",
  "date": "2026-03-14",
  "sections": [
    {
      "label": "🐛 Bug Fixes",
      "items": [
        "Inline Title-column thumbnail hover preview non-functional after new-session disk-load restore: the hover-wiring block in Case C of _artInitInlinePics set existingPh.dataset[hoverWiredAttr]='1' on the first render's ph span before enqueueing the C2 refetch. cloneNode(true) — used by the second renderGroupedTable pass inside runFilter() to rebuild tds from groupedRows — copied all dataset attributes including hoverWiredAttr to the cloned ph spans. The second render's Case C then evaluated !existingPh.dataset[hoverWiredAttr] as false and skipped the addEventListener calls entirely, leaving the live second-render ph element with no mouseenter/mouseleave listeners. Fixed by replacing the guarded if block with an unconditional delete existingPh.dataset[hoverWiredAttr] followed by the listener attachment, so any cloneNode-inherited value is always cleared before re-wiring. The hoverWiredAttr idempotency guard is unnecessary inside Case C in any case: Case C only fires when td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr] is absent, and every exit path of Case C stamps that marker (C1 immediately, C2 after successful refetch), making it impossible for Case C to re-enter on the same physical DOM node — and therefore impossible for duplicate listeners to accumulate."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

entity type are sorted in their own sub-table AND the h3 header has already been used on the same final rendered page

So, e.g. take the "Composed" relationship. In theory different entity types could use the same sub-table name for rows
with different relatinships depending on the entity type.

#+end_example

#+begin_example
Now I have a complete understanding of the bug. Let me confirm the exact code section to fix:Architected concurrency fix
for image loading bottleneckArchitected concurrency fix for image loading bottleneckView the exact non-IDB bigbox load
block to fixNow I fully understand the bug and have the fix. Let me also look at the _artRetryTable to see if it
properly handles things after the fix.

The fix: in the non-IDB path of _artInitBigPics, instead of immediately setting img.src = imgurl, we need to enqueue the
load through _caaQueue. This prevents the browser from being overwhelmed with hundreds of simultaneous image requests.

The _caaQueue concurrency is sa_caa_fetch_concurrency (default 4), which handles small pics and enrichment. By sharing
this budget, bigbox images compete fairly with small pics.

I'm considering whether to use the same queue or create a separate higher-concurrency queue for bigbox images, but since
bigbox is typically just one per row like the small pics, sharing the same queue should work fine. Now I'm implementing
the fix by wrapping the image load in the queue instead of s
#+end_example

#+begin_example
Now I see the full picture. _artFetchCachedImage does NOT go through _caaQueue - it does its own network fetching via _artGmFetchBlob (GM_xmlhttpRequest). So the IDB path for bigbox also bypasses the queue.
But in the IDB case, the network fetch uses _artGmFetchBlob which might have different characteristics than img.src. And for IDB mode, the user would typically have images cached in memory (session Map), so it would be instant. For the non-IDB path, we're using raw img.src.
The real issue is that when IDB is disabled, _artInitBigPics synchronously sets img.src for every bigbox image in a tight loop, triggering hundreds of concurrent requests to coverartarchive.org. On pages with many sub-tables, this can easily exceed 500 simultaneous requests, overwhelming the browser and causing many to fail silently without firing load or error events.
The solution is to queue these image loads through _caaQueue instead, the same throttling mechanism already used for small icons, so requests are processed concurrently but within reasonable limits. I need to modify the non-IDB fallback path to push each image load onto the queue rather than setting img.src directly.
Wrapping the load/error handlers... I'm seeing the error handling for the IDB fetch path and considering whether the same queue-throttling issue affects it too. The IDB path has three tiers of performance—memory cache, IndexedDB lookup, and network fallback via GM_xhr—but since the reported bug is specifically about the non-IDB path and uses a different network mechanism, I'll focus on fixing just the immediate issue for now.Show less
#+end_example

Root Cause Summary:
In _artInitBigPics, the non-IDB path (lines 22510-22518) fires img.src = imgurl immediately in a synchronous forEach loop for every image across ALL sub-tables. With an artist page having 31 sub-tables and hundreds of images, this creates a burst of 500+ simultaneous HTTP requests to coverartarchive.org. The browser cannot handle all of these, and many fire img.onerror silently (the error handler removes the wrapper without logging), resulting in only a fraction of images being shown.
The IDB path also bypasses the queue, but since that's not the reported issue and uses GM_xmlhttpRequest which might behave differently, I should focus on fixing the non-IDB path first. Though I should double-check whether the IDB bigbox path needs the same fix.
Looking at the IDB code, _artFetchCachedImage uses GM_xmlhttpRequest for network requests, which is throttled differently than regular img.src assignments, and images from IDB are usually served from the session memory Map anyway so the burst loading doesn't matter there.
The real problem is in the non-IDB case where I need to wrap the img.src assignment in a queue task, similar to how inline-pics handles it. So I'll modify _artInitBigPics to queue the image loading...

Now I understand the issue clearly and have all the context I need for the fix. Let me also check the IDB path for bigbox - should we also route it through _caaQueue?
For the IDB path:

_artFetchCachedImage is called directly (not through _caaQueue)
Tier 1 (memory) is instant - no issue
Tier 2 (IDB) is fast local DB lookup - no issue
Tier 3 (GM_xhr network) - this COULD also be a burst, but:

GM_xmlhttpRequest has different characteristics from img.src
It uses the Tampermonkey/Greasemonkey background request mechanism
The user hasn't reported an issue with IDB mode
Adding queue routing to the IDB path is a separate concern



I should focus on fixing the non-IDB path first, but I'll also add queue routing to the IDB bigbox path since it has the same potential burst issue. Looking at the IDB path implementation, I can see where the cached image fetch is triggered and how it chains through the promise handlers.
The IDB path bypasses the queue entirely when enabled, calling the fetch directly for each image. Since the user only reported the bug with IDB disabled and GM_xmlhttpRequest has its own built-in queuing at the browser level, the concurrent burst problem is less severe there, but I should still apply the same queue routing for consistency. let me also wrap the IDB bigbox path in _caaQueue.
Actually, let me be careful here. The IDB path for bigbox fires _artFetchCachedImage for EVERY image in ALL sub-tables in one pass. That's hundreds of GM_xhr requests fired simultaneously. This could be the same issue for IDB mode.
But the user said "non-IDB mode" is where the bug is. Let me focus on that for now and not change the IDB path, to minimize the diff.
The Fix:
I need to route the fallback image loading through _caaQueue so bigbox requests share the same concurrency throttle as small-pic icon loads. Without queuing, _artInitBigPics fires img.src for every image in every sub-table in a single synchronous pass, creating a burst of hundreds of

For the IDB path:

_artFetchCachedImage is called directly (not through _caaQueue)
Tier 1 (memory) is instant - no issue
Tier 2 (IDB) is fast local DB lookup - no issue

It uses the Tampermonkey/Greasemonkey background request mechanism

I should focus on fixing the non-IDB path first, but I'll also add queue routing to the IDB bigbox path since it has the same potential burst issue. Looking at the IDB path implementation, I can see where the cached image fetch is triggered and how it chains through the promise handlers.
The IDB path bypasses the queue entirely when enabled, calling the fetch directly for each image. Since the user only reported the bug with IDB disabled and GM_xmlhttpRequest has its own built-in queuing at the browser level, the concurrent burst problem is less severe there, but I should still apply the same queue routing for consistency. let me also wrap the IDB bigbox path in _caaQueue.
Actually, let me be careful here. The IDB path for bigbox fires _artFetchCachedImage for EVERY image in ALL sub-tables in one pass. That's hundreds of GM_xhr requests fired simultaneously. This could be the same issue for IDB mode.
But the user said "non-IDB mode" is where the bug is. Let me focus on that for now and not change the IDB path, to minimize the diff.
The Fix:
I need to route the fallback image loading through _caaQueue so bigbox requests share the same concurrency throttle as small-pic icon loads. Without queuing, _artInitBigPics fires img.src for every image in every sub-table in a single synchronous pass, creating a burst of hundreds of simultaneous requests to coverartarchive.org. The browser silently drops or rate-limits the excess, which is why img.onerror fires with no log.
Continuing with the fix... Finishing the queue logic...
I'm realizing the IDB path has different timing characteristics — memory and IDB hits are instant, but network requests all fire at once. Since the user only reported issues with sa_art_idb_enable: false, and GM_xhr might handle queuing differently than img.src, I'll leave that path alone to keep the fix focused.
But looking at how _artInitSmallPics wraps both IDB and non-IDB paths through _caaQueue, I should probably apply the same consistency here and route the IDB big pics through the queue as well to prevent the same simultaneous request problem.
Writing the IDB fix...
Now I'm documenting the bug for the changelog: the artist-releasegroups page was making unthrottled requests for all bigbox images simultaneously when IDB was disabled, causing the browser to drop excess requests and only load a subset of images per sub-table, which is why the CAA toggle showed the wrong count.Show less


Root cause: In _artInitBigPics, the non-IDB path fires img.src = imgurl for every single bigbox image across all
sub-tables in one synchronous loop. With 31 sub-tables on this artist page, that's a burst of 500+ simultaneous HTTP
requests to coverartarchive.org. The browser silently drops or fails the excess (the img.onerror handler removes
wrappers without logging), so only the first ~12 images from table 1 loaded before the burst saturated the browser. The
small-pic path works correctly because it uses _caaQueue (concurrency=4), exactly as the inline-pics path does. The
bigbox path needs the same treatment.



#+begin_example
Root cause
_artInitBigPics fires all bigbox image requests simultaneously in a single synchronous forEach loop. On an artist page with 31 sub-tables this produces a burst of 500+ concurrent HTTP requests to coverartarchive.org. The browser silently drops or rate-limits the excess. Because the img.onerror handler removes the wrapper from the bigbox without any log message, those images vanish silently — leaving only the ~12 that survived the initial burst visible in the strip and counted in the badge.
The small-pic icon column and inline Title-column thumbnails are unaffected because both already route img.src through _caaQueue (concurrency=4). The bigbox path was the only one that didn't. The retry button "worked" because it rebuilt the bigbox from scratch after the burst had subsided.
What was confirmed from the debug log
ObservableMeaningcaaInitBigPics: built bigbox for table 1 with 45 image(s)45 img.src fired simultaneously (along with hundreds more from other tables)Only 12 of those 45 fire caaInitBigPics: loaded OK in the initial windowBrowser dropped the restAll 45 corresponding caaLoadIcon: loaded OK fire at 10:48:16–20Same images succeed fine through the throttled queuecaaRetryTable: starting forced reload for table 1 → all 45 load at 11:55:17Rebuild with fresh DOM + smaller in-flight count = success
The fix (v9.99.154)
Both the non-IDB (img.src) and the IDB (_artFetchCachedImage) bigbox fetch paths are now wrapped in a _loadBigImg / _loadBigImgIdb task function and routed through _caaQueue.enqueue(...), exactly mirroring the pattern already used by _artInitSmallPics and _artInitInlinePics. The resolve() call on both load and error lets the queue correctly proceed to the next item.Showallentitydata user  1
#+end_example

* DONE NOT using indexedDB - fixes for CAA/Title columns when loading from disk - https://musicbrainz.org/artist/b3c01c39-429c-4b1e-8ad4-c7b04b601ca4 - Artist-RGs
** initial

There are a couple of bugs when NOT using indexedDB (sa_art_idb_enable: false) e.g. on the page "multi"-table pageType
https://musicbrainz.org/artist/b3c01c39-429c-4b1e-8ad4-c7b04b601ca4 and reloading daat from the serialized state from
disk

In the following list are the steps to reproduce

1.)

after the final page is rendered and all CAA images have been loaded from the network (so this path works OK), when I
first uncollapse the big-picture stripe, and on some rows toggle the display of all CAA art images in the multi-cell
"CAA" data column, and now use the "Save to Disk" button, the whole display state seems to be serialzed to disk in it's current form

2.)

when loading in the same session from disk, the multi-row toggle icon

#+begin_example
<span class="mb-cell-collapse-toggle" title="Show all 6 image(s) — click to expand" 
#+end_example

is gone, and also does NOT rerender even when cklicking the button

#+begin_example
<button id="mb-caa-toggle-btn-retry-1" type="button" title="Retry loading all CAA cover art images for this sub-table (forces network reload)"
#+end_example

3.)

when loading in the same session from disk the previously uncollapsed artwork icons in multi-row cells show up (they are
rendered after the disk load) but are NOT hoverable

4.)

when loading in the same session from disk and previously before saving, if I also had uncollapsed with the following button

#+begin_example
<span data-erg-btn="1" title="Toggle display of underlying entity page" style="cursor: pointer; margin-right: 4px; color: rgb(119, 119, 119);">▶</span>
#+end_example

the display of the underlying entity page, it will be RERENDERED again as a ghost entry (after loadinf from disk again),
BUT because you can't uncollapse it again for example. Additionally a new indpendant '<span data-erg-btn="1" title="Toggle display of underlying entity page"' icon
is rendered anyway in the data cells

5.)

even when loading in a NEW session from disk, the same happens

** thinking

#+begin_example
Let me now implement all the fixes. I need to:

Fix getCleanCellHtml to strip transient DOM state before serialization:

Strip data-caa-enriched, data-caa-multi-built, data-eaa-enriched, data-eaa-multi-built from anchors/cells
Remove expanded ERG tables (appended dynamically) and reset ERG button state
Strip background-image from icon spans (they'll be re-fetched)
Strip inline-pic placeholders with blob: URLs (they'll be re-fetched)


Fix the inline-pic hover bug for expanded li items (though this may already be handled by the IDB/non-IDB inline pics path)
Fix the artwork icon selector - the current selector only targets icons inside cover-art links within table cells, but I need to also capture the icons nested inside the expanded li items that get dynamically added. I see the issue now - bug 3 is about the image rows that get rendered after loading, but they're not hoverable because _artLoadIcon only sets up hover previews for the span.caa-icon elements in the summary row, not the actual <img> elements inside the multi-row cells that _artBuildImageLi creates. If enrichedAttr is blocking re-enrichment, then those image rows never get their hover listeners attached in the first place.
#+end_example

#+begin_example
All five bugs share a single root cause: getCleanCellHtml() only stripped filter-highlight spans; it left all transient JS-only state in the serialized HTML. On disk-load this dead state blocked every feature from re-initialising.
Precise cause of each bug
#BugCause in serialized HTML1mb-cell-collapse-toggle gone, retry does nothingdata-caa-enriched on art anchors → _artEnrichIcon returns immediately → _artBuildMultiRowArtCell never called → no toggle built, no delegation wired2Expanded artwork icons not hoverableSame blockage — _artBuildImageLi never called → no mouseenter/mouseleave on per-image <img> elements3Multi-row cells still expanded after restoreli.mb-caa-art-li-image items saved with display:"" (expanded state) instead of display:none; mb-cell-collapse-toggle saved as ◀4&5ERG ghost sub-table + dead new button (same/new session)The ERG inline <table> (no class/id, just style="marginTop:1em…") was serialized as cell content; on reload initExpandRGsFeature injected a fresh button with its own fresh empty table in its closure, but couldn't remove the ghost
The fix (v9.99.155)
getCleanCellHtml() now performs 11 cleanup steps on the cloned cell before returning innerHTML: strips enrichment/built/done dataset markers; resets expanded art li items to display:none; resets ◀ toggles to ▶; removes background-image from icon spans; removes inline-ph placeholder spans; unwraps cache-hint overlays; resets ERG button glyph ▼→▶; removes direct-child <table> elements (ERG ghost tables); and strips data-erg-injected.
#+end_example

** final fix + explanations

#+begin_example
Both files delivered — v9.99.155 with all five disk-load restore bugs fixed. Here's a summary of the complete change:
Single fix location: getCleanCellHtml() — the one function called for every cell during saveTableDataToDisk(). It now performs these 11 cleanup steps on the cloned <td> before serializing its innerHTML:

Strip filter-highlight spans (pre-existing)
Delete data-caa-enriched / data-eaa-enriched from art anchors — unblocks _artEnrichIcon on restore
Delete data-caa-multi-built / data-eaa-multi-built from <td> — unblocks _artBuildMultiRowArtCell
Delete data-caa-inline-done / data-eaa-inline-done — unblocks _artInitInlinePics
Reset li.mb-caa-art-li-image to display:none — collapse expanded multi-row art rows
Reset ◀ toggle → ▶ with matching title/aria — match the collapsed li state
Strip background-image from caa-icon/eaa-icon/artwork-icon spans — dead on reload, re-fetched anyway
Remove .mb-caa-inline-ph / .mb-eaa-inline-ph spans — contain session-scoped blob: URLs
Unwrap .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap and remove .mb-art-cache-hint-big / .mb-art-cache-hint-inline — rebuilt on each render
Reset [data-erg-btn] glyph ▼→▶ — matches the stripped sub-table state
Remove direct-child <table> elements and strip data-erg-injected — eliminates the ERG ghost sub-table that could never be removed by the newly injected fresh button
#+end_example

** *.archive.org

a couple of issues:

1.)

the button "Save to Disk" popsup a browser alert dialog which says "Your are about to leave this page". Please exlude
this specific button click inside the function

#+begin_example
function initNavigationGuard() {
#+end_example
  
to NOT produce this alert, as it actually dos NOT leave the page

2.)

the following two @connect headers must also be set, because coverartarchive.org redirects to archive.org which in turn
redirects to arbitrary cache server which ends in *.archive.org

#+begin_example
// @connect      archive.org
// @connect      *.archive.org
#+end_example

rename "textContent" and "title" values + documentation strings in the following code block from

#+begin_example
                // Single toggle button that alternates between "Show all sections"
                // and "Hide all sections".  Starts in "Show all" state (all sub-tables
                // are initially collapsed so "Show" is the natural first action).
                const masterToggle = document.createElement('button');
                masterToggle.className  = 'mb-master-toggle';
                masterToggle.type       = 'button';
                masterToggle.dataset.state = 'collapsed'; // 'collapsed' → click shows; 'expanded' → click hides
                masterToggle.textContent = 'Show all sections';
                masterToggle.title       = 'Show all sub-tables';

                const _updateMasterToggleState = (expanded) => {
                    masterToggle.dataset.state = expanded ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed';
                    masterToggle.textContent   = expanded ? 'Hide all sections' : 'Show all sections';
                    masterToggle.title         = expanded ? 'Hide all sub-tables' : 'Show all sub-tables';
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
                // Single toggle button that alternates between "Show all sub-sections"
                // and "Hide all sub-sections".  Starts in "Show all" state (all sub-tables
                // are initially collapsed so "Show" is the natural first action).
                const masterToggle = document.createElement('button');
                masterToggle.className  = 'mb-master-toggle';
                masterToggle.type       = 'button';
                masterToggle.dataset.state = 'collapsed'; // 'collapsed' → click shows; 'expanded' → click hides
                masterToggle.textContent = 'Show all sub-sections';
                masterToggle.title       = 'Click to uncollapse all sub-sections (Ctrl+Click to uncollapse ALL sub-sections)';

                const _updateMasterToggleState = (expanded) => {
                    masterToggle.dataset.state = expanded ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed';
                    masterToggle.textContent   = expanded ? 'Hide all sub-sections' : 'Show all sub-sections';
                    masterToggle.title         = expanded ? 'Click to collapse all sub-sections (Ctrl+Click to collapse ALL sub-sections)' : 'Show all sub-sections';
#+end_example

instead of the following (these are the current HTML fragments for a "CAA" column, the '[artwork thumbnail] ▶' and '[artwork thumbnail] ▼' parts are missing on non-CAA columns)

#+begin_example
<th data-col-name="CAA" style="background-color: rgb(184, 200, 184); cursor: default; position: relative; min-width: 28px;"><div class="mb-col-hdr-flex"><span class="sort-icon-btn" title="Restore original sort order (clears multi-sort columns)">⇅</span> CAA <span class="sort-icon-btn" title="Sort ascending — Ctrl+Click to add to multi-column sort">▲</span><span class="sort-icon-btn" title="Sort descending — Ctrl+Click to add to multi-column sort">▼</span><span class="mb-caa-col-hdr-btn" role="button" data-caa-col-hdr-state="collapsed" title="Show all CAA images" aria-expanded="false"><img alt="" src="//coverartarchive.orghttps://musicbrainz.org/release/d1f6e86d-66d4-4760-9e59-8a9676ee3a06/front-250" style="width: 16px; height: 16px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 2px; vertical-align: middle; display: inline-block;"><span>▶</span></span><span class="mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn" data-col-index="5" title="Expand all multi-row CAA image cells in this table column" role="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Expand all collapsed cells in: CAA" style="margin-left: auto;">▶</span><span class="mb-col-uniq-btn" title="Show unique values for this column — Click or press Enter on an entry to apply as filter for this column" tabindex="0" role="button" aria-label="Unique values for CAA">📊</span></div><div class="column-resizer" style="position: absolute; right: 0px; top: 0px; width: 8px; height: 100%; cursor: col-resize; user-select: none; z-index: 1; background: transparent;"></div></th>

                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['EAA'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Location', extractor: 'splitLocation', syntheticColumns: ['Place', 'Area', 'Country'] }
                ],
                collapsableColumns: [ 'Artists', 'Location', 'EAA' ],
                addEAA: 'Event',
                extractMainColumn: 'Event'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'artist-events',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/events'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Events for Artist' } ],
            features: {
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'cancelledEvent', syntheticColumns: ['Cancelled'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['EAA'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Location', extractor: 'splitLocation', syntheticColumns: ['Place', 'Area', 'Country'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'primaryAlias', syntheticColumns: ['Primary Alias'] }
                ],
                collapsableColumns: [ 'Location', 'EAA' ],
                addEAA: 'Event',
                extractMainColumn: 'Event'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        }
#+end_example

* DONE OBSOLETE: split artist releasegroup pages in official (h2 header name "Official Discography") and non-official (h2 header name "Non-Official Discography")
** initial

given the fact that the following action button (also it's labeled '🧮 Non-official RGs')

#+begin_example
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Non-official RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
            ],
#+end_example

produces on the final rendered page, both the "official" and "non-offical" sub-tables with the different types of releases (like "Album", "Album + Live", etc)

and the following action button 

#+begin_example
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Official RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
            ],
#+end_example

actually produces ONLY the "official" sub-tables.

Create a definition for a new action button with label '🧮 Artist RGs',

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Artist RGs', params: { all: ['1', '0'], va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Official RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Non-official RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Official VA RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '1' } },
                { label: '🧮 Non-official VA RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '1' } }
            ],
#+end_example

that when clicked iterates over the "all: ['1', '0']" array, so in effect downloads first both the "official" and
"non-offical" sub-tables (the are also rendered in the order) and thern 'subtracts' the sub-tables for the "official" releases.

Now 'subtracts' in this context means, we need to now where in the list of "all" sub-tables, the "non-official" ones
start. We can deduce this from the names of the sub-tables in the "official" releases download path, by comparing these names with the names from the "all" path (which fetches "official" and
"non-offical" sub-tables). As soon as we can't match the h3 header namees from these pathes, we know that the official sub-tables start.

When this happens a new h2 header with name "Non-Official Artist Discography" should be rendered before the sub-tables for the non-official case.
The original h2 header which preceeds the "official" sub-tables, should then be renamed from "Discography" to "Official Artist Discography"

** initial

given the fact that the following action button (also it's labeled '🧮 Non-official RGs')

#+begin_example
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Non-official RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
            ],
#+end_example

produces on the final rendered page, both the "official" and "non-offical" sub-tables with the different types of releases (like "Album", "Album + Live", etc)

and the following action button 

#+begin_example
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Official RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
            ],
#+end_example

actually produces ONLY the "official" sub-tables.

Create a definition for a new action button with label '🧮 Artist RGs',

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',
            // Root artist page (Official/Non-Official/VA views handled by specific buttons)
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Artist RGs', params: { all: ['1', '0'], va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Official RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Non-official RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Official VA RGs', params: { all: '0', va: '1' } },
                { label: '🧮 Non-official VA RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '1' } }
            ],
#+end_example

that when clicked iterates over the "all: ['1', '0']" array, so in effect downloads first both the "official" and
"non-offical" sub-tables (the are also rendered in the order) and thern 'subtracts' the sub-tables for the "official" releases.

Now 'subtracts' in this context means, we need to now where in the list of "all" sub-tables, the "non-official" ones
start. We can deduce this from the names of the sub-tables in the "official" releases download path, by comparing these names with the names from the "all" path (which fetches "official" and
"non-offical" sub-tables). As soon as we can't match the h3 header namees from these pathes, we know that the official sub-tables start.

When this happens a new h2 header with name "Non-Official Artist Discography" should be rendered before the sub-tables for the non-official case.
The original h2 header which preceeds the "official" sub-tables, should then be renamed from "Discography" to "Official Artist Discography"

│ …                                    │

Click to select, double-click to open for editing
Inline form (Add/Edit) with Apply/Discard
Alt+A Add · Alt+E Edit · Alt+R Remove · Alt+S Save · Alt+C/Escape Cancel
Unsaved-changes warning on Cancel/Escape
Draggable, resizable

Navigation flows: quick-filter ↓ → pin list → lru list; ↑ reverses; Escape from list → quick-filter; Escape with empty quick-filter → close panel.

        {
            type: 'artist-releasegroups',

            buttons: [
                { label: '🧮 Official RGs',        params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Official VA RGs',     params: { all: '0', va: '1' } },
                { label: '🧮 Artist RGs',          params: { all: '1', va: '0' } },
                { label: '🧮 Various Artists RGs', params: { all: '1', va: '1' } }
            ],
            features: {
                columnErasers: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Title', erasers: ['▶', 'jesus2099'] }
                ],
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['CAA'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'primaryAlias', syntheticColumns: ['Primary Alias'] }
                ],
                collapsableColumns: [ 'CAA' ],
                addCAA: 'Title',
                extractMainColumn: 'Title'
            },
            tableMode: 'multi' // native tables, h3 headers
        },

relocate the following block of code in the variable "const configSchema"

#+begin_example
        sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation_on_initial_page: {
            label: "Relocate H1 comment-span alias block (initial page)",
            type: "checkbox",
            default: true,
            description: "On the original MusicBrainz entity page (before any 'Show all' button is pressed), move the alias <i title='...'> block from the h1's <span class='comment'> into a dedicated italic line injected below the page title. Only affects release-group and event pages where MusicBrainz injects an alias marker directly inside the h1 element. Standard disambiguation comments (e.g. '(live)') that contain no <i title> are never touched."
        },

        sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation_on_final_page: {
            label: "Relocate H1 comment-span alias block (final rendered page)",
            type: "checkbox",
            default: true,
            description: "After the consolidated table has been rendered (i.e. after a 'Show all' button completes), re-apply the H1 comment-span relocation so the alias line remains visible below the page title on the final page view. Same structural rules as the initial-page variant: only acts when an <i title='...'> alias marker is present inside the h1's <span class='comment'>."
        },
#+end_example

for Org-Mode export change the comment symbol "#" to a "*" in front of each sub-table data org table block

3,)

even for "single"-table mode pageTypes add the pendant to what we do in "multi"-table mode (- for the JSON export each sub-table and it's data could be wrapped in a data strucure with the name of the sub-table derived from
  the h3 header

- for the Org-mode exporter, the name of the sub-table derived from the h3 header should be prepended as a comment in

* DONE on "multi"-table pageTypes when filtering a sub-table with the sub-table specific filter field, the h2 header line (which displays and summarizes row counts over all the sub-tables) correctly displays the corresponding changed total count
** initial

on "multi"-table pageTypes when filtering a sub-table with the sub-table specific filter field, the h2 header line
(which displays and summarizes row counts over all the sub-tables) correctly displays the corresponding changed total
count

But when clearing the sub-table filter field, the h2 header line count does not change, so it does NOT reflect the reset
of the sub-table filter field (only the sub-table row count is reflected in it's h3 header line). Filtering on a column
level filter field in a sub-table does NOT show this effect. After resetting a column level filter on a sub-table BOTH
counts in the sub-table h3 header line and the global count in the h2 header line are correct. please fix

** problem is

#+begin_example
Now I see the actual problem clearly. In clearSubFilter:

Line 18764-18767: rows have data-mb-stf-hidden deleted and their display restored
Line 18776: updateH2CountFromSubtables() is called
In updateH2CountFromSubtables, the new guard at line 18922:
jsif (!anySubtableFilterActive && !anyStfHiddenRows) return;After clearSubFilter runs, both anySubtableFilterActive is false (input cleared) AND anyStfHiddenRows is false (the delete row.dataset.mbStfHidden already ran on line 18766). So the function still returns early! The fix needs to bypass this check entirely when called from clearSubFilter.The cleanest fix: pass a {force: true} parameter to skip the guard, or simply always recalculate (remove the guard entirely and call updateH2Count unconditionally). The guard was only to avoid unnecessary work — it can be removed safely since the function is lightweight:Now let me also read the h2 relocation code for Bug 2:Viewed 2 files, ran a commandViewed 2 files, ran a commandNow I understand both bugs completely. Let me implement:Bug 1: The updateH2CountFromSubtables early-return guard is still wrong. When clearSubFilter calls it, mbStfHidden has already been deleted AND anySubtableFilterActive is false → returns early. Fix: remove the guard entirely. The function is called rarely (only on STF apply/clear) so the cost is negligible.Bug 2: After finalCleanup, find all h2 elements that come AFTER the data h2 (the one with .mb-row-count-stat) and move them to just before the data h2.Copy to working dirCopy to working dirFix Bug 1 — remove the guard entirely:
#+end_example

* DONE numeric columns "DD","MM", "YYY",  ... should be centered aligned in the data cells like currently the "Releases" column (actually "Releases" gets render by the MB stylesheet, it seems, it's already centered on the initial page)
** question

In the uploaded userscript, is it possible to align generally in a table a numeric column centered inside a data cell,
but still left or right align the numbers overall when rendering them. SO the whole colomn of numbers itself is lets say
right aligned to itself, but as a whole centered in the middle of the table column. If this is possible, how is such an aligment feature called ?

Technique: centered inline-block + right/left/centered-aligned (R/L/C) content

#+begin_example
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Year', align: 'C'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Releases', align: 'R'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Year', align: 'R'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Length', align: 'R'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Track'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Total Tracks', align: 'R'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: '#', align: 'L'} ],
#+end_example

** todo

add a new feature, based on the Technique: centered inline-block + right/left/centered-aligned (R/L/C) content

There is a new parameter "integerColumns" in the variable "const pageDefinitions" for different pageType definitions, e.g

#+begin_example
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ],
#+end_example

The implementation of "integerColumns" should be: during rendering of a table column which matches a "sourceColumn" from
the above definition, the "sourceColumn" itslef sould be centered-inline block in the table, but the cell content
aligement should be left 'L', right 'R' or centered 'C' (default). Also when "align:" is missing in the definition it
means centered 'C'

- maybe also rewrite the following function in terms of the new "integerColumns" feature
  
#+begin_example
        // --- Helper: is a column name numeric? ---------------------------------
        const isNumericCol = (name) =>
            name.includes('Year') || name.includes('Releases') || name.includes('Track') ||
            name.includes('Length') || name.includes('#');
#+end_example

** is it possible to align according a special character like ":"

is it possible to align according a special character like ":"

#+begin_example
 13:57.000
  9:59
 10:05
#+end_example

so that in each row the ":" character of each 'numeric string' sits exactly below each other

** align lenght

change the definition for

#+begin_example
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Length', align: 'R'} ],
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Length', align: ':'} ],
#+end_example

and 
Algorithm:
 - Scan column
 - Split at :
 - Compute max width of left part
 - Render with padded left side OR split spans

#+begin_example
Example: 13:57.000

Split each value into:

left part (before :) → right-aligned

right part (after :) → left-aligned

Example structure
<td class="time">
  <span class="left">13</span><span class="sep">:</span><span class="right">57.000</span>
</td>
#+end_example
   
This is exactly like “decimal alignment” but for the character ":"   

** align, in such a way that ":" should be centered inside the table column 

align, in such a way that the ":" character of a typical value like "10:05" is centered inline-block

some bugs

1.)

the "Length" column can actually also be in the following format: 

- "[<hour>:]<minutes>:<seconds>[.<thousends_of_a_second>]"

So there can optionally be a colon separated hour in front of the minutes, e.g.

<td data-mb-int-col-styled="1" style="text-align: center;"><span class="mb-ic-wrap" style="display: inline-block; white-space: nowrap;"><span class="mb-ic-left" style="display: inline-block; text-align: right; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; min-width: 2ch;">2</span><span class="mb-ic-sep">:</span><span class="mb-ic-right" style="display: inline-block; text-align: left; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;">02:05</span></span></td>
#+end_example
