3.)

please fix the placement of the following comment block, somehow it looks concatenated from different function decsriptions.

#+begin_example
    /**
    /**
     * initTreleasesObserver — installs a MutationObserver on every `table.tbl tbody`
     * in the document that removes `[class="treleases"]` elements the instant they
     * are inserted into the rendered table.
     *
     * Background: the jesus2099 "mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL Ⅱ X TURBO" userscript
     * injects a node such as:
     *   <… class="treleases" title="SUPER MIND CONTROL Ⅱ X TURBO">
     * into Length cells of the *live* rendered table — AFTER our render pipeline
     * has already finished.  Because our pass-1 (applyIntegerColumnStyling) runs
     * during row import (before the rows hit the DOM) and our pass-2
     * (finalizeColonAlignedColumns) runs just before render, neither pass can see
     * the annotation; the only reliable interception point is a MutationObserver
     * that fires synchronously as each node is added.
     *
     * The observer:
     *   - watches `childList` and `subtree` on each `table.tbl tbody`
     *   - for every added node, checks whether it or any of its descendants
     *     carries `class="treleases"` (exact match) — the attribute value used by
     *     the jesus2099 script
     *   - removes the offending element immediately, before the browser paints
     *
     * Called once after each `renderFinalTable` / `renderGroupedTable` completes.
     * A module-level `WeakSet` prevents double-observing the same tbody across
     * repeated re-renders.
     *
     * @returns {void}
     */
    /**
     * _repairTreleasesTd — re-styles a <td class="treleases"> in place.
     *
     * Jesus2099's "mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL Ⅱ X TURBO" mutates Length cells of
     * the live rendered table: it sets class="treleases", a plugin title,
     * `style="text-align:right"`, and replaces the text with a precise time
     * (e.g. "0:28.000").  The cell is NOT a new element — it is the existing
     * <td> mutated in place.  Removing it would shift every subsequent column.
     * Instead: extract the new text, strip Jesus2099's overrides, re-apply our
     * split/L/R/C alignment, and re-sync min-width across the whole column.
     *
     * @param {HTMLTableCellElement} td
     */
#+end_example

*** DONE create info popup

In the following function, after the line

#+begin_example
        Lib.info('export', `Exported ${rowsExported} data rows, skipped ${rowsSkipped} hidden rows`);
#+end_example

include code that pops up an info box which says that the enduser should monitor the browser downloading the file.
Include a "Close" button and also "Escape" key closing of the popup.


#+begin_example
    function exportTableToCSV() {
        const table = document.querySelector('table.tbl');
        if (!table) {
            alert('No table found to export');
            Lib.error('export', 'No table found for CSV export');
            return;
        }

        Lib.info('export', 'Starting CSV export...');

        const rows = [];
        let totalCells = 0;

        // Get headers from first row
        const headerRow = table.querySelector('thead tr:first-child');
        if (headerRow) {
            const headers = [];
            Array.from(headerRow.cells).forEach(cell => {
                // Skip hidden columns
                if (cell.style.display === 'none') return;

                // Clean header text (remove sort icons)
                let headerText = cell.textContent.replace(/[⇅▲▼]/g, '').trim();
                // Remove extra whitespace
                headerText = headerText.replace(/\s+/g, ' ');
                headers.push(headerText);
            });
            rows.push(headers);
            totalCells += headers.length;
            Lib.debug('export', `Exported ${headers.length} headers: ${headers.join(', ')}`);
        }

        // Get data rows (only visible ones)
        const dataRows = table.querySelectorAll('tbody tr');
        let rowsExported = 0;
        let rowsSkipped = 0;

        dataRows.forEach(row => {
            // Skip hidden rows (filtered out)
            if (row.style.display === 'none') {
                rowsSkipped++;
                return;
            }

            const cells = [];
            Array.from(row.cells).forEach((cell, index) => {
                // Skip hidden columns
                if (cell.style.display === 'none') return;

                // Get text content and clean it up
                let text = cell.textContent.trim();

                // Remove extra whitespace
                text = text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ');

                // Escape quotes (CSV standard: " becomes "")
                text = text.replace(/"/g, '""');

                // Wrap in quotes if contains comma, newline, or quote
                if (text.includes(',') || text.includes('\n') || text.includes('"')) {
                    text = `"${text}"`;
                }

                cells.push(text);
            });

            if (cells.length > 0) {
                rows.push(cells);
                totalCells += cells.length;
                rowsExported++;
            }
        });

        Lib.info('export', `Exported ${rowsExported} data rows, skipped ${rowsSkipped} hidden rows`);

        // Create CSV string
        const csv = rows.map(row => row.join(',')).join('\n');

        // Create Blob and download
        const blob = new Blob([csv], { type: 'text/csv;charset=utf-8;' });
        const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
        const link = document.createElement('a');
        link.href = url;

        // Generate filename with timestamp
        const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 19).replace(/:/g, '-');
        const pageName = pageType || 'table';
        const filename = `musicbrainz-${pageName}-${timestamp}.csv`;
        link.download = filename;

        // Trigger download
        link.click();

        // Cleanup
        URL.revokeObjectURL(url);

        // Update status
        const statusDisplay = document.getElementById('mb-status-display');
        if (statusDisplay) {
            statusDisplay.textContent = `✓ Exported ${rowsExported} rows to ${filename}`;
            statusDisplay.style.color = 'green';
        }

        Lib.info('export', `CSV export complete: ${filename} (${rowsExported} rows, ${totalCells} cells)`);
    }
#+end_example

Two bugs:

1.)

Actually the "description" parameters for the following two items are completely wrong

#+begin_example
        sa_ui_row_count_color: {
            label: 'Row-count stat: text color',
            type: 'color_picker',
            default: '#111111',
            description: 'Foreground (text) color of the (N of M) row-count stat span in H2/H3 headers. Default: dark black (#111111).'
        },

        sa_ui_row_count_bg: {
            label: 'Row-count stat: background color',
            type: 'color_picker',
            default: '#e8e8e8',
            description: 'Background color of the (N of M) row-count stat span in H2/H3 headers. Default: light grey (#e8e8e8).'
        },
#+end_example

These colors are for the foreground an background colors for the row counts in the h2/h3 row count tooltips. There they actually are woking OK.

2.)

The background colors of the row counters on the h2/h3 lines itself, should'n be touched at all, they should inherit the background colors of the h2/h3 lines itself

padding: 2px 6px; border: 2px solid orange; border-radius: 3px 0px 0px 3px; width: 500px; height: 24px; box-sizing: border-box; transition: box-shadow 0.2s; box-shadow: orange 0px 0px 2px 2px;"><span id="mb-global-filter-clear" title="Clear global filter" style="display: inline-flex; border-image: initial; border-left: none; border-right: none; background: rgb(250, 250, 250); transition: color 0.15s, background 0.15s;">✕</span><span id="mb-global-filter-drag" title="Drag to resize filter field" style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 10px; flex-shrink: 0; cursor: col-resize; user-select: none; border-top: 2px solid orange; border-right: 2px solid orange; border-bottom: 2px solid orange; border-image: initial; border-left: none; border-radius: 0px 3px 3px 0px; background: rgb(240, 240, 240); font-size: 7px; color: rgb(170, 170, 170); letter-spacing: -1px;">⋮</span></span><label id="mb-global-filter-case-label" title="Case Sensitive Filtering (ALL sub-tables)" style="font-size: 0.8em; color: rgb(51, 51, 51); cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 0px; user-select: none; font-weight: normal; height: 24px;"><input id="mb-global-filter-case-checkbox" type="checkbox" style="margin-right: 2px; vertical-align: middle;">Cc</label><label id="mb-global-filter-rx-label" title="RegExp Filtering (ALL sub-tables)" style="font-size: 0.8em; color: rgb(51, 51, 51); cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 0px; user-select: none; font-weight: normal; height: 24px;"><input id="mb-global-filter-rx-checkbox" type="checkbox" style="margin-right: 2px; vertical-align: middle;">Rx</label><label id="mb-global-filter-exclude-label" title="Exclude Matches (ALL sub-tables)" style="font-size: 0.8em; color: rgb(51, 51, 51); cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 0px; user-select: none; font-weight: normal; height: 24px;"><input id="mb-global-filter-exclude-checkbox" type="checkbox" style="margin-right: 2px; vertical-align: middle;">Ex</label><span id="mb-preload-filter-msg" style="font-size: 0.8em; color: red; margin-left: 4px; font-weight: bold; white-space: nowrap; display: none;"></span><button id="mb-toggle-prefilter-btn" title="Toggle prefilter highlighting" style="font-size: 0.8em; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgb(240, 240, 240); border: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); cursor: pointer; vertical-align: middle; transition: background-color 0.3s; display: none;">Prefilter</button><button id="mb-col-collapse-all-btn" title="Expand ALL collapsed multi-row cells in EVERY collapsable table column" style="font-size: 0.8em; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; background-image: initial; background-position: initial; background-size: initial; background-repeat: initial; background-attachment: initial; background-origin: initial; background-clip: initial; border: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); cursor: pointer; vertical-align: middle; align-items: center; gap: 4px; display: inline-flex;"><span style="align-self:center;font-size:1em;">▶</span><span style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;line-height:1;font-size:0.9em;"><span>Expand</span><span>ALL</span></span><span style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;line-height:1;font-size:0.9em;"><span>multi-row</span><span>cells</span></span></button><button id="mb-toggle-filter-highlight-btn" title="Toggle filter string highlighting for global filter, ALL sub-table filters and ALL sub-table column filters in ALL sub-tables (except the filter string when &quot;Loading from Disk&quot; was used)" style="font-size: 0.8em; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgb(240, 240, 240); border: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); cursor: pointer; vertical-align: middle; transition: background-color 0.3s; display: inline-block;">🎨 Toggle ALL highlighting</button><button id="mb-clear-column-filters-btn" title="Clear ALL column-specific filters in ALL sub-tables (triggered by 'Shift+Esc')" style="font-size: 0.8em; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgb(240, 240, 240); border: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); cursor: pointer; vertical-align: middle; display: none;"><span style="color: red; font-size: 1em; font-weight: bold;">✗ </span>Clear ALL COLUMN filters</button><button id="mb-clear-all-filters-btn" title="Clear global filter, ALL sub-table filters and ALL column filters in All sub-tables (triggered by 'Ctrl+Shift+G')" style="font-size: 0.8em; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgb(240, 240, 240); border: 1px solid rgb(204, 204, 204); cursor: pointer; vertical-align: middle; display: inline-block;"><span style="color: red; font-size: 1em; font-weight: bold;">✗ </span>Clear ALL filters</button><span id="mb-status-display" style="font-size: 0.9em; color: rgb(51, 51, 51); display: flex; align-items: center; height: 24px; font-weight: bold; gap: 4px;"><span id="mb-filter-status-display" class="mb-filter-status" style="color: green;">✓ Global filter [global:"6"]</span><span id="mb-sort-status-display" class="mb-sort-status"></span></span></span></h2>
#+end_example

** claude
*** initial

The '<div id="mb-stats-panel"' statistics panel needs a revamp. The current UI design should be followed. Please use as
much unicode symbols/glyphs wherever possible. Also use different fonts/styles/sizes where it makes sense. If too much
information is presented, make sure that the dialog is scollable and has a quickfilter at the top with highlightning
support for match filter strings.

1.)

Lets start with the "single" table pages (we will later add support for "multi"-table pages and specifically the
"multi"-table pages for the pageType "artist-releasegroups", which needs an even special treatment):

After the quick filter dialog, there should be global section which should be layed out in a table form like in the following ascci art (org-mode table graphics)

from each other by their configurable background color. Synthetic columns get their background color from the

which gets extracted by a "columnExtractor" function, flag it with a class "mb-extracted-column"

from the already extracted new colums, flag it with a class "mb-derived-extracted-column"

there are some bugs

1.)

#+begin_example

            label: 'Synthetic column header background color',
            type: 'color_picker',
            default: '#b8c8b8',
            description: 'Background color for synthetic table column-header cells (injected columns such as Event-Type, Event-Date, Country, Date, MB-Name, Comment, CAA, EAA, etc.). ' +

        },
#+end_example

please rename this variable (and adjust the label and description accordingly) to represent the variable for "extracted columns".
Then add a new background setting which represents the "derived columns" (it should have a slightly different color from the background color of the "extracted columns").

Use all these background colors (and also the background color for the standard columns, to colorize the rows in the "Table Details" sections inside the "Statistics" UI panel

#+begin_example
<tr data-stats-row="true" style="background: rgba(184, 200, 184, 0.333);"><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; text-align: center; white-space: nowrap;">2</td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; white-space: nowrap;"><strong style="color:#1565c0">Release</strong></td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; text-align: center; white-space: nowrap;"><span style="color:#388e3c">✔ yes</span></td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; text-align: center; white-space: nowrap;"><span style="color:#c62828">▼ desc</span></td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; text-align: right; white-space: nowrap;">64</td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; text-align: right; white-space: nowrap;"><span style="color:#bbb">0</span></td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; white-space: nowrap;"><span style="color:#bbb">—</span></td><td style="padding: 3px 6px; border: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; white-space: nowrap;"><span style="color:#bbb">default</span></td></tr>

** DONE CAA/EAA count wrong

A couple of artwork related fixes:

1.)

In the "Statistics" UI panel, rename the current

#+begin_example
"`🖼️ Total ${_artKey} front artwork count`"
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
"`🖼️ Total table rows with ${_artKey} artwork`"
#+end_example

it better describes what it's about.

2.)

There is some miscalculation going on with the current value of "`🖼️ Total ${_artKey} front artwork count`" (before it
gets renamed). The value shown is always the same as the total number of rows in the table, which is clearly wrong.

#+begin_example
            {
                // id used for live refresh from _showCaaCompletionToast when panel is open.
                stat: `🖼️ Total ${_artKey} front artwork count`,
                value: _totalCaaCount > 0
                    ? `<span style="color:${C.accent};font-weight:600">${_totalCaaCount.toLocaleString()}</span>`
                    : '<span id="mb-stats-art-count-val" style="color:#999">—</span>',
                comment: _totalCaaCount > 0
                    ? `Unique artwork links across ${subTblCount} sub-table${subTblCount > 1 ? 's' : ''}`
                    : `Counting ${_artKey} links… (updates when loading completes)`,
            },
#+end_example

Also the artwork count per sub-table (in the "multi"-table case) is wrong, eg.

#+begin_example
<td style="padding: 5px 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); border-right: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; text-align: right; white-space: nowrap;">🖼️ <span style="color:#1565c0;font-weight:700">100</span></td>
<td style="padding: 5px 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); border-right: 1px solid rgb(200, 230, 201); vertical-align: middle; font-size: 0.84em; color: rgb(85, 85, 85); text-align: right;"><span style="font-size:0.8em;color:#555">Filtered CAA artwork count</span></td>
#+end_example

It also is shown currently as the actual total number of rows per sub-table (well, at least the wrong counts per
sub-table when summed up are consistent with what is shown for the grand total)

The real artwork numbers per sub-table are not known until every table row is checked in the CoverArt Archive if there is artwork available (and then downloaded).

A better approach would probably be, to check if the stripe of big images per sub-table have been completely downloaded
and THEN display that count as the TOTAL number of CAA images per sub-table (which is then a fixed number which will not change again).

When filters are in effect on a sub-table, count the number of big images in the CAA stripe again and display that value
as the "Filtered CAA artwork count" per sub-table. Actually that number IS already calculated and displayed inside the
toggle CAA artwork button, e.g. "102" in the following example

#+begin_example
<button id="mb-caa-toggle-btn-global" type="button" class="mb-caa-art-toggle-btn mb-global-art-toggle-btn" title="Globally toggle CAA cover art images - Show" style="cursor: pointer; padding: 1px 5px 1px 3px; border: 1px solid rgb(170, 170, 170); border-radius: 3px; background: rgb(245, 245, 245); vertical-align: middle; font-size: 0.8em; margin-left: 5px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; line-height: 1; box-sizing: border-box; transition: transform 0.1s, box-shadow 0.1s;"><img class="mb-caa-toggle-thumb" src="https://coverartarchive.org/release/46e5c6a9-b0a7-4e42-9162-8f412705b09d/front-250" style="height: 18px; width: 18px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 2px; vertical-align: middle; display: block;"><span class="mb-caa-toggle-count">102</span></button>
#+end_example

whereas the total number of rows is always equal or greater then this number e.g. "104" for this example

#+begin_example
<span class="mb-row-count-stat" data-mbtt="There are &lt;span style=&quot;background:#e8e8e8;color:#111111;border-radius:2px;padding:0 3px;font-weight:bold;&quot;&gt;100&lt;/span&gt; rows total unfiltered in this sub-table">(100)</span>
#+end_example

** initial

In the case of running the page with the indexedDB "sa_art_idb_enable: true", I made sure that during initial rendering
of the page e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/3c39a076-8e52-38f2-8076-260d0672fb23 ALL the CAA images were
downloaded and cached in an indexedDB.

When I then start filtering, I would expect because of the "sa_rt_show_bigbox" and "sa_rt_show_inline" both TRUE, that
the cache icons on the images in the big images stripe above the table data and the inline images in the column
"Release" and "CAA" (here also the tooltip says consistenly "Timing unavailable") be either "🟢 memory cache" or "🟡 disk cache", but instead they consistently show up as "⚠️ no data"

I uploaded the debug log of a filtering action in the file "debugIndexedDB". Please explain and fix

OK, it now initially (when all CAA images are in the indexedDB) sets the cache hint icon in the "CAA" column to

#+end_example

sets the cache hint icon in the "CAA" column and the cache-hint overlay icon in the big picture image stripe correctly to 

#+begin_example

** bug with row count

There is a bug on "multi"-table pageTypes
e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/c497fc44-ddaf-3cce-a9b4-bfec958a0f3c regarding filtering on the sub-table
level and displaying the filtered row count in the h3 header line

In the sub-table "Promotion release", the total number of rows is 4. When specifiying the sub-table filter condition
"jp", I would expect 3 rows left (this is actually true, there are ONYL 3 rows dislayed in the sub-table) and the row
count in the h3 header line to be (3 of 4), but instead it renders as (4)

#+begin_example
<span class="mb-row-count-stat" data-mbtt="There are &lt;span style=&quot;background:#e8e8e8;color:#111111;border-radius:2px;padding:0 3px;font-weight:bold;&quot;&gt;3&lt;/span&gt; rows matching the &lt;span style=&quot;background:#90ee90;color:#000;border-radius:2px;padding:0 3px;font-style:italic;&quot;&gt;sub-table&lt;/span&gt; filter &lt;span class=&quot;mbtt-stf&quot;&gt;jp&lt;/span&gt; from the &lt;span style=&quot;background:#e8e8e8;color:#111111;border-radius:2px;padding:0 3px;font-weight:bold;&quot;&gt;4&lt;/span&gt; rows in this sub-table">(4)</span>
#+end_example

The interesting parts are:

- the tooltip for the "(4)" is actually OK, it says 3 out 4 are matching
- the big picture stripe correctly shows 3 artworks for the 3 rows displayed
- the "Statistics" panel correctly displays 3/4 filtered rows for this sub-table

Please fix..

when there is at least one front cover image in the cover art archive available for a release, e.g.

#+begin_example
<span class="mb-caa-inline-ph" style="display: inline-block; width: 20px; height: 20px; margin-right: 3px; vertical-align: middle; flex-shrink: 0; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 2px; cursor: crosshair; position: relative;" title="10 images found for this release / release-group">

  </span>
</span>
#+end_example

So you probably should be counting the number of cells in such a column, which have the "mb-art-cache-hint-inline" class, as that's when visually a front artwork thumbnail is rendered in the cell

- "NO front-image available"

when there is NO front cover image in the cover art archive available for a release, e.g.

#+begin_example
<span class="mb-caa-inline-ph" style="display: inline-block; width: 20px; height: 20px; margin-right: 3px; vertical-align: middle; flex-shrink: 0; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 2px;" title="1 image found for this release / release-group">
  <img alt="" style="width: 20px; height: 20px; object-fit: cover; display: none; border-radius: 2px;" src="//coverartarchive.org/release/4d2b4351-a374-442e-9349-685eee8be9ad/front-250">
</span>
#+end_example

So you probably should be counting the number of cells in such a column, which DO NOT have the "mb-art-cache-hint-inline" class, as that's when visually NO front artwork thumbnail is rendered in the cell

for "addEAA" columns there should be the following synthetic entries

when there is a poster image in the event art archive available for an event, e.g.

The fix is to inject an invisible sort-key span into cells in the addCAA/addEAA column whenever _artInitInlinePics loads (or fails to load) a thumbnail:Ran 2 commandsRan 2 commandsNow I'll add a sort-key class property to the CTX objects and inject the invisible span in _artInitInlinePics after each image loads:Ran a command, edited a fileRan a command, edited a fileThe correct fix is the same approach as the CAA/EAA column: add an invisible sort-key span (display:none) to each cell in the addCAA/addEAA column with text 'caa-inline-yes' / 'caa-inline-no' after the inline thumbnail loads. #+begin_example
Now I understand the exact pattern. The fix is to inject an invisible sort-key span into cells in the addCAA/addEAA
column whenever _artInitInlinePics loads (or fails to load) a thumbnail

I'll add a sort-key class property to the CTX objects and inject the invisible span in _artInitInlinePics after each image
loads:

The correct fix is the same approach as the CAA/EAA column: add an invisible sort-key span (display:none) to each cell in the addCAA/addEAA column with text
'caa-inline-yes' / 'caa-inline-no' after the inline thumbnail loads. Then makeInlineArtItem writes that exact text to
cfInp.value and calls runFilter() directly (no input event dispatch to avoid the prefix race). Since
getCleanColumnText() walks display:none spans, regular text matching handles everything — including Escape, ✕, and all
clear buttons — without any special bypass logic.

Now add a helper that injects/updates the sort-key span in the td, then wire it into every load path. First find the four load paths in _artInitInlinePics:Orchestrated integration of

Now wire _artSetInlineSortKey into every path in _artInitInlinePics.

I'll do targeted replacements at each stamp site to add _artSetInlineSortKey(ctx, td, true) for successful loads and _artSetInlineSortKey(ctx, td, false) for errors
#+end_example

when choosing one of it for filtering, NO row at all is shown. When clearing the filter from the synthtic value, at least ALL rows are shown again. Please fix

Your proposed fix is:

Root cause (v9.99.294 fix):

In the multi-table (addCAA) path runFilter() does:

#+begin_example
jsconst clone = r.cloneNode(true);
Array.from(clone.cells).forEach(td => _stripTransientCellState(td)); // ← stripped the span here
testRowMatch(clone, matchCtx); // ← span already gone → 0 matches
#+end_example


_stripTransientCellState was removing .mb-inline-art-sort-key from the clone before testRowMatch could read it via getCleanColumnText. The single-table EAA path was unaffected because renderFinalTable appends source rows directly without cloning them, so the span survived.

Fix: removed the el.querySelectorAll('.mb-inline-art-sort-key').forEach(sk => sk.remove()) line from _stripTransientCellState, replacing it with an explanatory comment. The span is idempotent — _artSetInlineSortKey updates it in-place after every fetch cycle, so no explicit removal is needed anywhere.

Please implement ..

** solution
* DONE changelog missing

Somehow the '"version": "9.99.294"' changelog is missing from the file "ShowAllEntityData_CHANGELOG.json"

And I think, you added again the fix from the previous session

#+begin_example
el.querySelectorAll('.mb-inline-art-sort-key').forEach(sk => sk.remove());
#+end_example

#+begin_example
@@ -25117,13 +25130,8 @@ a { color: #1565c0; }`;
 
         // Inline-thumbnail placeholder spans (_artInitInlinePics re-injects them)
         el.querySelectorAll('.mb-caa-inline-ph, .mb-eaa-inline-ph').forEach(ph => ph.remove());
-        // NOTE: .mb-inline-art-sort-key spans are intentionally NOT removed here.
-        // In the multi-table (addCAA) path runFilter() clones rows and calls
-        // _stripTransientCellState() before testRowMatch(); removing the sort-key span
-        // from the clone would cause getCleanColumnText() to see no synthetic value and
-        // match nothing, silently hiding all rows.  The span is idempotent —
-        // _artSetInlineSortKey() updates it in-place after every fetch cycle — so no
-        // explicit removal is ever needed.
+        // Sort-key spans for inline-art filter (_artSetInlineSortKey re-stamps them after fetch)
+        el.querySelectorAll('.mb-inline-art-sort-key').forEach(sk => sk.remove());
 
         // Cache-hint overlays (cosmetic, rebuilt on render)
         el.querySelectorAll('.mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap').forEach(wrap => wrap.remove());
#+end_example

It should probably NOT be there for the "_restoreArtExpandState" functionality

Please implement the following solution:

Root cause: The issue is a timing problem, not a TreeWalker issue:

1.) testRowMatch() calls highlightText() which injects mb-column-filter-highlight spans into the clone row's text nodes (including .mb-caa-type-badge and .mb-caa-art-comment).
2.) renderFinalTable() appends those highlighted clones to the DOM.
3.) Then initCaaPics() / initEaaPics() → _artEnrichTable() → _artEnrichIcon() → _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() rebuilds the entire <ul> structure inside the art <td>, creating fresh text nodes for type badges and comments — wiping all highlights.

Why EAA appears to work but CAA doesn't: Both get wiped. What you're seeing in the EAA example is a cell that already had data-eaa-multi-built="1" set on the source row — but _stripTransientCellState removes that on the clone, so it gets rebuilt. Both paths destroy highlights. The EAA example in your bug report showing a working highlight is likely from an IDB cache hit that fires near-synchronously before the DOM paint, while CAA network fetches are slower.

Fix plan: Module-level variable to hold the active highlight context:

#+begin_example
jslet _activeFilterHighlightCtx = null; // { globalQueryRaw, colFilters, isCaseSensitive, isRegExp }
#+end_example

Set it in runFilter() before the filter pass runs, clear it when no filters are active.

In _artBuildMultiRowArtCell(), after _artBuildImageLi() creates each li, call a new helper _artHighlightImageLi(li, colIdx) that reads _activeFilterHighlightCtx and applies highlightCrossTag for any matching global or column filter — naturally covering both .mb-caa-type-badge and .mb-caa-art-comment text nodes.

** fix

#+begin_example
Root cause & fix summary
The analysis in your bug report is exactly correct. Here are the 4 precise changes to make:

Change 1 — Add _activeFilterHighlightCtx module-level variable
Right after line let _caaQueue = null; (around line 27627), add:
javascript/**
 * Active filter highlight context, set by runFilter() before the filter pass
 * and cleared when no filters are active.
 *
 * _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() / _artHighlightImageLi() reads this to re-apply
 * highlights to freshly-built image <li> elements after the CAA/EAA art-cell
 * rebuild has wiped the marks that testRowMatch() + highlightText() placed on
 * the clone row.
 *
 * Shape:
 *   globalQueryRaw  {string}   — raw global filter string (pre-lowercasing)
 *   colFilters      {Array}    — output of getColFilters(); { val, idx } entries
 *   isCaseSensitive {boolean}
 *   isRegExp        {boolean}
 *
 * Null when no filter is active — _artHighlightImageLi() is a no-op in that case.
 *
 * @type {{ globalQueryRaw: string, colFilters: Array, isCaseSensitive: boolean,
 *          isRegExp: boolean }|null}
 */
let _activeFilterHighlightCtx = null;

Change 2 — Set/clear _activeFilterHighlightCtx in runFilter()
After the const matchCtx = { … } construction (around line 17507), before the let filteredArray line, add:
javascript// Publish the active highlight context so _artHighlightImageLi() can re-apply
// filter highlights to freshly-built CAA/EAA image <li> elements after
// _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() has wiped the marks placed by testRowMatch().
// colFilters starts as [] and is replaced per-table below; _activeFilterHighlightCtx
// is assigned a new object on each runFilter() call so per-table replacement is safe.
const _anyColFilter = () =>
    Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.mb-col-filter-input'))
        .some(inp => stripColFilterPrefix(inp.value) !== '');
if (globalQueryRaw || _anyColFilter()) {
    _activeFilterHighlightCtx = {
        globalQueryRaw,
        colFilters:      [],      // replaced at each getColFilters() call below
        isCaseSensitive,
        isRegExp
    };
} else {
    _activeFilterHighlightCtx = null;
}
Then in the multi-table loop, right after matchCtx.colFilters = getColFilters(…), add:
javascriptif (_activeFilterHighlightCtx) {
    _activeFilterHighlightCtx.colFilters = matchCtx.colFilters;
    _activeFilterHighlightCtx.isRegExp   = _colIsRegExp;
}
And in the single-table branch, right after matchCtx.colFilters = getColFilters(…), add:
javascriptif (_activeFilterHighlightCtx) {
    _activeFilterHighlightCtx.colFilters = matchCtx.colFilters;
}

Change 3 — Add _artHighlightImageLi() helper
Place this right before _artBuildImageLi (around line 28738):
javascript/**
 * Re-applies active filter highlights to a freshly-built image <li> element.
 *
 * Called by _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() after every _artBuildImageLi() call.
 * Because _artBuildMultiRowArtCell runs after testRowMatch() has already injected
 * mb-column-filter-highlight spans into the clone row (and then the DOM is rebuilt,
 * wiping those marks), we must re-highlight the new text nodes here using the
 * globally-stored _activeFilterHighlightCtx.
 *
 * Covers both .mb-caa-type-badge and .mb-caa-art-comment text nodes inside the li.
 *
 * @param {HTMLLIElement} li      - The image <li> produced by _artBuildImageLi().
 * @param {number}        colIdx  - Column index of the art cell in its parent row,
 *                                  used to match column-scoped filters.
 */
function _artHighlightImageLi(li, colIdx) {
    const ctx = _activeFilterHighlightCtx;
    if (!ctx) return;

    const flags = ctx.isCaseSensitive ? 'g' : 'gi';

    /**
     * Build a RegExp from a raw query string.
     * Returns null if the string is empty or the RegExp is invalid.
     *
     * @param {string}  raw
     * @param {boolean} isRegExp
     * @returns {RegExp|null}
     */
    const makeRegex = (raw, isRegExp) => {
        if (!raw) return null;
        try {
            return isRegExp
                ? new RegExp(raw, flags)
                : new RegExp(raw.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&'), flags);
        } catch (e) {
            return new RegExp(raw.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&'), flags);
        }
    };

    // Global query — applies to every column, so no colIdx check needed.
    const globalRegex = makeRegex(ctx.globalQueryRaw, ctx.isRegExp);
    if (globalRegex) {
        highlightCrossTag(li, globalRegex, 'mb-global-filter-highlight');
    }

    // Column-scoped filters — only apply when the filter targets this column.
    for (const f of ctx.colFilters) {
        if (f.isMultiRowFilter) continue;       // state filters have no text to mark
        if (f.idx !== colIdx)   continue;       // different column
        const colRegex = makeRegex(f.val, ctx.isRegExp);
        if (colRegex) {
            highlightCrossTag(li, colRegex, 'mb-column-filter-highlight');
        }
    }
}

Change 4 — Call _artHighlightImageLi() in _artBuildMultiRowArtCell()
The column index is needed. First, resolve it at the top of _artBuildMultiRowArtCell:
After function _artBuildMultiRowArtCell(ctx, artCell, images) { and the early return, add:
javascript// Resolve the column index of this art cell once — used by _artHighlightImageLi().
const _artColIdx = artCell.cellIndex;
Then in both the rebuild path and the first-build path, change the _artBuildImageLi calls:
Rebuild path (around line 28888):
javascript// Before:
images.forEach(img => existingUl.appendChild(_artBuildImageLi(img)));

// After:
images.forEach(img => {
    const imageLi = _artBuildImageLi(img);
    _artHighlightImageLi(imageLi, _artColIdx);
    existingUl.appendChild(imageLi);
});
First-build path (around line 28945):
javascript// Before:
images.forEach(img => ul.appendChild(_artBuildImageLi(img)));

// After:
images.forEach(img => {
    const imageLi = _artBuildImageLi(img);
    _artHighlightImageLi(imageLi, _artColIdx);
    ul.appendChild(imageLi);
});

Change 5 — Bump version and add changelog entry
@version header: 9.99.295+2026-03-23 → 9.99.296+2026-03-23
ShowAllEntityData_CHANGELOG.json — prepend a new entry:
json{
  "version": "9.99.296",
  "date": "2026-03-23",
  "sections": [
    {
      "label": "🐛 Bug Fixes",
      "items": [
        "CAA/EAA type-badge and art-comment filter highlights wiped on art-cell rebuild — both CAA and EAA columns lost all mb-column-filter-highlight / mb-global-filter-highlight marks the instant _artBuildMultiRowArtCell rebuilt the <ul>/<li> structure (which always happens after testRowMatch() has run, because initCaaPics/initEaaPics fire after renderFinalTable/renderGroupedTable). The EAA 'working' example in the original bug report was a rare IDB near-synchronous cache hit that painted before the highlight wipe — both archives were equally broken. Fix: added module-level _activeFilterHighlightCtx (set by runFilter() before the filter pass, updated per sub-table in multi-table mode, cleared when no filters are active). Added _artHighlightImageLi(li, colIdx) helper that reads _activeFilterHighlightCtx and calls highlightCrossTag for the global query and for any column filter whose idx matches the art cell. Called in _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() immediately after each _artBuildImageLi() call in both the first-build and rebuild paths. Covers .mb-caa-type-badge and .mb-caa-art-comment nodes in both CAA and EAA columns."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

These 5 changes are the complete, self-contained fix. The key insight is that _activeFilterHighlightCtx acts as a side-channel from runFilter() into _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() — since the art rebuild happens asynchronously after testRowMatch() returns, there's no way to pass the context through the call stack, so a module-level variable is the correct pattern (matching how _caaQueue itself is handled).
#+end_example

gets highlighted, you can see it DURING typing, BUT it immediately losses the highlightning again after typing

The bug is still present. No highlightning for the badge type or art comments.

This must be fixable. It has to do with the "multi"-table pageType URLs, because on "single"-table pageTypes its working
perfectly.

for series pages, we need to find the "type" of series from the h2 header name which precedes the table data. We then
must dynamically set the "Show all.." button text to that name

#+begin_example
Show all <h2 header name> for Series
#+end_example

Take into account for a section the big picture image stripe AND the actual table data after the stripe. Both can also be either in a collapsed or uncollapsed state. 
* DONE For https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f in the "Non-Official Discography" Ctrl-Click on the h2 header line, somehow shows in between the image stripes from the "Official Discography" view

In the uploaded userscript

for the URL https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f when the final page is rendered, the following happens:

- first click on the "Official Discography"-button and toggle all artwork images for all sub-tables, then wait until all
  images have been downloaded from the cover art archive

- then switch to the "Non-Official Discography". Wait until all images have been downloaded.

- then comes the bug: when "Ctrl-Click"-ing on one of the h2 header lines, somehow the image stripes from the "Official Discography" view
  are shown intermangled with the table data from the "Non-Official Discography"

Please explain and fix.

Is the following patch something which makes sense (lie numbers are probaly wrong) ?

                             }

-                            const prevH3 = node.previousElementSibling;
+                            const prevH3 = findH3ForTable(node);
                             if (prevH3 && prevH3.classList.contains('mb-toggle-h3')) {
                                 // Keep table hidden if its owning h3 is disc-view-hidden
                                 if (prevH3.dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true') {
#+end_example

And is the following analysis correct ?

Why images get “intermingled”

After switching views:

Official bigboxes still exist in DOM
Non-official tables get rendered
Ctrl-click expand restores tables, but:

👉 bigboxes are not re-bound to the correct table context

So you end up with:

[Bigbox from Official]
[Table from Non-Official]
💥 Root Cause (precise)

The h2 expand handler restores display blindly for content nodes but does not re-sync bigboxes with their owning table, nor remove stale ones.

Combined with:

bigboxes live outside the table structure
multiple render passes (Official → Non-Official)

👉 results in DOM mixing


OR, is this the better explanation:

This is a classic DOM-mutation race condition caused by the dynamic injection of the artwork image stripes.

Root Cause
The bug occurs in the makeH2sCollapsible() function, which handles the expand/collapse logic when you Ctrl+Click an h2 header.

When expanding, the script checks if a table's parent <h3> header is hidden by the discography view filter (e.g., hidden because it belongs to the "Official" view while you are in the "Non-Official" view). To find the parent <h3>, it uses const prevH3 = node.previousElementSibling;.

However, because you previously toggled the artwork images, the script injected a div.mb-caa-bigbox (the image stripe) immediately before the table. Consequently, node.previousElementSibling returns the bigbox <div> instead of the <h3>. Because the script fails to find the <h3>, it falls back to an else branch that unconditionally unhides the table (node.style.display = '';), completely ignoring the active view filter and exposing the "Official" tables intermingled with your "Non-Official" view.

The Fix: We need to replace the fragile previousElementSibling check with the script's built-in findH3ForTable() helper,
which correctly walks backwards past any injected image stripes to find the true <h3> header.

#+end_example

#+begin_example
Badge glyphs — Cs (blue, Case Sensitive), Re (purple, Regex), Ex (red, Exclude), shown only when active.
Keyboard flow:

Alt+H — toggle panel from anywhere in dialog
Quick-filter ↓ → moves to list; list ↑ past top → back to quick-filter
Enter — applies selected entry (query + all checkbox states), closes panel
Esc in list → quick-filter gets focus
Esc with quick-filter non-empty → clears it
Esc with quick-filter empty → closes panel (dialog stays open)
Esc with panel closed → closes dialog
#+end_example

** DONE solution
*** What changed

New shared factory: createFilterHistoryWidget(cfg) (inserted after setSubTableFilterBorder, before uiPrefilterInputCSS)
A single reusable function that builds the full Pin + History-toggle + dropdown panel for any filter input. It accepts a cfg object with filterInput, caseCheckbox, rxCheckbox, exCheckbox, onApply, and optional getQuery/setQuery/context overrides. Reads/writes the same lru-sa-hist-list and persistent-sa-hist-list GM storage keys as the Load dialog, so history is shared across all contexts. Returns { pinBtn, toggleBtn, dropdownAnchor, refreshDropdown, saveToLru }.

*** Global filter (filterWrapper / filterContainer area)

filterClear is now an absolutely-positioned <button> inside a new filterInputWrap span (position:relative), hidden when the field is empty, shown when there's typed content — matching the Load dialog style.
The drag-resize handle (filterDragHandle) remains as the flush right-edge ⋮ strip (unchanged behaviour, intact).
uiGlobalFilterInputCSS() updated: right padding increased to 28px to keep text clear of the ✕ button; border-right:none + border-radius trimmed on the right so the drag handle attaches flush.
A new gfHistAnchor span (containing pinBtn + dropdownAnchor) is appended to filterContainer between filterWrapper and the Cc/Rx/Ex labels.
Checkboxes are now created before the history widget construction so they can be captured by the closure.
_syncGfClearBtn is wired to the input event to keep the ✕ visible/hidden in sync.
setGlobalFilterBorder updated: no longer tries to sync a clearEl border strip (there isn't one anymore), only syncs the drag handle.

*** Sub-table filters (createSubTableFilterContainer)

Same ✕-inside-input redesign: stfInputWrap (mb-stf-input-wrap, position:relative) wraps the <input> and an absolutely-positioned clearBtn; drag handle gets class mb-stf-drag-handle for targeted border sync.
setSubTableFilterBorder updated to find the drag handle via .mb-stf-input-wrap → nextElementSibling.mb-stf-drag-handle instead of slicing wrap.children.
_stfHistWidget is built (same factory) and its pinBtn + dropdownAnchor are appended to container between filterWrapper and the Cc/Rx/Ex labels.
_syncStfClearBtn is wired to input events and called from updateInputStyle, the Escape handler, and the toggle-icon collapse path.
Old clearBtn click listener replaced: the new button wires directly to filterInput.value = '' → _syncStfClearBtn → dispatchEvent('input').
STF input event handler now also calls _syncStfClearBtn; Enter inside the STF input calls both applySubFilter() and _stfHistWidget.saveToLru().

** again

Please add 

#+begin_example
                try {
                    // If this page matches the current browser page and no specific overrides are requested, use the
                    // existing document instead of a redundant network fetch.
                    // For search pages the table is rendered by MusicBrainz JS after page load.
                    // Using the live `document` on first visit causes a race: the script reads the
                    // DOM before MusicBrainz has finished rendering, seeing either no table or
                    // the transient "does not contain enough information" error paragraph.
                    // Fix: always fetch search pages fresh from the network so the DOMParser
                    // receives a fully server-rendered response, regardless of whether p===1.
                    const _isSearchPage = activeDefinition && activeDefinition.type === 'search';
                    if (!_isSearchPage && p === currentPageNum && (!overrideParams || Object.keys(overrideParams).length === 0)) {
                        Lib.debug('fetch', `Page ${p} is current page. Using existing document.`);
                        doc = document;
#+end_example

Please also add a changelog

** DONE change the tooltip to a real HTML based tooltip

In the uploaded userscript

- for the the "Expanded cell thumbnail tooltip (_artBuildImageLi)", it should look like a 'botton'/'multiple " / " separated buttons' with a grey background and the "badge-type" as the button text

- change the big image tooltip to a real HTML based tooltip, which displays the artwork comment from '<span class="comment">' (inclusive the opening '(' and closing ')') in "italic"

** DONE solution not completely right

1. Type badge → button pills (_artBuildImageLi)
   
Each individual type string is now rendered as its own grey pill button (dark grey #c8c8c8 background, rounded corners, bold text, inline-block). Multi-type images like ["Front", "Back"] produce two separate pills with a literal / text separator between them — rather than the previous single span with joined text "Front / Back". A single type produces a single pill. The mb-caa-type-badge class is preserved on the outer wrapper span for backward compatibility with existing CSS/filter logic.

2. Bigbox strip HTML tooltip with italic comment (_artInitBigPics)
When a bigbox wrapper anchor's corresponding table row has a <span class="comment"> element, the wrapper now stores the entity name and full comment text (including the ( and ) parens exactly as found in the DOM) in data-art-tooltip-name / data-art-tooltip-comment. On mouseenter, a new singleton dark-panel div (#mb-art-bigbox-tooltip, styled to match the existing #mb-stat-tooltip) is positioned and shown with:

Entity name in normal weight on line 1
Comment text in italic on line 2 (e.g. (Pitman pressing))

A new _ensureArtBigboxTooltip() helper (modelled after _ensureArtHoverPreviewDiv) lazily creates this singleton. When no comment is present, behaviour is unchanged (plain wrapper.title only).

b.)

the HTML based tooltip should always be shown, not only when a "comment" is present

c.)

the plain NON-HTML tooltip, should NOT be shown anymore, ONLY the HTML based one

2.)

For the "Type badge → button pills (_artBuildImageLi)":

a.)

the current NON HTML based plain tooltip

#+end_example

in

** delivered the wrong changelog

#+begin_example
  {
    "version": "9.99.302",
    "date": "2026-03-24",
    "sections": [
      {
        "label": "📦 Feature",
        "items": [
          "_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): Enrich HTML tooltip with additional release row fields — Artist, Format (Tracks), Country/Date, Label (without comment) – Catalog#, Barcode — shown below the italic disambiguation comment when present.",
          "_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): HTML tooltip is now always shown (unconditionally), not only when a disambiguation comment is present.",
          "_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): Plain non-HTML wrapper.title is no longer set; the HTML tooltip is the sole tooltip mechanism.",
          "_artBuildImageLi (type badge pills): Replace plain img.title with an HTML tooltip on the <li> element, showing type pill labels in bold on line 1 and the italic comment (in parentheses) on line 2 when present."
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
#+end_example

** solution - needs to be generalized per pageType
*** Change 1 — _artInitBigPics bigbox strip tooltip

1a — Data collection (new _rowCellText helper + 7 extra dataset attributes):

A local helper _rowCellText(name) resolves a column header by name in the <thead> and returns the sibling cell's text
for the current row. It is used to collect: _tipArtist, _tipFormat, _tipTracks, _tipCD, _tipLabel (parens-stripped),
_tipCatNum, _tipBarcode. All are stored as data-art-tooltip-* attributes on the wrapper anchor, set only when non-empty.

1b — Always-on HTML tooltip: The guard was changed from if (wrapper.dataset.artTooltipComment) to if (wrapper.dataset.artTooltipName) so the HTML tooltip fires for every bigbox image, not only those with a disambiguation comment. artTooltipName is always populated.

1c — No more plain wrapper.title: Both the if (_commentForTooltip) { … } else { wrapper.title = … } branches were removed. The HTML tooltip is the sole mechanism.

1d — Extended mouseenter renderer now builds up to 7 rows:

Title (bold)
(italic comment) — when present
Artist — when present
Horizontal divider — when any detail field follows
Format (Tracks) — when Format present
Country/Date
Label – Catalog#
Barcode (muted colour)

*** Change 2 — _artBuildImageLi type-badge pill tooltip

2a — img.title = commentText ? typeText + ' – ' + commentText : typeText replaced with a comment explaining the HTML tooltip takes over.

2b — After the pending-approval block, li.dataset.liTooltipTypes and li.dataset.liTooltipComment are stored on the <li>, and mouseenter/mouseleave listeners are wired to _ensureArtBigboxTooltip() (the same shared singleton). The tooltip shows:

Type pill labels rendered as dark styled <span> tokens joined by / (bold)
(italic comment) — only when a comment is present

** some small fixes

some fixes and enhancements

1.)

for the "_artBuildImageLi (type badge pills)": always render the tooltip "top aligned" to the "right" of the big image
popup when hovering over with the mouse. The tooltip should render like in the case for "_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip)",
where the tooltip is also always "top right aligned" with the corresponding big image inside the artwork stripe.

2.)

For the "Data collection (new _rowCellText helper + 7 extra dataset attributes)" the extraction of the "extra dataset attributes" actually doesn't work,
although the actual entity title" + optional "comment" (to be used for the italic endering) extraction works fine.

But it doesn't really matter for now, as we need to generalize the whole column value extraction for rendering in the
big picture tooltip per pageType anyway:

a.)

Let's start with the two pageTypes "type: 'releasegroup-releases'" and "type: 'artist-releases'"

For both add a new tooltipColumns section

#+begin_example
tooltipColumns: [ 'Release' , 'Artist', '---', {'Format', '(', 'Tracks', ')'}, 'Country/Date', {'Label', '-', 'Catalog#'}, 'Barcode' ],
#+end_example

inside the pageType definition as follows, e.g for the pageTypes "type: 'releasegroup-releases'"

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'releasegroup-releases',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/release-group/'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Releases for ReleaseGroup' } ],
            features: {
                columnErasers: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release', erasers: ['▶'] }
                ],
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['CAA'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Country/Date', extractor: 'splitCountryDate', syntheticColumns: ['Country', 'Date'] },
                    {
                        sourceColumn:    'Tracks',
                        extractor:       'sumTracks',
                        syntheticColumns: ['Total Tracks']
                    },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Format', extractor: 'extractFormatTypes', syntheticColumns: ['Format Types'] }
                ],
                syntheticColumnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Date', extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['DD', 'MM', 'YYYY', 'Day', 'Month'] }
                ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'Total Tracks', align: 'R'} ],
                renderMultiRowCell: [ 'Label', 'Catalog#' ],
                collapsableColumns: [ 'Country/Date' ,'Country', 'Date', 'Label', 'Catalog#', 'CAA' ],
		tooltipColumns: [ 'Release' , 'Artist', '---', {'Format', '(', 'Tracks', ')'}, 'Country/Date', {'Label', '-', 'Catalog#'}, 'Barcode' ],
                addCAA: 'Release',
                extractMainColumn: 'Release'
            },
            tableMode: 'multi',
            non_paginated: false
        },
#+end_example

b.)

for the pageType "type: 'artist-recordings'" add a new tooltipColumns section of

#+begin_example
tooltipColumns: [ 'Release groups', 'Name' , 'Artist', '---', {'Length', '-', 'Video'}, 'ISRCs' ],
#+end_example

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'artist-recordings',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/recordings'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Recordings for Artist' } ],
            features: {
                columnErasers: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', erasers: ['▶', '➕', 'jesus2099'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Name', erasers: ['wiencek'] }
                ],
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Name', extractor: 'video', syntheticColumns: ['Video'] }
                ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Length', align: ':'} ],
                collapsableColumns: [ 'Release groups', 'CAA' ],
                tooltipColumns: [ 'Release groups', 'Name' , 'Artist', '---', {'Length', '-', 'Video'}, 'ISRCs' ],
                addCAA: 'Release groups',
                extractMainColumn: 'Name'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

c.)

When it comes to rendering the new "tooltipColumns" structure inside a tooltip, do the following

- render each comma separated column in "tooltipColumns" below each other
- except when there are multiple column names enclosed between "{" and "}", then render them inside the tooltip in the same line
- the following symbols have a special meaning:
  - '---': divider line (do NOT interpret as column name)
  - '(', ')', '-': render as is  (do NOT interpret as column name)
- when the column name in "tooltipColumns" matches the definition of "extractMainColumn" in the pageType, it's the column where the optional "disambiguation comment" should be extracted and be rendered in italic in brackets below the colum name

Here is an example for the pageType 'artist-recordings' with a typical row with the following column values:

#+begin_example
COLUMN_NAME: VALUE
Name: (4th of July, Asbury Park) Sandy (live, 1975‐09‐26: Hancher Auditorium, University of Iowa, Iowa City, IA, USA)   <=  (live, 1975‐09‐26: Hancher Auditorium, University of Iowa, Iowa City, IA, USA) is the disambiguation comment
Artist: Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band
ISRCs: US-UPV-11-00014
Lenght: 4:14
Release groups: The Best of Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Live
Video: [video icon]
#+end_example

It should be rendered like this

#+begin_example
The Best of Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Live
(4th of July, Asbury Park) Sandy
(live, 1975‐09‐26: Hancher Auditorium, University of Iowa, Iowa City, IA, USA)   <== in italic
---
4:14 - [video icon]
US-UPV-11-00014
#+end_example

** bugs

Actually nothing is being fixed:

1.)

Fix 1 — _artBuildImageLi li tooltip positioning DOESN't work at all, it's the same behaviour as before, but it's supposed to do the following:

The mouseenter handler now checks whether #mb-art-hover-preview is visible. If it is, the tooltip anchors top-right of that popup (matching the bigbox strip behaviour). If the popup is hidden it falls back to top-right of the <li> itself.

for the "_artBuildImageLi (type badge pills)": always render the tooltip "top aligned" to the "right" of the big image
popup when hovering over with the mouse. The tooltip should render like in the case for "_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip)",
where the tooltip is also always "top right aligned" with the corresponding big image inside the artwork stripe.

2.)

The tooltip in the big-picture stripe now shows for every picture in the stripe a black square (with rounded corners) top right aligend to the actual big image.

This is the HTML code e.g.

#+begin_example
<a href="/release/3ce46b79-5e8c-470a-bcdc-45f301d09f60" data-art-tooltip-name="Greetings From Asbury Park, N.J." data-art-tooltip-comment="(Pitman pressing)" data-caa-href="/release/3ce46b79-5e8c-470a-bcdc-45f301d09f60" style="display: inline-block; height: 100%; margin: 8px 8px 4px 4px; position: relative;">
  <img alt="Greetings From Asbury Park, N.J." style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline; max-height: 125px; box-shadow: black 1px 1px 4px;" src="//coverartarchive.org/release/3ce46b79-5e8c-470a-bcdc-45f301d09f60/front-250">
  <span class="mb-art-cache-hint-big" title="Network fetch (733 ms)" style="position: absolute; top: 2px; left: 2px; font-size: 0.8em; line-height: 1; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35); border-radius: 3px; padding: 1px 2px; user-select: none; cursor: default; pointer-events: none; z-index: 1;">🔵
  </span>
</a>
#+end_example

** bugs

some bugs need to be fixed:

1.)

on URLs of pageType  https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/c497fc44-ddaf-3cce-a9b4-bfec958a0f3c

the closing bracket aftert the "Tracks" column is missing

2.)

a.)

on URLs of pageType https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f/recordings 

there is a problem in the "ISRCs" column when there is NO value, then the following value is displayed in the tooltip

#+begin_example
{"isrcs":[]}
#+end_example

** missed changelog
** bugs

some bugs need to be fixed and some more pageTypes need to be supported:

1.)

the following "tooltipColumns" configuration should be changed (in two places in the configuration) from

#+begin_example
tooltipColumns: [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Artist', '---', ['Format', '(', 'Total Tracks', ')'], 'Country', 'Date', ['Label', '-', 'Catalog#'], 'Barcode' ],
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
tooltipColumns: [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Artist', '---', ['Format', '(', 'Tracks', ')'], 'Country/Date', ['Label', '-', 'Catalog#'], 'Barcode' ],
#+end_example

 - so the "Tracks" column instead of "Total Tracks" should be extracted
 - and instead of extracting the country and date information from the two "'Country', 'Date'" columns extract directly
   from the "Country/Date" column, see the following format

#+begin_example
<td>
  <script type="application/json">{"events":[{"date":{"day":21,"year":1974,"month":7},"country":{"iso_3166_2_codes":[],"entityType":"area","primaryAlias":"Japan","iso_3166_3_codes":[],"typeID":1,"name":"Japan","comment":"","gid":"2db42837-c832-3c27-b4a3-08198f75693c","editsPending":false,"last_updated":"2013-05-27T12:29:56Z","id":107,"ended":false,"iso_3166_1_codes":["JP"],"primary_code":"JP","end_date":null,"country_code":"JP","begin_date":null}}]}
  </script>
  <div class="release-events-container">
    <ul aria-label="Release events" class="release-events abbreviated">

	<span class="flag flag-JP release-country">

	    </abbr>
	  </a>
	</span>
	<span class="release-date">1974-07-21
	  <span class="mb-day-of-week non-standard" title="Expected Wednesday for Japan, but is Sunday." style="cursor: help;">Sun
	  </span>
	</span>
      </li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</td>
#+end_example

The MusicBrainz website renders the country flag "<span class="flag flag-JP release-country">" in the table data cells,
so because our tooltip is HTML based please render the complete information to the tooltips which is available in the
example

#+begin_example
[flag-icon] <abbr title> (<abbr> text) <class="release-date" text> <class="mb-day-of-week non-standard" text>
#+end_example

e.g.

#+begin_example
[flag-JP icon] Japan (JP) 1974-07-21 Sun
#+end_example

3.)

for series pages, we need to find the "type" of series from the h2 header name which precedes the table data. We then
must dynamically set the "Show all.." button text to that name, e.g

#+begin_example

#+end_example

As an example, on the series pageType https://musicbrainz.org/series/fab79f62-2fa5-4f85-9369-82c77811a290

the h2 header line name for the table section is called "Releases", as can be seen in the following HTML fragment

#+end_example

so the action button

</button>
#+end_example

on the final rendered page must have the title 

#+begin_example
Show all Releases for Series
#+end_example

4.)

for the URL https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f when the final page is rendered, the following happens:

- first click on the "Official Discography"-button and toggle all artwork images for all sub-tables, then wait until all
  images have been downloaded from the cover art archive

- then switch to the "Non-Official Discography". Wait until all images have been downloaded.

- then comes the bug: when "Ctrl-Click"-ing on one of the h2 header lines, somehow the image stripes from the "Official Discography" view
  are shown intermangled with the table data from the "Non-Official Discography"

Please explain and fix.

Is the following patch something which makes sense (lie numbers are probaly wrong) ?

#+begin_example
modified   claude/artTooltips/2/ShowAllEntityData_user.js
@@ -22324,7 +22324,7 @@ a { color: #1565c0; }`;
                                 node.style.display = '';
                             }
                         } else if (node.tagName === 'TABLE' && node.classList.contains('tbl')) {
-                            const prevH3 = node.previousElementSibling;
+                            const prevH3 = findH3ForTable(node);
                             if (prevH3 && prevH3.classList.contains('mb-toggle-h3')) {
                                 // Keep table hidden if its owning h3 is disc-view-hidden
                                 if (prevH3.dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true') {
#+end_example

And is the following analysis correct ?

Why images get “intermingled”

After switching views:

Official bigboxes still exist in DOM
Non-official tables get rendered
Ctrl-click expand restores tables, but:

👉 bigboxes are not re-bound to the correct table context

So you end up with:

[Bigbox from Official]
[Table from Non-Official]
💥 Root Cause (precise)

The h2 expand handler restores display blindly for content nodes but does not re-sync bigboxes with their owning table, nor remove stale ones.

Combined with:

bigboxes live outside the table structure
multiple render passes (Official → Non-Official)

👉 results in DOM mixing


OR, is this the better explanation:

This is a classic DOM-mutation race condition caused by the dynamic injection of the artwork image stripes.

Root Cause
The bug occurs in the makeH2sCollapsible() function, which handles the expand/collapse logic when you Ctrl+Click an h2 header.

When expanding, the script checks if a table's parent <h3> header is hidden by the discography view filter (e.g., hidden because it belongs to the "Official" view while you are in the "Non-Official" view). To find the parent <h3>, it uses const prevH3 = node.previousElementSibling;.

However, because you previously toggled the artwork images, the script injected a div.mb-caa-bigbox (the image stripe) immediately before the table. Consequently, node.previousElementSibling returns the bigbox <div> instead of the <h3>. Because the script fails to find the <h3>, it falls back to an else branch that unconditionally unhides the table (node.style.display = '';), completely ignoring the active view filter and exposing the "Official" tables intermingled with your "Non-Official" view.

The Fix: We need to replace the fragile previousElementSibling check with the script's built-in findH3ForTable() helper,
which correctly walks backwards past any injected image stripes to find the true <h3> header.

** DONE fix

a couple of fixes:

1.)

for the 'place-events' pageType https://musicbrainz.org/place/6a59a67c-fcc5-491f-949c-bfc45bc97463/events

a.)

add the following additional "extractor" definitions

#+begin_example
{ sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'cancelledEvent', syntheticColumns: ['Cancelled'] }
{ sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'primaryAlias', syntheticColumns: ['Primary Alias'] }
#+end_example

to "columnExtractors" in the following order

#+begin_example
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'cancelledEvent', syntheticColumns: ['Cancelled'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['EAA'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'primaryAlias', syntheticColumns: ['Primary Alias'] }
                ],
#+end_example

b.)

change the column display order (and add a new column 'Primary Alias') from

#+begin_example
tooltipColumns: [ 'Event', '---', 'Type', 'Artists', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled' ],
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
tooltipColumns: [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Primary Alias', 'Type', '---', 'Artists', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled' ],
#+end_example

2.)

for the 'artist-events' pageType https://musicbrainz.org/place/6a59a67c-fcc5-491f-949c-bfc45bc97463/events

on the final rendered page the displayed columns got shifted by one to the right in between the foolowing two rendered rows

#+begin_example
<tr class="odd" data-mb-row-idx="6499"><td>2010-12-29</td><td><span class="recordinglink"></span><span class="video" title="This recording is a video"></span><a href="/recording/b7497054-7492-4ea5-ac21-d3f9a77dd150"><bdi>The Question of U (instr.)</bdi></a> <span class="comment">(<bdi>live, 2010‐12‐29: Madison Square Garden, New York, NY, USA</bdi>)</span></td><td></td><td><bdi><a href="/artist/070d193a-845c-479f-980e-bef15710653e" title="Prince (“The Artist Formerly Known as…”)">Prince</a></bdi></td><td>?:??</td><td><a href="/recording/b7497054-7492-4ea5-ac21-d3f9a77dd150"><bdi>The Question of U (instr.)</bdi></a></td><td>live, 2010‐12‐29: Madison Square Garden, New York, NY, USA</td></tr>
<tr class="even" data-mb-row-idx="6500"><td>2010-12-29</td><td><span class="recordinglink"></span><span class="video" title="This recording is a video"></span><a href="/recording/74a592c7-6046-4c9b-b559-8df3234eda8d"><bdi>U Got the Look</bdi></a> <span class="comment">(<bdi>live, 2010‐12‐29: Madison Square Garden, New York, NY, USA</bdi>)</span></td><td></td><td></td><td><bdi><a href="/artist/070d193a-845c-479f-980e-bef15710653e" title="Prince (“The Artist Formerly Known as…”)">Prince</a></bdi></td><td>?:??</td><td><a href="/recording/74a592c7-6046-4c9b-b559-8df3234eda8d"><bdi>U Got the Look</bdi></a></td><td>live, 2010‐12‐29: Madison Square Garden, New York, NY, USA</td></tr>
#+end_example

What is the problem here, please explain and fix with a changelog entry

** solution

#+begin_example
// Inside the column cleanup logic
if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_release_events && pageType === 'place-performances') {
    // Force strict index-based removal to prevent shifting
    const releaseEventsIdx = Array.from(theadRow.cells).findIndex(th => 
        th.textContent.includes('Release events')
    );
    if (releaseEventsIdx !== -1) {
        // Remove from header and ensure all reconstructed rows skip this specific index
        removeColumnByIndex(releaseEventsIdx); 
    }
}
#+end_example

#+begin_example
{
  "version": "9.99.316",
  "date": "2026-03-27",
  "sections": [
    {
      "label": "🐛 Fix",
      "items": [
        "place/performances: Fixed a column shifting bug where rows became misaligned when the 'Release events' or 'Rating' columns contained empty cells. The removal logic now uses strict index-tracking to maintain structural integrity across all rows."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
#+end_example

** the real source code

#+begin_example
. Fix in ShowAllEntityData.user.js
Locate the cleanupHeaders or the loop inside the fetch process where columns are excluded. You need to modify the logic that handles the indicesToExclude.

Search for the indicesToExclude logic in the row processing loop (around the area where newRow cells are iterated):

JavaScript
// --- OLD CODE (Conceptual) ---
// table.querySelectorAll('tbody tr').forEach(row => {
//    indicesToExclude.forEach(idx => row.deleteCell(idx)); 
// });

// --- FIXED CODE ---
// Update the row processing loop to remove cells from right-to-left 
// to prevent index shifting during the removal process.
table.querySelectorAll('tbody tr:not(.explanation)').forEach(row => {
    if (row.cells.length > 1) {
        // Sort indices in descending order so removing index 5 
        // doesn't change the position of index 2.
        const sortedExclusions = [...indicesToExclude].sort((a, b) => b - a);
        
        sortedExclusions.forEach(idx => {
            if (row.cells[idx]) {
                row.deleteCell(idx);
            }
        });

        const newRow = document.importNode(row, true);
        // ... rest of the extraction logic
Additionally, ensure the sa_remove_release_events check specifically targets the column labeled "Release events" by name rather than a hardcoded index:

JavaScript
// Inside the header scanning section
const releaseEventsIdx = headerNames.findIndex(name => name.includes('Release events'));
if (Lib.settings.sa_remove_release_events && releaseEventsIdx !== -1) {
    indicesToExclude.push(releaseEventsIdx);
    Lib.debug('cleanup', `Flagging "Release events" at index ${releaseEventsIdx} for removal`);
}
2. Update ShowAllEntityData_CHANGELOG.json
Add the following entry to the top of your changelog file to document the fix:

JSON
  {
    "version": "9.99.316",
    "date": "2026-03-27",
    "sections": [
      {
        "label": "🐛 Fix",
        "items": [
          "place/performances: Fixed column shifting issue caused by 'Release events' column removal. The script now uses a descending-order deletion strategy to ensure row data remains aligned with headers even when optional columns are present."
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
#+end_example

3.)

extract from the following code block the functionality to query the "releaseEvents" information in a new function
"extractReleaseEvents" and use it in our userscript

#+begin_example
	// ## RELEASE_EVENT_COLUMN ## requested by Lotheric https://github.com/jesus2099/konami-command/issues/132
	// =========================================================================
	if (
		j2sets.RECORDING_LENGTH_COLUMN && (enttype == "work" && location.pathname.match(new RegExp("^/work/" + stre_GUID + "$")) || enttype == "artist" && location.pathname.match(new RegExp("^/artist/" + stre_GUID + "/relationships$")) || enttype == "place" && location.pathname.match(new RegExp("^/place/" + stre_GUID + "/performances$")))
		||
		j2sets.RELEASE_EVENT_COLUMN && location.pathname.match(new RegExp("^/(artist|label)/" + stre_GUID + "/relationships$"))
	) {
		debug("RECORDING_LENGTH_COLUMN and/or RELEASE_EVENT_COLUMN");
		var relationshipTable = document.querySelector("div#content table.tbl");
		if (relationshipTable) {
			var fetchRecordingLength = j2sets.RECORDING_LENGTH_COLUMN && relationshipTable.getElementsByClassName("treleases").length == 0 && relationshipTable.querySelector("a[href*='/recording/']");
			var fetchReleaseEvents = j2sets.RELEASE_EVENT_COLUMN && relationshipTable.getElementsByClassName(userjs.id + "releaseEvents").length == 0 && relationshipTable.querySelector("a[href*='/release/']");
			if (fetchRecordingLength || fetchReleaseEvents) {
				var xhr_recording_length_column = new XMLHttpRequest();
				xhr_recording_length_column.addEventListener("load", function(event) {
					var relations = JSON.parse(this.responseText).relations;
					var recordingLengths = {};
					var recordingLengthFound = false;
					var releaseEvents = {};
					var releaseEventFound = false;
					// collecting recording length and release events
					for (let r = 0; r < relations.length; r++) {
						if (fetchRecordingLength && relations[r].recording && relations[r].recording.id && relations[r].recording.length) {
							recordingLengthFound = true;
							recordingLengths[relations[r].recording.id] = relations[r].recording.length;
						}
						if (fetchReleaseEvents && relations[r].release && relations[r].release.id && relations[r].release["release-events"]) {
							releaseEventFound = true;
							releaseEvents[relations[r].release.id] = relations[r].release["release-events"];
						}
					}
					if (recordingLengthFound || releaseEventFound) {
						// column headers
						if (recordingLengthFound) {
							var length_header = relationshipTable.querySelector("thead > tr").lastChild;
							length_header.style.setProperty("text-shadow", "0 0 2px yellow");
							length_header.classList.add("treleases");
							length_header.setAttribute("title", userjs.name);
						}
						if (releaseEventFound) {
							relationshipTable.querySelector("thead > tr").insertBefore(createTag("th", {a: {title: userjs.name, class: userjs.id + "releaseEvents"}, s: {textShadow: "0 0 2px yellow"}}, "Release events"), relationshipTable.querySelector("thead > tr > th:nth-child(2)"));
						}
						var rows = relationshipTable.querySelectorAll("tbody > tr");
						for (let r = 0; r < rows.length; r++) {
							if (rows[r].classList.contains("subh")) {
								// sub title row
								if (releaseEventFound) {
									var secondHeader = rows[r].querySelector("tr.subh > th:nth-child(2)");
									if (secondHeader) {
										rows[r].insertBefore(document.createElement("th"), secondHeader);
									}
								}
							} else {
								// normal data row
								if (recordingLengthFound) {
									var length_cell = rows[r].lastChild;
									length_cell.style.setProperty("text-align", "right");
									length_cell.classList.add("treleases");
									length_cell.setAttribute("title", userjs.name);
									var recordingID = rows[r].querySelector("a[href*='/recording/']");
									if (recordingID && (recordingID = recordingID.getAttribute("href").match(re_GUID)[0])) {
										replaceChildren(document.createTextNode(recordingLengths[recordingID] ? time(recordingLengths[recordingID]) : "?:??"), length_cell);
									}
								}
								if (releaseEventFound) {
									var secondCell = rows[r].querySelector("tr:not(.subh) > td:nth-child(2)");
									if (secondCell) {
										let newCell = rows[r].insertBefore(document.createElement("td"), secondCell);
										var releaseID = secondCell.querySelector("a[href*='/release/']");
										if (releaseID && (releaseID = releaseID.getAttribute("href").match(re_GUID)[0]) && releaseEvents[releaseID]) {
											for (let e = 0; e < releaseEvents[releaseID].length; e++) {
												var releaseDate = releaseEvents[releaseID][e].date ? releaseEvents[releaseID][e].date : "\u00a0";
												var releaseEvent = createTag("div", {}, releaseDate);
												if (releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area) {
													if (releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area.name) {
														releaseEvent.setAttribute("title", releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area.name);
													}
													if (releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area["iso-3166-1-codes"] && releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area["iso-3166-1-codes"].length > 0) {
														releaseEvent.classList.add("flag");
														releaseEvent.classList.add("flag-" + releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area["iso-3166-1-codes"][0]);
														releaseEvent.setAttribute("title", releaseEvent.getAttribute("title") + " (" + releaseEvents[releaseID][e].area["iso-3166-1-codes"][0] + ")");
													}
												}
												newCell.appendChild(releaseEvent);
												if (releaseEvents[releaseID].length > 4) {
													if (e > 1 && e != releaseEvents[releaseID].length - 1) {
														releaseEvent.classList.add(userjs.id + "hiddenEvent");
														releaseEvent.style.setProperty("display", "none");
													}
													if (e == 1) {
														newCell.appendChild(createTag("div", {s: {padding: "8px"}}, ["(", createTag("a", {e: {click: tooManyEventsToggle}}, "show " + (releaseEvents[releaseID].length - 3) + " more"), ")"]));
													} else if (e == releaseEvents[releaseID].length - 1) {
														newCell.appendChild(createTag("div", {a: {class: userjs.id + "hiddenEvent"}, s: {display: "none", padding: "8px"}}, ["(", createTag("a", {e: {click: tooManyEventsToggle}}, "show less"), ")"]));
													}
												}
											}
										}
									}
								}
							}
						}
					}
				});
				xhr_recording_length_column.open("get", MBS + "/ws/2" + location.pathname.match(new RegExp("^/" + enttype + "/" + stre_GUID)) + "?inc=recording-rels+release-rels&fmt=json", true);
				xhr_recording_length_column.send(null);
			}
		}
	}
#+end_example
