** initial

Add support for tags in our userscript, e.g. for URLs like

https://musicbrainz.org/user/vzell/tags

1.)

Add the userscript "match" directive

#+begin_example
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/tags
#+end_example

after

#+begin_example
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/subscriptions/*
#+end_example

2.)

add the following new pageType definition to "const pageDefinitions"

#+begin_example
        // Tags pages
        {
            type: 'tags',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/tags'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Tags for User' } ],
            tableMode: 'single',
            features: {
                listToTable: [ 'genres', 'tags' ],
                extractMainColumn: 'Tags'
            }
        },
#+end_example

3.)

To support URLs of type e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/user/vzell/tags

implement for the "listToTable" feature a function that searches for all h3 header lines and checks the "<div
id=<value>" container after it. If the "value" of the "id" matches an array element of the "listToTable" parameter
the "<ul>" structure e.g.

#+begin_example
<div id="genres">
  <ul class="genre-list">
    <li class="odd">
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/acoustic%20rock">acoustic rock
      </a>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">2
	</span>
      </span>
    </li>
    <li class="even">
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/hard%20rock">hard rock
      </a>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">1
	</span>
      </span>
    </li>
    <li class="odd">
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/interview">interview
      </a>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">30
	</span>
      </span>
    </li>
    <li class="even">
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/pop">pop
      </a>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">10
	</span>
      </span>
    </li>
    <li class="odd">
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/rock">rock
      </a>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">15
	</span>
      </span>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>
#+end_example

must be changed into a proper two-column table first, so that our script can process it.

a.)

The name of the first column should be derived from the id value of

#+begin_example
<div id="genres">
#+end_example

but in singular and capitalized, so the result should be

#+begin_example
Genre
#+end_example

b.)

the name of the second column is always "Tag count"

c.)

the column values should be extracted from the "<li>" elements, e.g. the list element

#+begin_example
    <li class="odd">
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/acoustic%20rock">acoustic rock
      </a>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">2
	</span>
      </span>
    </li>
#+end_example

would become a table row like

#+begin_example
<tr class="odd">
  <td>
      <a href="/user/vzell/tag/acoustic%20rock">acoustic rock
      </a>
  </td>
  <td>
      <span class="tag-vote-buttons">
	<span class="tag-count">2
	</span>
      </span>
  </td>
</tr>
#+end_example

** bugs

There is still a bug:

After converting the "<ul>" structures to proper table definitions, we should have the case of multiple h3-grouped
sub-tables, so basically we should be in the "tableMode: 'multi'" case and on
e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/user/vzell/tags I would assume to have two h3 headers ("Genres" and "Other tags") each with
a direct table sibling.

Still the final rendered page shows just one rendered table (the first one) below an h2 header named "Unknown"

Please fix.

1.)

change the userscript "include" directive

1.)

The "function applyListToTable(def)" needs to be adjusted for the case of pageType "type: 'artist-tags'"

There is no '<div id="<value>">' right after the h3 header line. Instead it directly starts with the list '<ul class="<type>-list">'

Also the "Column-name derivation rule" needs to be changed, instead of deriving the "First column" from the id value of

#+begin_example
<div id="<value>"> 
#+end_example

(singular and capitalized) we should derive it directly from <type> in the class parameter of the "<ul>" container. So
from e.g.

#+begin_example
<ul class="genre-list">
#+end_example

we would derive the first column name as the capitalized substring from before "-list"

#+begin_example
Genre
#+end_example

3.)

remove the double misplaced JSDoc comment from the function "_hintFromSrc(src, fromIdb, fromMemory)"

#+begin_example
    /**
     * Probes the Resource Timing API for the most recent PerformanceResourceEntry
     * that matches `url` and classifies it as a memory-cache hit, disk-cache hit,
     * network fetch, or "timing unavailable" (no entry / API absent).
     *
     * Classification rules (in priority order):
     *
     *   1. transferSize === 0 AND encodedBodySize > 0
     *        → Memory cache (🟢).  Browser served from RAM; size is known even
     *          for cross-origin resources when served from memory.
     *
     *   2. transferSize > 0
     *        → Network fetch (🔵).  Actual bytes received over the wire.
     *
     *   3. transferSize === 0 AND encodedBodySize === 0 AND duration < 5 ms
     *        → Disk cache (🟡).  Both size fields are 0 (opaque cross-origin) but
     *          the response arrived in under 5 ms — consistent with a disk-cached
     *          redirect chain where BOTH the redirect and the final resource are
     *          served from cache.
     *          NOTE: this path is NEVER triggered for coverartarchive.org /
     *          eventartarchive.org.  Their CDN issues non-cacheable 302 redirects
     *          (Cache-Control: no-store or max-age=0), so the redirect round-trip
     *          always adds 2–4 s of network latency and duration never drops below
     *          5 ms on a page reload.  🟡 is therefore only observable for image
     *          URLs that are served directly (no redirect) or whose redirect chain
     *          is genuinely disk-cached.
     *
     *   4. transferSize === 0 AND encodedBodySize === 0 AND duration >= 5 ms
     *        → Network / redirect chain (🔵).  This is the only outcome ever
     *          produced for CAA/EAA archive URLs on any repeated page load, because
     *          the non-cacheable redirect to archive.org always requires a fresh
     *          network round-trip.  Even if the final image bytes are already in
     *          the disk cache, the redirect latency keeps duration > 5 ms.
     *
     *   5. No matching entry, or Performance API absent
     *        → Timing unavailable (⚠️).  This is the ONLY case that returns
     *          'unavailable'; every case where an entry exists resolves to one of
     *          the three statuses above.
     *
     * Returned object shape:
     * ```
     * {
     *   status:          'memory' | 'disk' | 'network' | 'unavailable',
     *   transferSize:    number | null,
     *   encodedBodySize: number | null,
     *   duration:        number | null,
     *   redirectStart:   number | null,   // 0 for opaque cross-origin
     *   responseStart:   number | null,   // 0 for opaque cross-origin
     * }
     * ```
     *
     * @param  {string} url  The exact URL to look up (matched against entry.name).
     * @returns {{ status: string, transferSize: number|null, encodedBodySize: number|null,
     *             duration: number|null, redirectStart: number|null, responseStart: number|null }}
     */
#+end_example

4.)

remove the double misplaced JSDoc comment from the function "_applyCollapseState(toggle, expand)"

#+begin_example
    /**
     * Wires the single global ▶/◀ toggle button for multi-table mode.
     *
     * In multi-table mode `initCollapsableColumns` is called once per sub-table
     * (because each rendered `<table>` is independent), so it cannot accumulate
     * all column-header buttons into one `collapseHdrBtns` array.  This helper
     * fills that role: it is called once after ALL sub-tables have been
     * processed by `initCollapsableColumns`, scans the live DOM for every
     * `.mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn` across every `table.tbl`, and then:
     *
     *   • Hides the global button when no such buttons exist (none of the
     *     filtered-in sub-tables has a multi-row column).
     *   • Shows the global button and re-wires its `onclick` to drive every
     *     found header button (and therefore every cell toggle across every
     *     sub-table) to the target expanded/collapsed state.
     *   • Calls `updateGlobalCollapseButtonHighlight(null)` to apply or clear
     *     the highlight tint on the global button based on the current filter
     *     match state across all sub-tables.
     *
     * Must be called from `renderGroupedTable` after the `dataArray.forEach`
     * loop completes — both on initial render and on every filter re-run.
     */
#+end_example

// @include      /^https?:\/\/(?:[^\/]+\.)?musicbrainz\.org\/(?:artist|release-group|release|work|recording|label|series|place|area|instrument|event)\/[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}\/(?:aliases|releases|recordings|works|events|relationships|discids|fingerprints|performances|places|artists|labels|tags)(?:\?.*)?$/
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
// @include      /^https?:\/\/(?:[^\/]+\.)?musicbrainz\.org\/(?:artist|release-group|release|work|recording|label|series|place|area|instrument|event)\/[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}\/(?:aliases|releases|recordings|works|events|relationships|discids|fingerprints|performances|places|artists|labels|tags|users)(?:\?.*)?$/

make the function

#+begin_example
dateParts(sourceCell)
#+end_example

which is currently part of only the synthetic columnData extractors "const SyntheticColumnDataExtractor" also callable
as part of the standard column extractors "const ColumnDataExtractor", as the "Date" column also exists in standard

#+begin_example
<td>2108-06-14 – 2108-06-17</td>

case.

** initial

some enhancements:

1.)

for "Release events" columns add the abbreviated country code after the flag icon:

In the function

#+begin_example
async function initReleaseEventsColumn()
#+end_example

the current rendered "Release events" data cell, e.g.

#+begin_example
<td class="mb-re-cell" data-mbid="5897f949-48a7-47f5-b3b8-206b1e5b7d9a" style="" data-re-done="1">
  <ul style="list-style: none; margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">
    <li class="flag flag-CA" title="Canada (CA)">1988-11
    </li>
  </ul>
</td>
#+end_example

should look like

#+begin_example
<td class="mb-re-cell" data-mbid="5897f949-48a7-47f5-b3b8-206b1e5b7d9a" style="" data-re-done="1">
  <ul style="list-style: none; margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">
    <li class="flag flag-CA" title="Canada (CA)">(CA) 1988-11
    </li>
  </ul>
</td>
#+end_example

So basically the 2-char abbreviated country name should be rendered in brackets after the "country flag" icon

#+end_example

** solution

The complete timing sequence is now:

#+begin_example
applyShowAllTags()          → exposes hidden tags
applyRenameH2ToH3/InsertH2  → restructure headings
applyListToTable()          → convert <ul> → <table> (inside div.all-tags)
performClutterCleanup()     → other cleanup, div.all-tags left intact
fetch loop                  → reads rows from the tables
renderGroupedTable()        → clones thead template, builds output tables
finalCleanup()              → removeSelector removes div.all-tags  ← here
#+end_example

3.)

Extend "Structure C: h2+ul"

#+begin_example
     * ── Structure C: h2+ul (/area/<mbid>/users and /tag/<v>/<entity> pages) ───
     *
     *   Triggered when `sectionId === ''` AND the current page path matches
     *   either `/area/<mbid>/users` (pageType 'area-users') OR the two-segment
     *   tag-entity pattern `/tag/<value>/<entity>` (pageType 'tag-value-entity').
     *   For all other pageTypes with `sectionId === ''`, Structure D is used.
     *
     *   area-users variant:
     *     Before:
     *       <h2>Users</h2>
     *       <p>…</p> <nav>…</nav>
     *       <ul>
     *         <li><a href="/user/__MarioMadrid"><img …><bdi>__MarioMadrid</bdi></a></li>
     *         …
     *       </ul>
     *     After:
     *       <h2>Users</h2>
     *       <table class="tbl">…</table>
     *     Column name: full h2 text (e.g. "Users").
#+end_example

to additionally support pageType "type: 'editor-subscribers'"

     *       </ul>

#+end_example

add the following new pagType definition

#+begin_example
        // User tag value entity pages (/user/<username>/tag/<tag>/<entity> e.g. /user/vzell/tag/gig/event)
        // Must come before the generic tag-value entry (narrower match).
        {
            type: 'user-tag-value-entity',
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/user\/.*\/tag\/.*/),
            buttons: [
                { label: 'Entities upvoted',   params: { show_downvoted: '0' } },
                { label: 'Entities downvoted', params: { show_downvoted: '1' } }
            ],
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Entities tagged' } ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the
                // function scans for <h2>…<ul> pairs in the content area and uses
                // the first word of the h2 text as the column name (e.g. "Labels").
                listToTable: [ '' ],
                // Remove the vote/sort form after rendering since the two buttons
                // above replace its function and the form is no longer needed.
                removeSelector: 'form:has(select[name="show_downvoted"])'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

in the newly introduced pageType "type: 'user-tag-value-entity'"
the name of the table derived from the "<ul>" list structure is wrong, currently e.g. "Events vzell" instead of just

The problem is the current extraction works fine for the pageType "type: 'tag-value-entity'", see the the comment in the
function "function applyListToTable(def, docContext = document)"

but should be

#+end_example

for the pageType "type: 'user-tag-value-entity'"

also change the current comment

#+begin_example
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the

#+end_example

in the pageType "type: 'user-tag-value-entity'" accordingly

** DONE table name bug

in the following two sections the comment and the corresponding code algorythms to extract the table column name

#+begin_example
        // User tag value entity pages (/user/<username>/tag/<tag>/<entity>
        // e.g. /user/vzell/tag/gig/event) — must come before user-tag-value
        // (same /user/.*/tag/ prefix, but has an additional entity segment).
        {
            type: 'user-tag-value-entity',
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/user\/[^/]+\/tag\/[^/]+\/.+/),
            buttons: [
                { label: 'Entities upvoted',   params: { show_downvoted: '0' } },
                { label: 'Entities downvoted', params: { show_downvoted: '1' } }
            ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the
                // function scans for <h2>…<ul> pairs in the content area and uses
                // the first word of the h2 text as the column name, e.g.
                // "Events vzell tagged as «gig»" → "Events".
                listToTable: [ '' ],
                // Remove the vote/sort form after rendering since the two buttons
                // above replace its function and the form is no longer needed.
                removeSelector: 'form[style*="margin-top"]'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
        // Tag pages per entity (e.g. /tag/country/labels) — must come before
        // the generic tag-value entry because its match is narrower (two path
        // segments after /tag/ instead of one).
        {
            type: 'tag-value-entity',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/tag\/[^/]+\/.+/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Entities tagged' } ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the
                // function scans for <h2>…<ul> pairs in the content area and uses
                // the first word of the h2 text as the column name (e.g. "Labels").
                listToTable: [ '' ]
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

must be changed in the following ways:

1.)

for the pageType "type: 'user-tag-value-entity'":

the column name should be the <h2> text from the beginning up to the word "tagged" but with out the "<username>", e.g. "vzell"

e.g: "Release groups vzell tagged as «gig»" → "Release groups"

2.)

for the pageType "type: 'tag-value-entity'":

the column name should be the <h2> text from the beginning up to the word "tagged"

e.g: "Release groups tagged as «gig»" → "Release groups"

* DONE ghosting bug still present also checkbox column removed for date column in first column

There is a ghosting bug present when a "stickyColumn" is defined in a table BUT it is NOT the first column of the table, e.g. in the following pageTypes 

#+begin_example
"Year" in first column  - https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f

"Date" in first column  - https://musicbrainz.org/work/f4a77656-8cdb-365c-aef9-034c098e249f
"Date" in first column  - https://musicbrainz.org/work/f4a77656-8cdb-365c-aef9-034c098e249f?direction=2&link_type_id=278&page=1

"#, Year" in first two columns - https://musicbrainz.org/series/6d28ac8e-7a62-4237-ae36-00803a57aed0
#+end_example

When scrolling horizontally to the right, the problem on these pageTypes is, the sticky column is NOT completely sticked
to the left page border when scrolling, but insteaf it leaves an artificially space before getting sticky. In this space
you can see the columns coming after the sticky one, srolling by when horizontally scrolling.

But strangely this effect is NOT visible for the following pageTypes (no ghosting effect), even if they also have a
"Date" column before the column defined as sticky

#+begin_example
"Date" in first column - https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f/relationships
"Date" in first column  - https://musicbrainz.org/place/6a59a67c-fcc5-491f-949c-bfc45bc97463/performances
#+end_example

The sticky column in these cases when srolling to the right gets sticky when it reaches the exact left page border

add some new "columnExtractors" for the pageType "'tag-value-entity'"

1.)

#+begin_example
            features: {
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Areas',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },

                    { sourceColumn: 'Instruments',    extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Labels',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Releases',       extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Recordings',     extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Series',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Works',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] }
                ],
#+end_example

2.)

the "tagCount_Name_Comment" extractor function should by implemented the following way:

the standard structure looks like e.g.:

#+begin_example
<td style="position: sticky; left: 0px; z-index: 1; background: rgb(255, 255, 255);" class="mb-sticky-col">26 -
  <a href="/artist/d43d12a1-2dc9-4257-a2fd-0a3bb1081b86" title="Cash, Johnny (country music legend)">
    <bdi>Johnny Cash
    </bdi>
  </a>
  <span class="comment">(
    <bdi>country music legend
    </bdi>)
  </span>
</td>
#+end_example

the "syntheticColumns" should be extracted according to the following rules:

- 'Name':

the text from the "<bdi>" container, eg. "Johnny Cash"

- 'Tag count':

the number count right inside the beginning of the column data cell,  e.g "26" (without the " -")

- 'Comment':

the text from the "<bdi>" container inside the span container '<span class="comment">', eg. "country music legend"

** DONE bugs


There is a bug with the pageType "type: 'tag-value-entity'" regarding the "columnExtractors":

1.)

the pageType "type: 'tag-value-entity'" is in itself a single column table, so actually ONLY one of the
"columnExtractors" array really applies, e.g. for the URL "https://musicbrainz.org/tag/country/event" the "sourceColumn" definition

#+begin_example
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
#+end_example

should take over, although ALL entity "sourceColumn" definitions are defined on the pageType.

So in essence, the mapping for the extraction should be:

#+begin_example
https://musicbrainz.org/tag/country/<entity type singular>
#+end_example

maps to the 

#+begin_example
            features: {
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: '<<entity type plural>>',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
#+end_example

** DONE event

change the following "columnExtractors" for the pageType "'tag-value-entity'"

1.)

for the "sourceColumn" 'Events' from

#+begin_example
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Date_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Date', 'Comment'] },
#+end_example

The new "tagCount_Name_Date_Comment" function additionally to what the generic "tagCount_Name_Comment" does, should extract the "Date" column from e.g.

#+begin_example
<td style="position: sticky; left: 0px; z-index: 1; background: rgb(242, 242, 242);" class="mb-sticky-col">1 -
  <a href="/event/750bca04-5cdc-4545-818b-72fab4206395">
    <bdi>Roots &amp; Boots 90's Electric Throwdown
    </bdi>
  </a> (2019-07-05)
  <!-- -->
  <span class="comment">(
    <bdi>Kamas, Utah
    </bdi>)
  </span>
</td>
#+end_example

So basically the date is located in brackets after the closing " </a>". Extract it without the brackets.

2.)

for the "sourceColumn" 'Recordings', 'Releases' and 'Release groups' from

#+begin_example
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Releases',       extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Recordings',     extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
#+end_example

to

some fixes:

1.)

change the definition for the pageType "type: 'tag-value-entity'" from

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'tag-value-entity',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/tag\/[^/]+\/.+/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Entities tagged' } ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the
                // function scans for <h2>…<ul> pairs in the content area and uses
                // the h2 text up to "tagged" as the column name
                // (e.g. "Release groups tagged as «gig»" → "Release groups").
                listToTable: [ '' ],
                // Split the entity cell ("26 - Johnny Cash (comment)") into three
                // synthetic columns. The sourceColumn matches the h2-derived table
                // header (e.g. "Labels", "Release groups", …).
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Areas',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Artists',        extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Date_Comment',    syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Date', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Instruments',    extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Labels',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Places',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Releases',       extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Recordings',     extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Series',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Works',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] }
                ]
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'tag-value-entity',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/tag\/[^/]+\/.+/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Entities tagged' } ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the
                // function scans for <h2>…<ul> pairs in the content area and uses
                // the h2 text up to "tagged" as the column name
                // (e.g. "Release groups tagged as «gig»" → "Release groups").

                // Split the entity cell ("26 - Johnny Cash (comment)") into three

                // header (e.g. "Labels", "Release groups", …).
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Areas',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Artists',        extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Date_Comment',    syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Date', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Instruments',    extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Labels',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Places',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Releases',       extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Recordings',     extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Series',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Works',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] }
                ],
                syntheticColumnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Date', extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['DD', 'MM', 'YYYY', 'Day', 'Month'] }
                ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ]
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

** initial

some fixes:

1.)

for the pageType "type: 'tag-value'" fix the first column name for each table under each h3 section. Currently it is
always "Areas". It looks like it's currently taken from the first h3 header line text.

It should instead be the singular form of the h3 header line text preceding each table.

So e.g. if the h3 header line is

#+begin_example
<h3>Release groups</h3>
#+end_example

the first column name of the corresponding table should be "Release group". The only exception is for 

#+begin_example
<h3>Series</h3>
#+end_example

where the column name should also be "Series"

2.)

for the pageType "type: 'tag-value'" definition add the following new "columnExtractors" so that it looks like

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'tag-value',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/tag\//),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Entities tagged' } ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure D in applyListToTable.
                listToTable: [ '' ],
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Areas',          extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Artists',        extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'Name_Date_Comment',    syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Date', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Instruments',    extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Labels',         extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Places',         extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', extractor: 'Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Releases',       extractor: 'Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Recordings',     extractor: 'Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Series',         extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Works',          extractor: 'Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Comment'] }
                ],
                syntheticColumnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Date', extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['DD', 'MM', 'YYYY', 'Day', 'Month'] }
                ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ]
            },
            tableMode: 'multi'
        },
#+end_example

            type: 'tag-value-entity',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/tag\/[^/]+\/.+/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Entities tagged' } ],
            features: {
                // Empty sectionId triggers Structure C in applyListToTable: the
                // function scans for <h2>…<ul> pairs in the content area and uses
                // the h2 text up to "tagged" as the column name
                // (e.g. "Release groups tagged as «gig»" → "Release groups").
                listToTable: [ '' ],
                // Split the entity cell ("26 - Johnny Cash (comment)") into three
                // synthetic columns. The sourceColumn matches the h2-derived table
                // header (e.g. "Labels", "Release groups", …).
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Areas',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Artists',        extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Events',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Date_Comment',    syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Date', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Instruments',    extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Labels',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Places',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Release groups', extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Releases',       extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Recordings',     extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists', syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment', 'Artists'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Series',         extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Works',          extractor: 'tagCount_Name_Comment',         syntheticColumns: ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment'] }
                ],
                // Split the extracted Date column into DD/MM/YYYY/Day/Month sub-columns
                // only when Events are shown (i.e. when the Date synthetic column exists).
                syntheticColumnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Date', extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['DD', 'MM', 'YYYY', 'Day', 'Month'] }
                ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Tag count', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ]
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

to

            entityFeatures: {

                },
                'Events': {

                    integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Tag count', align: 'R'} ],

                    integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Tag count', align: 'R'} ]

                },

                    integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Tag count', align: 'R'} ]

** DONE bugs

please revert the changes from the last session

actually the comment should change from

#+begin_example
    // There are different types of MusicBrainz pages
    // | Page type                    | multiple tables           | paginated | table header                         |
    // |------------------------------+---------------------------+-----------+--------------------------------------|
    // | Artist-Releasegroups         | native                    | x         | h3, h2 not repeating on paginated    |
    // | Releasegroup-Releases        | single table, subgrouping | x         | h2, repeating on paginated pages     |
    // | Collections-Releasegroups    | native                    | x         | h2, repeating on paginated pages     |
    // | Place-Performances, ...      | single table, subgrouping |           | h2, repeating on single page         |
    // | Events                       | single table              | x         | h3,                                  |
    // | Search                       | single table              | x         | p.pageselector-results               |
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
    // There are different types of MusicBrainz pages
    // | Page type                    | multiple tables           | paginated | table header                         |
    // |------------------------------+---------------------------+-----------+--------------------------------------|
    // | Artist-Releasegroups         | native                    | x         | h3, not repeating on paginated pages |
    // | Releasegroup-Releases        | single table, subgrouping | x         | h3, repeating on paginated pages     |
    // | Collections-Releasegroups    | native                    | x         | h3, repeating on paginated pages     |
    // | Place-Performances, ...      | single table, subgrouping |           | h3, repeating on single page         |
    // | Events                       | single table              | x         | h3,                                  |
    // | Search                       | single table              | x         | p.pageselector-results               |
#+end_example

On "type: 'collections-releases'" and the entityFeatures "'Release groups':" it's the h3 header which could repeat on
each paginated page.  So there could be rows under the e.g. "Album" h2 header which represent the "Album" table on the
final rendered page on multiple paginated fetched pages. But on the final rendered page ALL these rows from the h2
headers (maybe from multipel paginated pages) should appear ONLY in one "Album" table. The same for other h2 headers like "Single", "Compilation" etc.

** solution

#+begin_example
he user is clarifying that on collections-release-groups pages, the heading that repeats on paginated pages is actually H3 (not H2). So the fix from v9.99.467 was wrong in assuming H2 - it should be H3. Let me re-read the situation.
The user says: "it's the h3 header which could repeat on each paginated page". So the current h3 backwards walk was actually correct! The issue was something else - or maybe the page structure uses H3 throughout, and the existing code (which only looks for H3) was already correct.
Wait, but then why were all the rows collapsing to 'Other'? Let me re-think.
Actually, I see it now - the comment in the code was just mislabeled as H2 when it should say H3. The real logic is already handling this correctly: when the same category (like "Album") appears across multiple paginated fetches, the existing check for whether the category changed prevents creating duplicate group entries, so all rows from that category get merged into a single table regardless of which paginated page they came from.
Wait, but that's not quite right either. If page 2 starts with "Album" again after page 1 ended with "EP", the condition category !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages would be true, and it would create a new "Album" group instead of appending to the existing one. So the current logic actually does create separate groups when a category repeats, which is the bug.
The fix is to check if a group for that category already exists in the groupedRows array, and if it does, append to that existing group instead of creating a new one. That way, when "Album" appears again on page 2, it gets added to the same "Album" group from page 1.
For the v9.99.467 changes, I need to revert the H2/H3 detection change and instead fix the actual grouping logic to handle repeating categories across paginated pages correctly. I see the issue now—the current logic treats each H3 heading as a new category boundary, so when "Album" appears again on page 2, it creates a duplicate group instead of merging with the existing one. I need to track which categories have already been seen and only push a new group if it's truly a new category, not just a repeat from pagination. Refactoring the grouping logic...
The key insight is that artist-releasegroups don't repeat categories across pages since they're continuous, while collections-releasegroups do repeat them. So switching to a find-or-create approach using groupedRows.find() handles both cases safely—it'll work for non-repeating categories and correctly merge repeating ones.
Now I need to revert the H2/H3 detection back to H3-only, update the documentation, and fix the merge logic for when categories repeat.
#+end_example

#+begin_example
Now I have the full picture. The user is clarifying that it is H3 throughout (not H2), and the real bug is the grouping logic: when H3 categories repeat across paginated XHR pages, groupedRows.push() creates duplicate groups instead of merging into the existing one. The two fixes are:

Revert the H2/H3 detection change back to H3-only
Fix the group find-or-create logic so repeated H3 categories merge into one group
Update the documentation comment correctly
#+end_example

  <span data-erg-btn="1" title="Toggle display of underlying entity page" style="cursor: pointer; margin-right: 4px; color: rgb(119, 119, 119);">▼

* DONE Most popular tags in MusicBrainz
** initial

add most popular tags in MusicBrainz support:

1.)

add a new userscript "match" directive

#+begin_example
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/tags*
#+end_example

after

#+begin_example
// @match        *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/tags*
#+end_example

2.)

add a new pageType definition along the line of

#+begin_example
        // Most popular tags in MusicBrainz
        {
            type: 'popular-tags',
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/tags/),
            buttons: [
                { label: 'Show most popular tags', params: { show_list: '1' } }
            ],
            renameH2ToH3: true,
            insertH2: 'Popular tags',
            features: {
                listToTable: [ 'genres', 'tags' ]
            },
            tableMode: 'multi'
        },
#+end_example

after the pageType "type: 'user-tags'"


3.)

Adjust the pageType definition to what needs to be done to get the following implemented:

The structure for this new pageType is

#+begin_example
<h1>
<p>
<h2>Genres</h2>
<ul class="tag-list" top-tag-list">
<h2>Other Tags</h2>
<ul class="tag-list" top-tag-list">
#+end_example

Rename the H2 header lines first to H3 equivalents and add a new H2 headerline after the <p>.  Each list <ul> after each
H3 header line should be converted to a one column table with the column name derived from the singular form of the h3
header line in front of it.

Then feed it in the standard rendering machinery for "multi"-table pageTypes.

** initial

some more changes to the pageType definitions:

1.)

for the pageType "type: 'area-artists'"

change

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'area-artists',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/area\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/artists/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Artists for Area' } ],
            features: {
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Area', extractor: 'splitArea', syntheticColumns: ['MB-Area', 'Country'] }
                ],
                extractMainColumn: 'Artist',
                stickyColumn: 'Artist'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

to

#+begin_example
        {
            type: 'area-artists',
            match: (path) => path.match(/\/area\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/artists/),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all Artists for Area' } ],
            features: {
                columnExtractors: [
                    { sourceColumn: 'Area', extractor: 'splitArea', syntheticColumns: ['MB-Area', 'Country'] }
                    { sourceColumn: 'Area',       extractor: 'splitArea', syntheticColumns: ['MB-Area', 'Country'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Begin area', extractor: 'splitArea', syntheticColumns: ['MB-Begin area', 'Begin country'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'End area',   extractor: 'splitArea', syntheticColumns: ['MB-End area', 'End country'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'Begin',      extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['B-DD', 'B-MM', 'B-YYYY', 'B-Day', 'B-Month'] },
                    { sourceColumn: 'End',        extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['E-DD', 'E-MM', 'E-YYYY', 'E-Day', 'E-Month'] }
                ],
                integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'B-DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'B-MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'B-YYYY', align: 'C'}, {sourceColumn: 'E-DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'E-MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'E-YYYY', align: 'C'} ],
                extractMainColumn: 'Artist',
                stickyColumn: 'Artist'
            },
            tableMode: 'single'
        },
#+end_example

2.)

To all pageTypes where an "extractor: 'dateParts'" is defined and the following definition is missing

#+begin_example
integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ]

#+begin_example

parameter to the following pageType definitons

for the following two

extend the existing definition

to

+* DONE https://musicbrainz.org/collection/3d73c67c-76c6-459d-ac8b-3145cb49d672 - for RGs add multi-table support
+** DONE initial
+
+add support for release groups collection pages:
+
+1.)
+
+for the pageType "type: 'collections-releases'," change the "entityFeatures" from
+
+#+begin_example
+                'Release groups': {
+                    columnExtractors: [
+                        { sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['CAA'] }
+                    ],

+                    injectedColumns: [ 'Relationships' ],

+
+to
+
+#+begin_example
+                'Release groups': {
+                    columnExtractors: [
+                        { sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['CAA'] }
+                    ],
+                    injectedColumns: [ 'Relationships' ],
+                    integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Releases', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'Year', align: 'C'} ],
+                    collapsableColumns: [ 'CAA' ],
+                    tooltipColumns: [ 'Title', 'Artist', '---', ['Year', '(', 'Releases', ')'] ],
+                    addCAA: 'Title',
+                    extractMainColumn: 'Title',
+                    stickyColumn: 'Title',
+		    tableMode: 'multi' // native tables, h3 headers
+                },
+#+end_example
+
+Also special case the code path for entityFeatures "'Release groups':" as it's the same as the standard pageType "type: 'artist-releasegroups',"
+
+So it's actually "multi" tableMode, but it still needs the "columnHeaderErasers: [ '▴/▾' ]" applied for each fetched
+paginated page.
+
+** DONE bugs
+
+please extend the handling for the pageType "type: 'collections-releases'" and the entityFeatures "'Release groups':"
+to support the "h2, repeating on paginated pages" feature like it exists for the "Releasegroup-Releases" pageType "type: 'releasegroup-releases'".
+
+See the following documentation located before the "const pageDefinitions"
+
+#+begin_example
+    // There are different types of MusicBrainz pages
+    // | Page type               | multiple tables           | paginated | table header                         |
+    // |-------------------------+---------------------------+-----------+--------------------------------------|
+    // | Artist-Releasegroups    | native                    | x         | h2, not repeating on paginated pages |
+    // | Releasegroup-Releases   | single table, subgrouping | x         | h2, repeating on paginated pages     |
+
+NEW:
+    // | Collections-Releasegroups    | native                    | x         | h2, repeating on paginated pages |
+#+end_example
+
+Actually it wouldn't harm if the feature is also implemented for the "Artist-Releasegroups" case also its "not repeating on paginated pages" there
+
+** DONE bugs
+
+please revert the changes from the last session
+
+actually the comment should change from
+
+#+begin_example
+    // There are different types of MusicBrainz pages
+    // | Page type                    | multiple tables           | paginated | table header                         |
+    // |------------------------------+---------------------------+-----------+--------------------------------------|
+    // | Artist-Releasegroups         | native                    | x         | h3, h2 not repeating on paginated    |
+    // | Releasegroup-Releases        | single table, subgrouping | x         | h2, repeating on paginated pages     |
+    // | Collections-Releasegroups    | native                    | x         | h2, repeating on paginated pages     |
+    // | Place-Performances, ...      | single table, subgrouping |           | h2, repeating on single page         |
+    // | Events                       | single table              | x         | h3,                                  |
+    // | Search                       | single table              | x         | p.pageselector-results               |
+#+end_example
+
+to
+
+#+begin_example
+    // There are different types of MusicBrainz pages
+    // | Page type                    | multiple tables           | paginated | table header                         |
+    // |------------------------------+---------------------------+-----------+--------------------------------------|
+    // | Artist-Releasegroups         | native                    | x         | h3, not repeating on paginated pages |
+    // | Releasegroup-Releases        | single table, subgrouping | x         | h3, repeating on paginated pages     |
+    // | Collections-Releasegroups    | native                    | x         | h3, repeating on paginated pages     |
+    // | Place-Performances, ...      | single table, subgrouping |           | h3, repeating on single page         |
+    // | Events                       | single table              | x         | h3,                                  |
+    // | Search                       | single table              | x         | p.pageselector-results               |
+#+end_example
+
+On "type: 'collections-releases'" and the entityFeatures "'Release groups':" it's the h3 header which could repeat on
+each paginated page.  So there could be rows under the e.g. "Album" h2 header which represent the "Album" table on the
+final rendered page on multiple paginated fetched pages. But on the final rendered page ALL these rows from the h2
+headers (maybe from multipel paginated pages) should appear ONLY in one "Album" table. The same for other h2 headers like "Single", "Compilation" etc.
+
+** solution
+
+#+begin_example
+he user is clarifying that on collections-release-groups pages, the heading that repeats on paginated pages is actually H3 (not H2). So the fix from v9.99.467 was wrong in assuming H2 - it should be H3. Let me re-read the situation.
+The user says: "it's the h3 header which could repeat on each paginated page". So the current h3 backwards walk was actually correct! The issue was something else - or maybe the page structure uses H3 throughout, and the existing code (which only looks for H3) was already correct.
+Wait, but then why were all the rows collapsing to 'Other'? Let me re-think.
+Actually, I see it now - the comment in the code was just mislabeled as H2 when it should say H3. The real logic is already handling this correctly: when the same category (like "Album") appears across multiple paginated fetches, the existing check for whether the category changed prevents creating duplicate group entries, so all rows from that category get merged into a single table regardless of which paginated page they came from.
+Wait, but that's not quite right either. If page 2 starts with "Album" again after page 1 ended with "EP", the condition category !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages would be true, and it would create a new "Album" group instead of appending to the existing one. So the current logic actually does create separate groups when a category repeats, which is the bug.
+The fix is to check if a group for that category already exists in the groupedRows array, and if it does, append to that existing group instead of creating a new one. That way, when "Album" appears again on page 2, it gets added to the same "Album" group from page 1.
+For the v9.99.467 changes, I need to revert the H2/H3 detection change and instead fix the actual grouping logic to handle repeating categories across paginated pages correctly. I see the issue now—the current logic treats each H3 heading as a new category boundary, so when "Album" appears again on page 2, it creates a duplicate group instead of merging with the existing one. I need to track which categories have already been seen and only push a new group if it's truly a new category, not just a repeat from pagination. Refactoring the grouping logic...
+The key insight is that artist-releasegroups don't repeat categories across pages since they're continuous, while collections-releasegroups do repeat them. So switching to a find-or-create approach using groupedRows.find() handles both cases safely—it'll work for non-repeating categories and correctly merge repeating ones.
+Now I need to revert the H2/H3 detection back to H3-only, update the documentation, and fix the merge logic for when categories repeat.
+#+end_example
+
+#+begin_example
+Now I have the full picture. The user is clarifying that it is H3 throughout (not H2), and the real bug is the grouping logic: when H3 categories repeat across paginated XHR pages, groupedRows.push() creates duplicate groups instead of merging into the existing one. The two fixes are:
+
+Revert the H2/H3 detection change back to H3-only
+Fix the group find-or-create logic so repeated H3 categories merge into one group
+Update the documentation comment correctly
+#+end_example
+

+    /**
+     * Returns true when the current fetch should use the artist-releasegroups
+     * h3-category scraping path.  This covers two cases:
+     *
+     *   1. The page is `artist-releasegroups` (always multi, native h3-grouped tables).
+     *   2. The page is `collections-releases` AND the resolved entityFeatures block
+     *      declared `tableMode: 'multi'` (currently only `'Release groups'`), meaning
+     *      the collection page contains native h3-grouped release-group sub-tables
+     *      identical to the artist-releasegroups layout.
+     *
+     * Using this predicate avoids repeating the same compound condition at every
+     * relevant guard site (fetch loop, clutter cleanup, sanojjonas removal).
+     *
+     * @returns {boolean}
+     */
+    function _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode() {
+        return pageType === 'artist-releasegroups' ||
+               (pageType === 'collections-releases' &&
+                activeDefinition && activeDefinition.tableMode === 'multi');
+    }
+

@@ -22594,6 +22640,16 @@ a { color: #1565c0; }`;
                         // findH3ForTable() requires that class and always returns null
                         // here, collapsing every group into "Other".  Use a plain
                         // backwards walk with no class restriction instead.
+                        //
+                        // For artist-releasegroups the h3 does NOT repeat across
+                        // paginated XHR pages — categories are seen at most once.
+                        //
+                        // For collections-release-groups (Release groups entity) the
+                        // h3 DOES repeat: each paginated XHR page begins with the
+                        // same h3 headers ("Album", "Single", …) followed by their

+                        // merges all rows for a given category name into a single

                         let _prev = table.previousElementSibling, _steps = 0, _h3 = null;

+                        // Find-or-create: if a group with this category already exists

modified   ShowAllEntityData_CHANGELOG.json
@@ -1,4 +1,40 @@
 [
+  {
+    "version": "9.99.468",
+    "date": "2026-04-11",
+    "sections": [
+      {
+        "label": "🐛 Fix",
+        "items": [
+          "Reverted the H2-detection code added in v9.99.467 (incorrect: the heading on collections-release-group and releasegroup-releases pages is H3 throughout, not H2). The actual bug was in the group accumulation logic: the old code used 'if category !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages → push new group' which created duplicate groupedRows entries when the same H3 category (e.g. 'Album') repeated on successive paginated XHR pages instead of appending to the existing group. Fix: replaced the push-on-change logic with a find-or-create pattern (groupedRows.find(g => g.category === category) || push new) so all rows for a given category are merged into one group regardless of how many paginated pages contribute them. artist-releasegroups is unaffected because its categories never repeat across pages (the find() always returns null on first occurrence, so the behavior is identical to the old push). Updated the documentation table: all heading levels corrected to 'h3'; artist-releasegroups 'not repeating', releasegroup-releases and collections-releasegroups 'repeating on paginated pages'."
+        ]
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  {
+    "version": "9.99.467",
+    "date": "2026-04-11",
+    "sections": [
+      {
+        "label": "✨ Feature",
+        "items": [
+          "collections-releases 'Release groups' and artist-releasegroups: extended the _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode fetch-loop h3-backwards-walk to also detect H2 category headers. On collections-release-group pages (and on releasegroup-releases pages) MusicBrainz repeats the H2 heading (e.g. 'Album', 'Single') at the top of each fetched XHR page, but no H3 siblings precede the table. The old walk only looked for H3, so all tables on these pages collapsed to category 'Other'. Fix: the backwards walk now records both H3 and H2 hits; H3 takes precedence when found (preserving artist-releasegroups behaviour unchanged), with H2 as the fallback. The heading text is cleaned of sort glyphs (▴▾▼▲⇅) and count spans before being used as the group key. Updated the page-type documentation table to record 'Collections-Releasegroups' as a new row."
+        ]
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  {
+    "version": "9.99.466",
+    "date": "2026-04-11",
+    "sections": [
+      {
+        "label": "✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix",
+        "items": [
+          "collections-releases 'Release groups': full support for native h3-grouped release-group collection pages. The entityFeatures 'Release groups' entry already carried tableMode:'multi', the correct integerColumns, tooltipColumns, and injectedColumns from v9.99.455. This version wires up the matching code paths: (1) New module-level predicate _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode() returns true for both artist-releasegroups and collections-releases when activeDefinition.tableMode === 'multi', avoiding repetition across call sites. (2) The fetch-loop h3-category scraping branch (native backwards-walk, no mb-toggle-h3 class restriction) now also fires for collections-releases Release-groups pages, populating groupedRows with the correct h3-derived category keys. (3) Both removeSanojjonasContainers() call sites extended to use the new predicate. (4) All three renderGroupedTable(…, isArtistMain) arguments updated from pageType === 'artist-releasegroups' to _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode(). The pre-fetch official-categories pass and post-render discography-view injection remain artist-releasegroups-only (collections pages don't have an Official/Non-official split). columnHeaderErasers: ['▴/▾'] continues to apply correctly because it is read from activeDefinition.columnHeaderErasers (spread from baseDef) which is unaffected by the entityFeatures tableMode override."
+        ]
+      }
+    ]
+  },
   {
     "version": "9.99.465",

#+end_example

** question

In the following change

#+begin_example
  {
    "version": "9.99.468",
    "date": "2026-04-11",
    "sections": [
      {
        "label": "🐛 Fix",
        "items": [
          "Reverted the H2-detection code added in v9.99.467 (incorrect: the heading on collections-release-group and releasegroup-releases pages is H3 throughout, not H2). The actual bug was in the group accumulation logic: the old code used 'if category !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages → push new group' which created duplicate groupedRows entries when the same H3 category (e.g. 'Album') repeated on successive paginated XHR pages instead of appending to the existing group. Fix: replaced the push-on-change logic with a find-or-create pattern (groupedRows.find(g => g.category === category) || push new) so all rows for a given category are merged into one group regardless of how many paginated pages contribute them. artist-releasegroups is unaffected because its categories never repeat across pages (the find() always returns null on first occurrence, so the behavior is identical to the old push). Updated the documentation table: all heading levels corrected to 'h3'; artist-releasegroups 'not repeating', releasegroup-releases and collections-releasegroups 'repeating on paginated pages'."
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
#+end_example

+                        //
+                        // For artist-releasegroups the h3 does NOT repeat across
+                        // paginated XHR pages — categories are seen at most once.
+                        //
+                        // For collections-release-groups (Release groups entity) the
+                        // h3 DOES repeat: each paginated XHR page begins with the
+                        // same h3 headers ("Album", "Single", …) followed by their
+                        // respective sub-tables.  The find-or-create logic below
+                        // merges all rows for a given category name into a single

-                            groupedRows.push({ category: category, rows: [] });
-                            lastCategorySeenAcrossPages = category;

+                        if (!currentGroup) {

#+end_example

which are not quite right.

1.)

They work perfectly for the pageType "type: 'collections-releases'," and the "entityFeatures" ==> 'Release groups'.
"collections-releasegroups" repeat categories across pages, so repeated H3 categories should be merged into one group.

2.)

For the standard pageType "type: 'artist-releasegroups'" the original code path was actually OK and should be restored.

"artist-releasegroups" actually COULD repeat categories across pages at least ONCE:

a.)

The first occurence of a category e.g. "Album + Live" on a specific downloaded paginated page internally ALWAYS
corresponds to rows (release groups) of the "Official Discography" type, although it could extend over multiple
consecutive pagined pages if there are lots of entries for that section, as MusciBee only allows 100 entries per
page. In that case all fetched rows should go in the same current category.

b.)

If on a later fetched paginated page (after h3 header sections have changed in between), the same already detected
category e.g. "Album + Live" shows up again, these rows (release groups) internally ALWAYS correspond to the
"Non-Official Discography" type and SHOULD go into a same named new category.

c.)

We already have code for handling and displaying these accumulated doubled sections for "artist-releasegroups" pages

#+begin_example
    /**
     * Injects four discography view-mode buttons into controlsContainer for
     * artist-releasegroups pages after the combined (all:'1') render completes
     * and the h3 category arrays have been built.
     *
     * Button order (left → right, before the Save/Load divider):
     *   ① 📋 Complete Discography          — shows ALL h3/table pairs (default)
     *   ② 📀 Official Discography          — shows only official h3/table pairs
     *   ③ 📼 Non-Official Discography      — shows only non-official h3/table pairs
     *   ④ 🗂️ Complete Discography (merged) — deduplicates same-name categories,
     *                                          combining their rows into one sub-table
     *
     * Button ① starts with the active (green) tint because the initial render
     * shows all categories.  The tint always follows the last-clicked button.
     *
     * Idempotent: removes any previously injected buttons/divider before
     * re-creating them, so a second button press (page reload) starts clean.
     *
     * Called from: startFetchingProcess() after the main fetch pipeline completes.
     */
    function _injectDiscographyViewButtons() {
#+end_example

Please fix, thouroughly document the changed code and apply an appropriate changelog entry

** solution

#+begin_example
v9.99.479+2026-04-11 — one block rewritten, _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode fetch branch
The group-accumulation logic is now split on pageType with full inline documentation of the reasoning:
if (pageType === 'artist-releasegroups') {
    // Continuation model (restored from original):
    // same-as-last → append to existing group
    // different     → new group (even if name already appeared earlier)
    if (category !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages) { ... push new }
    currentGroup = groupedRows[groupedRows.length - 1];
} else {
    // Find-or-create model (for collections-releases Release groups):
    // all rows for a category always merge into the one canonical group
    currentGroup = groupedRows.find(...) || push new;
}
Why artist-releasegroups needs the continuation model:

Section > 100 entries → same h3 on consecutive XHR pages → must append (not create duplicate)
Later, Non-Official Discography has identical h3 names → same name but different position in stream → must create new group
lastCategorySeenAcrossPages captures position-in-stream; find() (by name) cannot distinguish these two cases
_injectDiscographyViewButtons relies on the resulting separate groups to power the Official/Non-Official/Merged view buttons

Why collections-releases needs find-or-create:

Every XHR page starts with ALL h3 headers → position-in-stream test would always see a "different" category on page boundaries → creates M×N duplicate groups instead of M canonical ones
#+end_example

* DONE fix Artist-Releasegroup pages

                            .includes(ev.target.tagName)) return;
                        ev.preventDefault();
                        ev.stopPropagation();

** DONE initial

In the NEWLY uploaded userscript,

For the next implentation you have to do, first of all the big picture:

For the special case if URLs have a pageType of "type: 'artist-releasegroups'" e.g.
https://musicbrainz.org/artist/70248960-cb53-4ea4-943a-edb18f7d336f the full fetch pipeline fetches the combined ("all: '1'")
discography of an artist, the 'official' and the 'non-official' discography defined with "params: { all: '1', va: '0' }"
in the page definition variable "const pageDefinitions".

Currently the final rendered page shows after the h2 header line named "Discography", first all sub-tables for the
'official discography' where each sub-table is preceeded with a h3 header line with the release-group 'category' of the
discography data, e.g. "Album", "Album + Compilation", etc. After all the 'official' categories, the sub-tables for the
'non-official discography' are rendered the same way. There is currently no way to know where the 'non-official' section
starts. And, to make it worse, a h3 header name like "Album" could exist in both sections the 'official' and
'non-official' one.

In order to know which sub-tables belong to which section types, the plan is to pre-fetch all the paginated webpages
belonging to just the 'official' artist-releasegroups and EXTRACTING (but NOT rendering them) just the h3 header names
in a new array variable "h3_official_category_header_array", BEFORE we do the usual full fetch pipeline for the combined
("all: '1'") discography and the rest of the processing logic.

There is a new "subType: 'artist-releasegroups'" definition in the variable "const pageDefinitions"

#+begin_example
        {
            subType: 'artist-releasegroups',
            match: (path, params) => path.match(/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}$/) && !path.endsWith('/releases'),
            buttons: [
                { mainLabel: '🧮 Artist RGs',         pre_fetch_type: 'Official', params: { all: '0', va: '0' } },
                { mainLabel: '🧮 Various Artist RGs', pre_fetch_type: 'Official', params: { all: '0', va: '1' } },
            ]
        },
#+end_example

which defines, when clicking on the inital page the "mainLabel" button, which URL ("match") with which query_parameters ("params") should be used 
to get the corresponding webpages for JUST the h3 header name extraction for the pre_fetch_type: 'Official' (like in the main fetch loop)

The commented block between

#+begin_example
        // ── BEGIN: Artist-Releasegroups pre-fetch pass ────────────────────────────────────────
        // ── END: Artist-Releasegroups pre-fetch pass ──────────────────────────────────────────────
#+end_example

is probably the place where the pre-fetch logic belongs to.

On the final rendered page we additionally extract ALL the h3 category header names from the full fetch pipeline (they
represent the 'official' and 'non-official' ones) in a new array variable "h3_all_category_header_array".

The category names from array "h3_all_category_header_array" are then compared against the array
"h3_official_category_header_array" in order to create a third array "h3_non_official_category_header_array" which holds
ONLY the 'non-offical' entries.

In the comparison strategy, as long as names match, those entries are 'official'. The first name in
"h3_all_category_header_array" that is not in the "h3_official_category_header_array" sequence marks the start of the
'non-official' section.

For later display purposes another array "h3_all_category_unique_header_array" is also created which should hold ONLY
the unique values of the "h3_all_category_header_array" array, but leaving the order intact. That means, if an entry
with the same name repeats in the array "h3_all_category_header_array" the second entry should be disgarded in the newly
created array "h3_all_category_unique_header_array".

When we have that information the goal is to create on the final rendered page two new buttons "Official Discography" and
"Non-Official Discography" right beside the existing buttons rerendered from the initial page and

- the "globalStatusDisplay.textContent" and the '<span id="mb-fetch-progress-outer"' should display the ongoing prefetching state 

- when clicking the "Official Discography"-button we only display the 'offical' headers with their sub-tables and hide
  the 'non-offical' ones. 

- when clicking the "Non-Official Discography"-button we only display the 'non-offical' headers with their sub-tables and hide
  the 'offical' ones

- depending on which button has been clicked, the h2 header name initially displayed as "Discography" should be cahnge
  either to "Official Discography" or "Non-Official Discography"

- also the total row count calculation in the span

#+begin_example
<span class="mb-row-count-stat">(2123)</span>
#+end_example

should account which subType has been selected.

#+begin_example
globalStatusDisplay.textContent = 'Loading (pass 1 of 2)…';
#+end_example

add the following new pageType definition

#+begin_example
        // CDtoc pages
        {
            type: 'cdtoc',
            match: (path) => path.includes('/cdtoc'),
            buttons: [ { label: 'Show all CDToc attached to Releases' } ],
            tableMode: 'single',
            features: {
              columnExtractors: [
                  { sourceColumn: 'Country/Date', extractor: 'splitCountryDate', syntheticColumns: ['Country', 'Date'] }
              ],
              syntheticColumnExtractors: [
                  { sourceColumn: 'Date', extractor: 'dateParts', syntheticColumns: ['DD', 'MM', 'YYYY', 'Day', 'Month'] }
              ],
              injectedColumns: [ 'Relationships' ],
              integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'DD', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'MM', align: 'R'}, {sourceColumn: 'YYYY', align: 'C'} ],
              renderMultiRowCell: [ 'Label', 'Catalog#' ],
              collapsableColumns: [ 'Country/Date' ,'Country', 'Date', 'Label', 'Catalog#' ],
              tooltipColumns: [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Artist', '---', 'Format', 'Country/Date', ['Label', '-', 'Catalog#'], 'Barcode' ],
              addCAA: 'Title',
              extractMainColumn: 'Title',
              stickyColumn: 'Title'
            }
        },
#+end_example

** ChatGPT

#+begin_example
    async function renderFinalTable(rows) {
        const rowCount = Array.isArray(rows) ? rows.length : 0;
        Lib.debug('render', `Starting renderFinalTable with ${rowCount} rows.`);

        const tbody = document.querySelector('table.tbl tbody');
        if (!tbody) {
            Lib.error('render', 'Abort: #tbody container not found.');
            return;
        }

        tbody.innerHTML = '';

        const table = tbody.closest('table');
        const thCount = table?.querySelectorAll('thead th')?.length || 0;
        const tdCount = rows[0]?.cells?.length || 0;

        Lib.debug(
            'render',
            `Final table structure → headers=${thCount}, rowCells=${tdCount}`
        );

        if (rowCount === 0) {
            Lib.error('render', 'No rows provided to renderFinalTable.');
            return;
        }

        // Use threshold setting for when to enable chunked rendering
        const chunkThreshold = Lib.settings.sa_chunked_render_threshold || 1000;

        // For small datasets, use fast simple append
        if (chunkThreshold === 0 || rowCount < chunkThreshold) {
            rows.forEach(r => tbody.appendChild(r));
            Lib.debug('render', `Fast render: Injected ${rowCount} rows into DOM.`);
        } else {
            // For large datasets, use chunked async rendering with progress
            await renderRowsChunked(tbody, rows, 'single');
        }

        // Show the save button now that data is rendered
        if (Lib.settings.sa_enable_save_load) {
            saveToDiskBtn.style.display = 'inline-block';
        }

        // Re-apply multi-sort column tints if a multi-sort is active for this table.
        // renderFinalTable replaces all tbody content with fresh clones that carry no tint
        // classes, so tints must be repainted after every render.
        const tintEntry = multiSortTintRegistry.get('main_table');
        if (tintEntry) tintEntry.applyTints();

        // Install (or re-confirm) the MutationObserver that strips jesus2099 treleases
        // nodes from Length cells the instant they are injected into the live tbody.
        if (Lib.settings.sa_enable_numeric_alignment !== false) {
            initTreleasesObserver();
        }

        // --- rebind tracklist toggle (event delegation) ---
        document.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
            const toggle = e.target.closest('a.toggle');
            if (!toggle) return;
    
            // prevent any default navigation behavior
            e.preventDefault();
    
            const tr = toggle.closest('tr');
            if (!tr) return;
    
            const next = tr.nextElementSibling;
            if (!next) return;
    
            // MusicBrainz uses hidden rows for tracklists
            const isHidden = next.style.display === 'none' || getComputedStyle(next).display === 'none';
    
            next.style.display = isHidden ? '' : 'none';
        }, true);

    }
#+end_example

#+begin_example
// You can change the default port below if you have running Picard on a
// different port then the default 8000.
const TAGGER_DEFAULT_PORT = 8000

// As Picard can end up running on a higher port if the configured port is in
// use, this script probes a range of ports up to TAGGER_MAX_PORT. You can
// lower this value or even set it to TAGGER_DEFAULT_PORT to reduce the amount
// of probing requests.
const TAGGER_MAX_PORT = 8010

// Usually Picard will listen on your local device only. You might want to
// set this to a different IP address in your local network if Picard is
// running on a different computer then your browser.
const TAGGER_HOST = '127.0.0.1'

** TODO function isExpandRGsPage()

Adjust the function

#+begin_example
    /**
     * Returns true when the current page matches the expandRGs @match / @exclude criteria:
     *   artist/*                             (all artist sub-pages, no exclusions)
     *   label/<UUID>                         (root label page only)
     *   release-group/<UUID>                 (root release-group page only)
     *   series/<UUID>                        (root series page only)
     *   recording/<UUID>                     (root recording page only — "Appears on" releases table)
     *
     * @returns {boolean}
     */
    function isExpandRGsPage() {
        const p    = window.location.pathname;
        const mbid = MBID_REGEX.source;
        if (new RegExp(`^/artist/${mbid}`).test(p)) return true;
        if (new RegExp(`^/recording/${mbid}$`).test(p)) return true;
        if (new RegExp(`^/collection/${mbid}`).test(p)) return true;
        return new RegExp(`^/(label|release-group|series)/${mbid}$`).test(p);
    }
#+end_example

to support additionally the pageType "type: 'taglookup'"

A typical URL looks like https://musicbrainz.org/taglookup/index?tag-lookup.artist=Bruce+Springsteen&tag-lookup.release=The+Rising&tag-lookup.tracknum=&tag-lookup.track=&tag-lookup.duration=&tag-lookup.filename=
